item.title: 大规模语言模型推理部署的高效调度方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出两种启发式算法提升LLM推理部署效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在严格延迟、准确性和预算约束下，大规模语言模型（LLM）推理部署的优化问题。传统精确的混合整数线性规划（MILP）方法虽然能保证最优解，但扩展性差。为此，作者提出了两种基于约束感知的启发式方法：单次遍历的贪心算法（GH）和通过多起点构造、移动局部搜索和GPU整合改进的自适应贪心算法（AGH）。此外，还设计了三种约束感知机制以确保在内存、延迟、错误和预算等紧密耦合条件下的可行性。实验表明，AGH在大规模实例上实现了超过260倍的速度提升，且在压力测试中表现出稳定的性能和成本控制能力。

item.title: 基于相位同步的视觉Transformer改进方法
item.content:encoded:
KoPE通过相位同步提升视觉模型训练效率与结构学习能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Kuramoto Oscillatory Phase Encoding (KoPE) 的新型相位编码机制，将其引入视觉Transformer模型中，以模拟神经系统的同步振荡特性。该方法通过神经启发的同步机制，提升模型在训练、参数和数据效率方面的表现，并在语义分割、全景分割、语言对齐及少样本抽象视觉推理等任务中展现出优势。理论分析与实验验证表明，KoPE有助于加速注意力集中，提高学习效率，为先进神经网络模型提供了一种可扩展的神经启发机制。

item.title: CrashSight: A Phase-Aware, Infrastructure-Centric Video Benchmark for Traffic Crash Scene Understanding and Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Cooperative autonomous driving requires traffic scene understanding from both vehicle and infrastructure perspectives. While vision-language models (VLMs) show strong general reasoning capabilities, their performance in safety-critical traffic scenarios remains insufficiently evaluated due to the ego-vehicle focus of existing benchmarks. To bridge this gap, we present \textbf{CrashSight}, a large-scale vision-language benchmark for roadway crash understanding using real-world roadside camera data. The dataset comprises 250 crash videos, annotated with 13K multiple-choice question-answer pairs organized under a two-tier taxonomy. Tier 1 evaluates the visual grounding of scene context and involved parties, while Tier 2 probes higher-level reasoning, including crash mechanics, causal attribution, temporal progression, and post-crash outcomes. We benchmark 8 state-of-the-art VLMs and show that, despite strong scene description capabilities, current models struggle with temporal and causal reasoning in safety-critical scenarios. We provide a detailed analysis of failure scenarios and discuss directions for improving VLM crash understanding. The benchmark provides a standardized evaluation framework for infrastructure-assisted perception in cooperative autonomous driving. The CrashSight benchmark, including the full dataset and code, is accessible at https://mcgrche.github.io/crashsight.

item.title: Latent Structure of Affective Representations in Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: The geometric structure of latent representations in large language models (LLMs) is an active area of research, driven in part by its implications for model transparency and AI safety. Existing literature has focused mainly on general geometric and topological properties of the learnt representations, but due to a lack of ground-truth latent geometry, validating the findings of such approaches is challenging. Emotion processing provides an intriguing testbed for probing representational geometry, as emotions exhibit both categorical organization and continuous affective dimensions, which are well-established in the psychology literature. Moreover, understanding such representations carries safety relevance. In this work, we investigate the latent structure of affective representations in LLMs using geometric data analysis tools. We present three main findings. First, we show that LLMs learn coherent latent representations of affective emotions that align with widely used valence--arousal models from psychology. Second, we find that these representations exhibit nonlinear geometric structure that can nonetheless be well-approximated linearly, providing empirical support for the linear representation hypothesis commonly assumed in model transparency methods. Third, we demonstrate that the learned latent representation space can be leveraged to quantify uncertainty in emotion processing tasks. Our findings suggest that LLMs acquire affective representations with geometric structure paralleling established models of human emotion, with practical implications for model interpretability and safety.

item.title: From Static to Interactive: Adapting Visual in-Context Learners for User-Driven Tasks
item.content:encoded: Visual in-context learning models are designed to adapt to new tasks by leveraging a set of example input-output pairs, enabling rapid generalization without task-specific fine-tuning. However, these models operate in a fundamentally static paradigm: while they can adapt to new tasks, they lack any mechanism to incorporate user-provided guidance signals such as scribbles, clicks, or bounding boxes to steer or refine the prediction process. This limitation is particularly restrictive in real-world applications, where users want to actively guide model predictions, e.g., by highlighting the target object for segmentation, indicating a region which should be visually altered, or isolating a specific person in a complex scene to run targeted pose estimation. In this work, we propose a simple method to transform static visual in-context learners, particularly the DeLVM approach, into highly controllable, user-driven systems, i.e., Interactive DeLVM, enabling seamless interaction through natural visual cues such as scribbles, clicks, or drawing boxes. Specifically, by encoding interactions directly into the example input-output pairs, we keep the philosophy of visual in-context learning intact: enabling users to prompt models with unseen interactions without fine-tuning and empowering them to dynamically steer model predictions with personalized interactions. Our experiments demonstrate that SOTA visual in-context learning models fail to effectively leverage interaction cues, often ignoring user guidance entirely. In contrast, our method excels in controllable, user-guided scenarios, achieving improvements of $+7.95%$ IoU for interactive segmentation, $+2.46$ PSNR for directed super-resolution, and $-3.14%$ LPIPS for interactive object removal. With this, our work bridges the gap between rigid static task adaptation and fluid interactivity for user-centric visual in-context learning.

item.title: Small Vision-Language Models are Smart Compressors for Long Video Understanding
item.content:encoded: Adapting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for hour-long videos is bottlenecked by context limits. Dense visual streams saturate token budgets and exacerbate the lost-in-the-middle phenomenon. Existing heuristics, like sparse sampling or uniform pooling, blindly sacrifice fidelity by discarding decisive moments and wasting bandwidth on irrelevant backgrounds. We propose Tempo, an efficient query-aware framework compressing long videos for downstream understanding. Tempo leverages a Small Vision-Language Model (SVLM) as a local temporal compressor, casting token reduction as an early cross-modal distillation process to generate compact, intent-aligned representations in a single forward pass. To enforce strict budgets without breaking causality, we introduce Adaptive Token Allocation (ATA). Exploiting the SVLM's zero-shot relevance prior and semantic front-loading, ATA acts as a training-free $O(1)$ dynamic router. It allocates dense bandwidth to query-critical segments while compressing redundancies into minimal temporal anchors to maintain the global storyline. Extensive experiments show our 6B architecture achieves state-of-the-art performance with aggressive dynamic compression (0.5-16 tokens/frame). On the extreme-long LVBench (4101s), Tempo scores 52.3 under a strict 8K visual budget, outperforming GPT-4o and Gemini 1.5 Pro. Scaling to 2048 frames reaches 53.7. Crucially, Tempo compresses hour-long videos substantially below theoretical limits, proving true long-form video understanding relies on intent-driven efficiency rather than greedily padded context windows.

item.title: Small-scale photonic Kolmogorov-Arnold networks using standard telecom nonlinear modules
item.content:encoded: Photonic neural networks promise ultrafast inference, yet most architectures rely on linear optical meshes with electronic nonlinearities, reintroducing optical-electrical-optical bottlenecks. Here we introduce small-scale photonic Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (SSP-KANs) implemented entirely with standard telecommunications components. Each network edge employs a trainable nonlinear module composed of a Mach-Zehnder interferometer, semiconductor optical amplifier, and variable optical attenuators, providing a four-parameter transfer function derived from gain saturation and interferometric mixing. Despite this constrained expressivity, SSP-KANs comprising only a few optical modules achieve strong nonlinear inference performance across classification, regression, and image recognition tasks, approaching software baselines with significantly fewer parameters. A four-module network achieves 98.4\% accuracy on nonlinear classification benchmarks inaccessible to linear models. Performance remains robust under realistic hardware impairments, maintaining high accuracy down to 6-bit input resolution and 14 dB signal-to-noise ratio. By using a fully differentiable physics model for end-to-end optimisation of optical parameters, this work establishes a practical pathway from simulation to experimental demonstration of photonic KANs using commodity telecom hardware.

item.title: When Does Context Help? A Systematic Study of Target-Conditional Molecular Property Prediction
item.content:encoded: We present the first systematic study of when target context helps molecular property prediction, evaluating context conditioning across 10 diverse protein families, 4 fusion architectures, data regimes spanning 67-9,409 training compounds, and both temporal and random evaluation splits. Using NestDrug, a FiLM-based architecture that conditions molecular representations on target identity, we characterize both success and failure modes with three principal findings. First, fusion architecture dominates: FiLM outperforms concatenation by 24.2 percentage points and additive conditioning by 8.6 pp; how you incorporate context matters more than whether you include it. Second, context enables otherwise impossible predictions: on data-scarce CYP3A4 (67 training compounds), multi-task transfer achieves 0.686 AUC where per-target Random Forest collapses to 0.238. Third, context can systematically hurt: distribution mismatch causes 10.2 pp degradation on BACE1; few-shot adaptation consistently underperforms zero-shot. Beyond methodology, we expose fundamental flaws in standard benchmarking: 1-nearest-neighbor Tanimoto achieves 0.991 AUC on DUD-E without any learning, and 50% of actives leak from training data, rendering absolute performance metrics meaningless. Our temporal split evaluation (train up to 2020, test 2021-2024) achieves stable 0.843 AUC with no degradation, providing the first rigorous evidence that context-conditional molecular representations generalize to future chemical space.

item.title: OceanMAE: A Foundation Model for Ocean Remote Sensing
item.content:encoded: Accurate ocean mapping is essential for applications such as bathymetry estimation, seabed characterization, marine litter detection, and ecosystem monitoring. However, ocean remote sensing (RS) remains constrained by limited labeled data and by the reduced transferability of models pre-trained mainly on land-dominated Earth observation imagery. In this paper, we propose OceanMAE, an ocean-specific masked autoencoder that extends standard MAE pre-training by integrating multispectral Sentinel-2 observations with physically meaningful ocean descriptors during self-supervised learning. By incorporating these auxiliary ocean features, OceanMAE is designed to learn more informative and ocean-aware latent representations from large- scale unlabeled data. To transfer these representations to downstream applications, we further employ a modified UNet-based framework for marine segmentation and bathymetry estimation. Pre-trained on the Hydro dataset, OceanMAE is evaluated on MADOS and MARIDA for marine pollutant and debris segmentation, and on MagicBathyNet for bathymetry regression. The experiments show that OceanMAE yields the strongest gains on marine segmentation, while bathymetry benefits are competitive and task-dependent. In addition, an ablation against a standard MAE on MARIDA indicates that incorporating auxiliary ocean descriptors during pre-training improves downstream segmentation quality. These findings highlight the value of physically informed and domain-aligned self-supervised pre- training for ocean RS. Code and weights are publicly available at https://git.tu-berlin.de/joanna.stamer/SSLORS2.

item.title: Just Pass Twice: Efficient Token Classification with LLMs for Zero-Shot NER
item.content:encoded: Large language models encode extensive world knowledge valuable for zero-shot named entity recognition. However, their causal attention mechanism, where tokens attend only to preceding context, prevents effective token classification when disambiguation requires future context. Existing approaches use LLMs generatively, prompting them to list entities or produce structured outputs, but suffer from slow autoregressive decoding, hallucinated entities, and formatting errors. We propose Just Pass Twice (JPT), a simple yet effective method that enables causal LLMs to perform discriminative token classification with full bidirectional context. Our key insight is that concatenating the input to itself lets each token in the second pass attend to the complete sentence, requiring no architectural modifications. We combine these representations with definition-guided entity embeddings for flexible zero-shot generalization. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art results on zero-shot NER benchmarks, surpassing the previous best method by +7.9 F1 on average across CrossNER and MIT benchmarks, being over 20x faster than comparable generative methods.

item.title: Agentivism: a learning theory for the age of artificial intelligence
item.content:encoded: Learning theories have historically changed when the conditions of learning evolved. Generative and agentic AI create a new condition by allowing learners to delegate explanation, writing, problem solving, and other cognitive work to systems that can generate, recommend, and sometimes act on the learner's behalf. This creates a fundamental challenge for learning theory: successful performance can no longer be assumed to indicate learning. Learners may complete tasks effectively with AI support while developing less understanding, weaker judgment, and limited transferable capability. We argue that this problem is not fully captured by existing learning theories. Behaviourism, cognitivism, constructivism, and connectivism remain important, but they do not directly explain when AI-assisted performance becomes durable human capability. We propose Agentivism, a learning theory for human-AI interaction. Agentivism defines learning as durable growth in human capability through selective delegation to AI, epistemic monitoring and verification of AI contributions, reconstructive internalization of AI-assisted outputs, and transfer under reduced support. The importance of Agentivism lies in explaining how learning remains possible when intelligent delegation is easy and human-AI interaction is becoming a persistent and expanding part of human learning.

item.title: NTIRE 2026 Challenge on Bitstream-Corrupted Video Restoration: Methods and Results
item.content:encoded: This paper reports on the NTIRE 2026 Challenge on Bitstream-Corrupted Video Restoration (BSCVR). The challenge aims to advance research on recovering visually coherent videos from corrupted bitstreams, whose decoding often produces severe spatial-temporal artifacts and content distortion. Built upon recent progress in bitstream-corrupted video recovery, the challenge provides a common benchmark for evaluating restoration methods under realistic corruption settings. We describe the dataset, evaluation protocol, and participating methods, and summarize the final results and main technical trends. The challenge highlights the difficulty of this emerging task and provides useful insights for future research on robust video restoration under practical bitstream corruption.

item.title: Boosted Distributional Reinforcement Learning: Analysis and Healthcare Applications
item.content:encoded: Researchers and practitioners are increasingly considering reinforcement learning to optimize decisions in complex domains like robotics and healthcare. To date, these efforts have largely utilized expectation-based learning. However, relying on expectation-focused objectives may be insufficient for making consistent decisions in highly uncertain situations involving multiple heterogeneous groups. While distributional reinforcement learning algorithms have been introduced to model the full distributions of outcomes, they can yield large discrepancies in realized benefits among comparable agents. This challenge is particularly acute in healthcare settings, where physicians (controllers) must manage multiple patients (subordinate agents) with uncertain disease progression and heterogeneous treatment responses. We propose a Boosted Distributional Reinforcement Learning (BDRL) algorithm that optimizes agent-specific outcome distributions while enforcing comparability among similar agents and analyze its convergence. To further stabilize learning, we incorporate a post-update projection step formulated as a constrained convex optimization problem, which efficiently aligns individual outcomes with a high-performing reference within a specified tolerance. We apply our algorithm to manage hypertension in a large subset of the US adult population by categorizing individuals into cardiovascular disease risk groups. Our approach modifies treatment plans for median and vulnerable patients by mimicking the behavior of high-performing references in each risk group. Furthermore, we find that BDRL improves the number and consistency of quality-adjusted life years compared with reinforcement learning baselines.

item.title: Making Room for AI: Multi-GPU Molecular Dynamics with Deep Potentials in GROMACS
item.content:encoded: GROMACS is a de-facto standard for classical Molecular Dynamics (MD). The rise of AI-driven interatomic potentials that pursue near-quantum accuracy at MD throughput now poses a significant challenge: embedding neural-network inference into multi-GPU simulations retaining high-performance. In this work, we integrate the MLIP framework DeePMD-kit into GROMACS, enabling domain-decomposed, GPU-accelerated inference across multi-node systems. We extend the GROMACS NNPot interface with a DeePMD backend, and we introduce a domain decomposition layer decoupled from the main simulation. The inference is executed concurrently on all processes, with two MPI collectives used each step to broadcast coordinates and to aggregate and redistribute forces. We train an in-house DPA-1 model (1.6 M parameters) on a dataset of solvated protein fragments. We validate the implementation on a small protein system, then we benchmark the GROMACS-DeePMD integration with a 15,668 atom protein on NVIDIA A100 and AMD MI250x GPUs up to 32 devices. Strong-scaling efficiency reaches 66% at 16 devices and 40% at 32; weak-scaling efficiency is 80% to 16 devices and reaches 48% (MI250x) and 40% (A100) at 32 devices. Profiling with the ROCm System profiler shows that >90% of the wall time is spent in DeePMD inference, while MPI collectives contribute <10%, primarily since they act as a global synchronization point. The principal bottlenecks are the irreducible ghost-atom cost set by the cutoff radius, confirmed by a simple throughput model, and load imbalance across ranks. These results demonstrate that production MD with near ab initio fidelity is feasible at scale in GROMACS.

item.title: 基于配对比较的大型语言模型评估不确定性量化研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种新型评估框架，用于量化大型语言模型的不确定性。



摘要： 本文研究了基于配对比较的大型语言模型（LLM）评估问题，将该过程建模为低秩潜在分数张量的半参数推断问题。通过Bradley-Terry-Luce型模型，文章探讨了在非均匀采样和成对对比下的张量补全问题。研究目标是估计如能力差距和胜率等函数性参数，并推导出信息算子、有效影响函数及半参数效率界。针对信息算子的各向异性问题，作者提出了一种分数去相关方法，以恢复最优样本复杂度下的稳定推断。该研究为LLM评估提供了理论基础，并扩展至从成对数据中进行低秩结构推断的更广泛场景。

item.title: 基于智能超表面的中继增强型无线物理神经网络架构
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型无线物理神经网络架构，提升非线性处理能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于激活集成堆叠智能超表面（AI-SIMs）的中继增强型无线物理神经网络（WPNN）架构。该架构通过将线性波操控的超表面层与实现模拟域非线性处理的激活超表面层级联，结合多跳无线传播结构，使中继放大矩阵和超表面相位矩阵共同作为可训练网络权重，硬件实现的激活函数提供了关键的非线性特性。仿真结果表明，该架构在分类任务中表现出高准确率，并显著优于纯线性物理实现。

item.title: 神经网络在地震波反演中的物理敏感性学习研究
item.content:encoded:
神经网络可学习物理敏感性结构，适用于地震波反演。



摘要： 本文研究了数据驱动的神经网络作为地球物理正演模型的适用性，重点探讨其是否能恢复物理敏感性结构而非仅映射数据。通过对比神经网络的自动微分梯度与理论敏感性核，发现其能够恢复主要深度依赖的物理结构，表明神经网络可以学习到有意义的物理信息，而不仅仅是黑箱预测器。然而，训练分布中的强结构先验可能导致推断出的敏感性出现系统性偏差。研究结果表明，神经网络正演代理模型可用于反演和不确定性分析，同时明确了其物理一致性条件。

item.title: PhageBench：评估噬菌体基因组理解能力的基准
item.content:encoded:
噬菌体基因组分析需更强的生物序列推理模型。



摘要： 文章介绍了PhageBench，这是首个用于评估噬菌体基因组理解能力的基准，涵盖五个核心任务和三个阶段。通过测试八种大语言模型，发现通用推理模型在噬菌体片段识别和宿主预测方面表现优于随机基线，但在涉及长距离依赖和精细功能定位的复杂任务中存在明显不足。研究强调了开发具备更强推理能力的下一代生物序列模型的重要性。

item.title: AI代理在真实任务中的评估框架 ClawBench
item.content:encoded:
ClawBench 评估 AI 代理在真实网络任务中的表现。



摘要： 文章介绍了 ClawBench，这是一个包含 153 项日常任务的评估框架，涵盖 15 个类别和 144 个在线平台。这些任务涉及从购买到求职申请等多种操作，要求 AI 代理具备获取信息、跨平台导航和填写表单等能力。与传统基准不同，ClawBench 在真实网站上运行，确保评估的真实性和挑战性。测试显示，目前主流 AI 模型仅能完成少量任务，表明 AI 代理仍需进一步发展以成为可靠的助手。

item.title: 基于PU学习的AtticPOT仓库去重方法
item.content:encoded:
通过PU学习实现AtticPOT仓库的去重，提升准确率。



摘要： 本文将AtticPOT仓库中的 curator-in-the-loop 去重问题建模为一个正负样本不明确（PU）学习问题。在每个文物仅提供一个锚点的情况下，利用增强视图训练轻量级的 Clone Encoder，并通过可解释的 l_2 范数阈值对未标记仓库进行评分。该系统提出候选记录供 curator 验证，发现未被事先验证的跨记录重复项。在 CIFAR-10 数据集上达到 F1=96.37（AUROC=97.97），在 AtticPOT 上达到 F1=90.79（AUROC=98.99），比最佳基线 SVDD 提高了 7.70 个点。实验表明该方法在视角和条件变化下仍保持稳定的邻域结构，适用于去重、记录链接和 curator-in-the-loop 工作流程。

item.title: 基于推理图的模型自适应优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过推理图提升模型推理一致性与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于推理图的模型优化方法，旨在解决传统语言模型在每次查询中重新推理导致的准确率低和结果不稳定问题。推理图结构将每条证据的推理过程以图形式持久化，允许后续查询基于历史判断进行反馈优化。同时引入检索图增强候选集筛选，形成自我改进的反馈循环，提高准确率并降低方差。该方法无需重新训练模型，仅通过图遍历实现性能提升，并在多跳问答任务中验证了其有效性。

item.title: Client-Side Optimization for AI Agents with AgentOpt
item.content:encoded:
AgentOpt 是首个客户端优化框架，提升多步骤代理管道的效率与成本效益。



摘要： 随着 AI 代理在现实世界中的广泛应用，研究重点逐渐从服务器端效率转向客户端资源分配问题。本文提出 AgentOpt，一个框架无关的 Python 工具，用于优化模型选择、本地工具和 API 预算的分配。通过多种搜索算法，如 Arm Elimination 和 Bayesian Optimization，AgentOpt 在保持准确性的同时显著降低评估成本。实验表明，在相同准确率下，最佳模型组合的成本比最差组合低 13-32 倍。该工具已在多个基准测试中验证其有效性。

item.title: LLM-based自动程序修复中的故障定位研究
item.content:encoded:
故障定位对LLM APR效果有显著影响，但过度增加上下文未必提升性能。



摘要： 本文针对基于大语言模型的自动程序修复（APR）中的故障定位（FL）进行了大规模实证研究，分析了不同层级上下文对修复效果的影响。实验基于500个SWE-bench验证实例，评估了61种配置，发现文件级定位是关键因素，能带来15-17倍的性能提升。扩展文件上下文通常有助于提高修复效果，而元素级和行级上下文则依赖于文件上下文质量，且行级上下文可能因噪声放大而降低性能。LLM检索方法在使用更少文件和标记的情况下优于结构启发式方法。研究结果表明，有效的FL策略应结合高层次语义理解和精准行级定位，挑战了传统认为增加上下文总能提升APR效果的假设。

item.title: 用户心智模型对AI写作助手使用行为的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
心智模型影响用户对AI写作助手的使用与纠错能力。



摘要： 本文研究了用户对AI写作助手的心智模型如何影响其使用行为和写作结果。通过实验发现，具有结构性心智模型的用户虽然更了解系统，但反而在写作中产生更多语法错误，表明系统理解、信任与控制之间的关系复杂，尤其在需要用户监督错误输出的场景中更为明显。

item.title: 基于长时嵌入的推荐系统优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过长时嵌入解决推荐系统中的短期偏好与长期偏好不匹配问题。



摘要： 现代基于Transformer的序列推荐系统在捕捉短期意图方面表现优异，但容易受到时间偏差的影响，忽视稳定的长期偏好。为了解决这一问题，本文提出长时嵌入（LTE）作为高惯性上下文锚点，以弥补短期和长期偏好之间的差距。针对生产环境中特征存储版本不一致导致的离线-在线不匹配问题，作者引入LTE框架，确保嵌入在不同版本间保持语义一致性。此外，研究还探讨了因果语言建模的集成策略，并通过两种表示方法（启发式平均和非对称自编码器）进行验证。在Zalando的在线A/B测试中，使用滞后窗口整合LTE作为上下文前缀显著提升了用户参与度和财务指标。

item.title: 基于强化微调的智能推荐框架ReRec提升大模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
ReRec通过强化微调提升LLM在复杂推荐任务中的推理能力。



摘要： 随着大语言模型（LLM）的发展，智能推荐助手需要处理复杂查询并提供个性化推理推荐。然而，LLM在多步骤推理中仍面临挑战，因此需要增强推理能力的系统。本文提出ReRec框架，通过三种关键组件提升LLM在复杂推荐任务中的表现：双图增强奖励设计、推理感知优势估计以及在线课程调度器。实验表明，ReRec优于现有基线，并保持了指令遵循和通用知识等核心能力。代码已开源。

item.title: AutoSOTA：自动化实现人工智能模型优化的系统
item.content:encoded:
AutoSOTA自动提升AI模型性能，发现105个新SOTA模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为AutoSOTA的端到端自动化研究系统，旨在加速人工智能模型的优化过程。该系统通过资源准备、实验评估和反思创新三个阶段，利用八个专业代理协同工作，实现从论文到代码的转化、环境初始化、实验跟踪、优化想法生成与验证。在多个顶级AI会议论文上进行的评估显示，AutoSOTA能够成功发现105个超越原始方法的新SOTA模型，平均每个论文耗时约五小时。案例研究表明，该系统不仅限于超参数调优，还能识别架构创新、算法重构和流程优化，为人工智能研究提供新的基础设施。

item.title: 基于有序流水线的模型压缩与加速方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合剪枝、量化和知识蒸馏的有序流水线提升模型推理效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在CPU资源受限环境下，如何通过结合无结构剪枝、INT8量化感知训练（QAT）和知识蒸馏（KD）的有序流水线来优化模型的准确率、大小和延迟。实验表明，INT8 QAT对运行时间有显著提升，而剪枝作为预处理步骤增强了后续低精度优化的鲁棒性。知识蒸馏则在不改变部署形式的前提下恢复了精度。在CIFAR-10/100数据集上的测试显示，该方法在准确率、模型大小和延迟之间取得了更优的平衡，验证了顺序对性能的重要影响。

item.title: 基于动态生成的摘要树方法提升问答系统性能
item.content:encoded:
DTCRS通过动态生成摘要树提升问答效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为DTCRS的方法，用于动态生成摘要树以提升问答系统的性能。该方法根据文档结构和查询语义决定是否需要构建摘要树，并通过分析问题类型来优化摘要过程。DTCRS通过分解问题并利用子问题嵌入作为初始聚类中心，有效减少冗余摘要，提高摘要与问题的相关性。实验表明，该方法显著缩短了摘要树构建时间，并在三个问答任务中取得显著提升。此外，研究还探讨了递归摘要在不同问题类型中的适用性，为未来研究提供了重要参考。

item.title: 基于Transformer的车内相机外参标定方法InCaRPose
item.content:encoded:
提出InCaRPose模型用于车内相机外参标定，提升精度与实时性。



摘要： 本文提出InCaRPose，一种基于Transformer的相对位姿预测架构，用于解决车内相机外参标定问题。该方法利用冻结的DINOv3特征和Transformer解码器，有效捕捉参考视图与目标视图之间的几何关系。与传统方法不同，InCaRPose可在单次推理中实现物理合理范围内的绝对度量尺度平移，适用于需要精确现实距离的安全感知场景。通过仅使用合成数据训练，模型能够泛化到真实车内环境，且无需依赖相同的相机内参，在7-Scenes数据集上表现优异。即使使用ViT-Small骨干网络，InCaRPose仍保持高精度，适用于实时驾驶监控等任务。

item.title: 基于Voter Interval域的最优委员会计算算法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多项式时间算法，解决Thiele规则下的最优委员会问题。



摘要： 本文提出一种多项式时间算法，用于在Voter Interval域下计算任意给定Thiele投票规则下的最优委员会。该结果解决了持续十年的开放性问题，适用于Proportional Approval Voting和所有Thiele规则。研究引入了一个新的结构定理——区间族的凹性定理，证明了在不同规模的解之间可以构造出中间规模的解，并保持得分不低于线性插值。通过将基数约束移入目标函数，构建了一个拉格朗日松弛优化框架，利用完全单模矩阵实现多项式时间求解。该算法和证明通过人机协作完成，部分关键结构定理由AI生成。

item.title: LLM生成代码的安全性验证研究
item.content:encoded:
55.8%的LLM生成代码含漏洞，GPT-4o表现最差。



摘要： 本研究对7个主流大语言模型在500个安全关键提示下生成的3,500个代码片段进行了形式化验证，使用COBALT分析流程和Z3 SMT求解器检测漏洞。结果显示，55.8%的代码包含至少一个漏洞，其中1,055个被Z3正式证明。GPT-4o漏洞率最高（62.4%，得F），Gemini 2.5 Flash表现最好（48.4%，得D）。所有模型均未获得高于D的评分。研究还发现，显式安全指令仅降低漏洞率4个百分点，行业工具遗漏97.8%的Z3检测到的漏洞，且模型在审查模式下能识别自身漏洞的78.7%，但默认生成时仍存在55.8%的漏洞率。

item.title: Gated Symile：提升多模态对比学习的鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
Gated Symile通过门控机制提升多模态对比学习性能。



摘要： 本文提出Gated Symile，一种改进的多模态对比学习方法。传统Symile在处理多模态数据时对所有模态一视同仁，未能考虑不同模态的可靠性差异，导致在存在不一致或不可靠模态时性能下降。Gated Symile引入基于注意力的门控机制，动态调整各模态贡献，抑制不可靠输入，并在缺乏可靠对齐时引入空值选项。实验表明，该方法在合成基准和真实三模态数据集上均优于现有模型，展示了门控机制在提升多模态对比学习鲁棒性方面的潜力。

item.title: MAT-Cell：基于神经符号推理的单细胞分析框架
item.content:encoded:
MAT-Cell通过符号约束提升单细胞分析的泛化能力与逻辑一致性。



摘要： 文章提出MAT-Cell，一种基于神经符号推理的单细胞分析框架。该框架将传统分类方法转化为可验证的推理过程，通过自适应检索增强生成（RAG）注入生物先验知识，减少转录组噪声，并采用辩证验证机制确保逻辑一致性。在大规模和跨物种基准测试中，MAT-Cell显著优于现有模型，在复杂场景下表现稳定。代码已开源。

item.title: UNDO Flip-Flop任务中状态检索的失败分析
item.content:encoded:
模型在UNDO Flip-Flop任务中未能正确检索历史状态。



摘要： 文章探讨了状态空间模型（SSMs）在处理非单调更新序列时的局限性。通过引入UNDO Flip-Flop任务，研究者发现尽管理论上有能力建模栈结构，但实际训练中模型无法可靠地恢复历史状态，而是采用局部切换策略。实验表明，Mamba-2模型在对抗性测试中准确率低于随机水平，结果表明失败是系统性的而非偶然的。研究强调了架构表达能力和梯度下降学习能力之间的差距。

提出ART模型，提升行人轨迹预测精度与效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种自适应关系Transformer（ART）模型，通过引入时序感知的关系图（TARG）和自适应交互剪枝机制（AIP），有效捕捉行人之间动态变化的交互关系，并减少冗余计算。在ETH/UCY和NBA数据集上的实验表明，该方法在保持高计算效率的同时，实现了最先进的轨迹预测性能。

item.title: SeLaR：一种提升大语言模型推理稳定性的轻量级框架
item.content:encoded:
SeLaR通过熵门机制提升推理稳定性与探索性。



摘要： 本文提出SeLaR，一种无需训练的轻量级框架，旨在解决传统Chain-of-Thought（CoT）在推理过程中因离散token采样表达能力有限而带来的问题。SeLaR引入熵门机制，在低置信度步骤中激活软嵌入，同时在高置信度步骤保持离散解码，以增强推理稳定性。此外，还设计了熵感知对比正则化，使软嵌入远离最高概率token的方向，从而鼓励更多潜在推理路径的探索。实验表明，SeLaR在五个推理基准上均优于标准CoT和最先进的无训练方法。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型与随机矩阵理论的犬类气胸自动诊断方法
item.content:encoded:
提出新方法用于犬类气胸自动诊断，提升准确性和可解释性。



摘要： 本文针对犬类气胸自动诊断中数据稀缺和模型可信度不足的问题，引入了一个公开的像素级标注数据集。同时，提出了一种新的诊断范式，将任务重构为信号定位与频谱检测的协同过程。通过视觉语言模型引导的迭代流匹配方法实现高精度分割，再利用随机矩阵理论分析病变特征，以识别非随机病理信号。该方法结合生成式分割与第一性原理统计分析，构建了高准确且可解释的诊断系统。

item.title: 基于概率建模的3D场景功能理解框架FunFact
item.content:encoded:
FunFact提升3D场景功能关系识别与置信度校准。



摘要： 本文提出FunFact，一种从RGB-D图像构建概率开放词汇功能3D场景图的框架。该方法通过构建以物体和部件为中心的3D地图，并利用基础模型生成语义合理的功能关系，再通过因子图变量进行联合概率推理，结合大语言模型的常识先验和几何约束，显著提升了功能边的发现召回率和置信度校准效果。为评估该框架，作者还推出了FunThor数据集，实验表明FunFact在多个基准上表现优异。

item.title: MUXQ：一种高效的低精度大语言模型量化方法
item.content:encoded:
MUXQ解决输入激活异常问题，提升边缘设备推理效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为MUXQ的混合到统一量化方法，用于优化大语言模型在边缘设备上的低精度推理。传统量化方法如ZeroQuant、LLM.int8()和SmoothQuant无法有效处理输入激活异常，导致硬件效率低下。MUXQ通过检测输入激活中的异常通道，并引入辅助矩阵重新分布异常幅度，从而减轻异常问题，使异常激活也能在低精度INT下量化，同时保持硬件友好的计算结构。实验表明，MUXQ在GPT-2不同规模模型上均优于朴素量化方法，在保持接近FP16精度的同时，实现INT8量化，具有较低的计算开销，适用于边缘设备部署。

item.content:encoded:
PanLUNA实现多模态生理信号联合处理，性能优异。



摘要： 本文介绍了PanLUNA，一个5.4M参数的多模态生理信号基础模型，能够同时处理EEG、ECG和PPG信号。通过统一的查询集和传感器类型嵌入，PanLUNA实现了高效的跨模态早期融合，并在缺失模态时仍保持鲁棒性。实验表明，其在TUAB异常EEG检测和HMC多模态睡眠分期任务中表现优异，且经过量化训练后性能损失极小。该模型成功部署在低功耗微控制器上，适用于可穿戴设备。

item.content:encoded:
Argus通过多智能体协作提升漏洞检测效率与准确性。



摘要： 文章介绍了Argus，一个基于大型语言模型（LLM）的新一代静态应用安全测试（SAST）框架。传统LLM方法在工业部署中存在误报率高、推理深度不足等问题，而Argus通过重新设计SAST工作流程，引入多智能体协作、供应链分析和RAG等技术，显著提升了漏洞检测能力。实验表明，Argus不仅能检测更多真实漏洞，还有效降低了误报率和成本，并成功识别出多个关键零日漏洞。

item.title: BodhiPromptShield：一种针对LLM/VLM代理中提示隐私风险的框架
item.content:encoded:
提出BodhiPromptShield框架以解决LLM/VLM代理中的隐私泄露问题。



摘要： 本文介绍了BodhiPromptShield，这是一种针对大语言模型和视觉语言模型代理中提示隐私风险的框架。该框架通过检测敏感内容、使用占位符、语义抽象或安全符号映射来防止隐私信息在多个阶段传播。与传统的去标识化方法相比，BodhiPromptShield引入了传播感知的中介机制和恢复时间作为安全变量。在受控评估中，该框架有效减少了检索、内存和工具阶段的隐私泄露，表现出优于通用去标识化方案的性能。

item.title: Preservation Is Not Enough for Width Growth: Regime-Sensitive Selection of Dense LM Warm Starts
item.content:encoded: Width expansion offers a practical route to reuse smaller causal-language-model checkpoints, but selecting a widened warm start is not solved by zero-step preservation alone. We study dense width growth as a candidate-selection problem over full training states, including copied weights, optimizer moments, and scheduler state. In a small-scale TinyStories proxy, we compare exact-copy, perturbative, asymmetric-reset, and structured non-clone warm starts under matched continuation budgets. We evaluate zero-step preservation, short-lag probe metrics, and downstream continuation utility in deterministic and stochastic regimes. The picture is mixed and partially replicated through a reduced-pool seed-1 check. Exact-copy symmetric warm starts rank first in every completed 16-step probe and in the completed stochastic 128-step continuations at seed-0 steps 1000 and 2000 plus reduced seed-1 step 2000. By contrast, the structured non-clone challenger wins deterministic 128-step continuation. Early escape from the inherited cloned subspace is therefore not a universal selector: it helps in long deterministic continuation, but it misleads at short lag and under stochastic continuation. The result is narrow but useful: for dense width growth at this scale, preservation is not a universal ranking criterion, and the best replacement signal depends on both regime and lag budget.

item.title: Cactus: Accelerating Auto-Regressive Decoding with Constrained Acceptance Speculative Sampling
item.content:encoded: Speculative sampling (SpS) has been successful in accelerating the decoding throughput of auto-regressive large language models by leveraging smaller draft models. SpS strictly enforces the generated distribution to match that of the verifier LLM. This is unnecessarily restrictive as slight variations of the verifier's distribution, such as sampling with top-$k$ or temperature, would also be acceptable. Typical acceptance sampling (TAS) alleviates this issue by accepting more tokens using entropy-based heuristics. However, this approach distorts the verifier distribution, potentially degrading output quality when the verifier encodes critical information. In this work, we formalize the speculative sampling algorithm through the lens of constrained optimization. Based on this formulation, we propose Cactus (constrained acceptance speculative sampling), a method that guarantees controlled divergence from the verifier distribution and increasing acceptance rates. Empirical results across a wide range of benchmarks confirm the effectiveness of our approach.

item.title: Hierarchical Point-Patch Fusion with Adaptive Patch Codebook for 3D Shape Anomaly Detection
item.content:encoded: 3D shape anomaly detection is a crucial task for industrial inspection and geometric analysis. Existing deep learning approaches typically learn representations of normal shapes and identify anomalies via out-of-distribution feature detection or decoder-based reconstruction. They often fail to generalize across diverse anomaly types and scales, such as global geometric errors (e.g., planar shifts, angle misalignments), and are sensitive to noisy or incomplete local points during training. To address these limitations, we propose a hierarchical point-patch anomaly scoring network that jointly models regional part features and local point features for robust anomaly reasoning. An adaptive patchification module integrates self-supervised decomposition to capture complex structural deviations. Beyond evaluations on public benchmarks (Anomaly-ShapeNet and Real3D-AD), we release an industrial test set with real CAD models exhibiting planar, angular, and structural defects. Experiments on public and industrial datasets show superior AUC-ROC and AUC-PR performance, including over 40% point-level improvement on the new industrial anomaly type and average object-level gains of 7% on Real3D-AD and 4% on Anomaly-ShapeNet, demonstrating strong robustness and generalization.

item.title: A Machine Learning Framework for Turbofan Health Estimation via Inverse Problem Formulation
item.content:encoded: Estimating the health state of turbofan engines is a challenging ill-posed inverse problem, hindered by sparse sensing and complex nonlinear thermodynamics. Research in this area remains fragmented, with comparisons limited by the use of unrealistic datasets and insufficient exploration of the exploitation of temporal information. This work investigates how to recover component-level health indicators from operational sensor data under realistic degradation and maintenance patterns. To support this study, we introduce a new dataset that incorporates industry-oriented complexities such as maintenance events and usage changes. Using this dataset, we establish an initial benchmark that compares steady-state and nonstationary data-driven models, and Bayesian filters, classic families of methods used to solve this problem. In addition to this benchmark, we introduce self-supervised learning (SSL) approaches that learn latent representations without access to true health labels, a scenario reflective of real-world operational constraints. By comparing the downstream estimation performance of these unsupervised representations against the direct prediction baselines, we establish a practical lower bound on the difficulty of solving this inverse problem. Our results reveal that traditional filters remain strong baselines, while SSL methods reveal the intrinsic complexity of health estimation and highlight the need for more advanced and interpretable inference strategies. For reproducibility, both the generated dataset and the implementation used in this work are made accessible.

item.title: Towards Rapid Constitutive Model Discovery from Multi-Modal Data: Physics Augmented Finite Element Model Updating (paFEMU)
item.content:encoded: Recent progress in AI-enabled constitutive modeling has concentrated on moving from a purely data-driven paradigm to the enforcement of physical constraints and mechanistic principles, a concept referred to as physics augmentation. Classical phenomenological approaches rely on selecting a pre-defined model and calibrating its parameters, while machine learning methods often focus on discovery of the model itself. Sparse regression approaches lie in between, where large libraries of pre-defined models are probed during calibration. Sparsification in the aforementioned paradigm, but also in the context of neural network architecture, has been shown to enable interpretability, uncertainty quantification, but also heterogeneous software integration due to the low-dimensional nature of the resulting models. Most works in AI-enabled constitutive modeling have also focused on data from a single source, but in reality, materials modeling workflows can contain data from many different sources (multi-modal data), and also from testing other materials within the same materials class (multi-fidelity data). In this work, we introduce physics augmented finite element model updating (paFEMU), as a transfer learning approach that combines AI-enabled constitutive modeling, sparsification for interpretable model discovery, and finite element-based adjoint optimization utilizing multi-modal data. This is achieved by combining simple mechanical testing data, potentially from a distinct material, with digital image correlation-type full-field data acquisition to ultimately enable rapid constitutive modeling discovery. The simplicity of the sparse representation enables easy integration of neural constitutive models in existing finite element workflows, and also enables low-dimensional updating during transfer learning.

item.title: MONETA: Multimodal Industry Classification through Geographic Information with Multi Agent Systems
item.content:encoded: Industry classification schemes are integral parts of public and corporate databases as they classify businesses based on economic activity. Due to the size of the company registers, manual annotation is costly, and fine-tuning models with every update in industry classification schemes requires significant data collection. We replicate the manual expert verification by using existing or easily retrievable multimodal resources for industry classification. We present MONETA, the first multimodal industry classification benchmark with text (Website, Wikipedia, Wikidata) and geospatial sources (OpenStreetMap and satellite imagery). Our dataset enlists 1,000 businesses in Europe with 20 economic activity labels according to EU guidelines (NACE). Our training-free baseline reaches 62.10% and 74.10% with open and closed-source Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLM). We observe an increase of up to 22.80% with the combination of multi-turn design, context enrichment, and classification explanations. We will release our dataset and the enhanced guidelines.

item.title: Beyond LLM-as-a-Judge: Deterministic Metrics for Multilingual Generative Text Evaluation
item.content:encoded: While Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly adopted as automated judges for evaluating generated text, their outputs are often costly, and highly sensitive to prompt design, language, and aggregation strategies, severely, which limits reproducibility. To address these challenges, we propose \textbf{\textit{OmniScore}}, a family of complementary, deterministic learned metrics developed using small size ($<$1B) parameter models. OmniScore approximates LLM-judge behavior while preserving the low latency and consistency of traditional model-based scoring. We trained the models large-scale synthetic supervision ($\sim$564k instances, in \textbf{107 languages}) and evaluated using 8,617 manually annotated instances. The OmniScore family supports reliable, multi-dimensional scores across a variety of settings, including reference-based, source-grounded, and hybrid evaluations. We evaluate these models across question answering (QA), translation, and summarization in \textbf{6 languages}. Our results demonstrate that lightweight, deterministic learned metrics provide a highly practical and scalable alternative to frontier LLMs. Our models and datasets can be found at https://huggingface.co/collections/QCRI/omniscore

item.title: 模型在类比推理中的表现与内部表示的关联研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示模型在类比推理中表现与内部表示的关系。



摘要： 本文探讨了类比推理在叙事理解中的重要性，并分析了大语言模型在表面和结构线索一致时表现良好，但在需要隐含信息的类比任务中表现不佳。通过对比模型的探测表示与提示性能，发现对于修辞类比，探测方法优于提示方法；而对于叙事类比，两者表现相似但均较低。这表明模型内部表示与提示行为之间的关系依赖于任务类型，可能反映了提示方式对信息获取的限制。

MVis-Fold实现SRUS图像三维微血管高精度重建。



摘要： 本文介绍了MVis-Fold模型，这是一种基于超分辨率超声（SRUS）的三维微血管重建方法。该模型采用跨尺度网络架构，能够从二维SRUS图像中高保真地重建三维微血管网络，并精确计算传统二维SRUS无法获取的三维参数。研究验证了该模型在实体瘤三维微血管重建中的准确性与可靠性，为微血管的定量分析提供了新工具，具有重要的诊断和监测应用价值。

item.title: Jeffreys Flow：解决能量景观复杂系统采样的新方法
item.content:encoded:
提出Jeffreys Flow框架，有效防止模式崩溃并提升采样精度。



摘要： 文章介绍了Jeffreys Flow，一种基于对称Jeffreys散度的生成框架，用于改进复杂能量景观下的系统采样。该方法通过从平行温度轨迹中提炼经验采样数据，平衡局部精确性和全局模式覆盖，有效抑制模式崩溃，并校正多模分布中的误差。实验表明，该框架在高维非凸基准测试中表现出色，能够修正复制交换随机梯度Langevin动力学中的随机梯度偏差，并显著加速路径积分蒙特卡洛中的精确重要性采样。

item.title: 基于群体运动建模与轨迹引导特征融合的空中无人机群跟踪方法
item.content:encoded:
提出SCT-MOT框架提升空中无人机群跟踪性能。



摘要： 本文针对空中无人机群跟踪中因复杂非线性群体运动和弱视觉线索导致的检测失败、轨迹碎片化和身份切换问题，提出SCT-MOT框架。该框架包含Swarm Motion-Aware Trajectory Prediction (SMTP)模块和Trajectory-Guided Spatio-Temporal Feature Fusion (TG-STFF)模块，分别从群体视角建模轨迹并融合时空特征，提升了轨迹预测精度和时空一致性。实验表明，SCT-MOT在多个公开数据集上表现优于现有方法，显著提高了跟踪准确性和鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于低密度EEG的视觉重建方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出EEG2Vision框架提升低密度EEG视觉重建质量。



摘要： 本文针对非侵入式脑电（EEG）在低空间分辨率和高噪声环境下视觉刺激重建的挑战，提出了EEG2Vision框架。该框架通过端到端的方式评估不同EEG通道数（128、64、32、24）下的重建性能，并引入提示引导的后处理机制提升图像质量。实验表明，随着通道数减少，语义解码准确率显著下降，但重建质量仅轻微下降。Boosting机制在所有配置中均有效提升感知指标，用户研究也验证了其优越性，为实时脑-图像应用提供了新可能。

item.title: Strengthening Human-Centric Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Integrity in LLMs via a Structured Prompt Framework
item.content:encoded: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting has been used to enhance the reasoning capability of LLMs. However, its reliability in security-sensitive analytical tasks remains insufficiently examined, particularly under structured human evaluation. Alternative approaches, such as model scaling and fine-tuning can be used to help improve performance. These methods are also often costly, computationally intensive, or difficult to audit. In contrast, prompt engineering provides a lightweight, transparent, and controllable mechanism for guiding LLM reasoning. This study proposes a structured prompt engineering framework designed to strengthen CoT reasoning integrity while improving security threat and attack detection reliability in local LLM deployments. The framework includes 16 factors grouped into four core dimensions: (1) Context and Scope Control, (2) Evidence Grounding and Traceability, (3) Reasoning Structure and Cognitive Control, and (4) Security-Specific Analytical Constraints. Rather than optimizing the wording of the prompt heuristically, the framework introduces explicit reasoning controls to mitigate hallucination and prevent reasoning drift, as well as strengthening interpretability in security-sensitive contexts. Using DDoS attack detection in SDN traffic as a case study, multiple model families were evaluated under structured and unstructured prompting conditions. Pareto frontier analysis and ablation experiments demonstrate consistent reasoning improvements (up to 40% in smaller models) and stable accuracy gains across scales. Human evaluation with strong inter-rater agreement (Cohen's k > 0.80) confirms robustness. The results establish structured prompting as an effective and practical approach for reliable and explainable AI-driven cybersecurity analysis.

item.title: BADAS-2.0：碰撞预警系统的最新进展
item.content:encoded:
BADAS-2.0提升碰撞预警系统性能，增强长尾场景识别与实时推理能力。



摘要： BADAS-2.0是碰撞预警系统的第二代版本，相较于前代在三个关键方面取得突破：构建了针对罕见和高风险场景的10组长尾基准，并通过主动标注方法扩大数据集至178,500个标记视频；采用知识蒸馏技术，将模型压缩为轻量级版本，实现7-12倍速度提升；引入可解释性机制，生成实时注意力热图并结合视觉语言模型进行推理。该系统在长尾场景中表现显著提升，且代码和评估基准已公开。

item.title: NIH人工智能与机器学习研究现状分析
item.content:encoded:
NIH人工智能研究占15.9%，但临床转化不足。



摘要： 本文通过大规模语义分析，对2025年58,746项NIH资助的生物医学研究项目进行了AI和ML应用的全面分析。结果显示，AI占NIH项目组合的15.9%，且获得更高的资金支持，主要集中在疾病领域的发现、预测和数据整合。然而，79%的AI项目仍处于研发阶段，仅有14.7%进入临床部署，同时健康不平等研究仅占5.7%，远低于其在NIH使命中的重要性。研究提出了一种人机协作的方法，为制定基于证据的政策提供依据，以促进AI研究与健康公平目标的一致性。

item.title: 平行采样与序列采样在大型推理模型中的比较研究
item.content:encoded:
平行采样优于序列采样，主要因探索不足。



摘要： 本文比较了平行采样和序列采样在大型推理模型中的表现，发现尽管序列采样理论上具有更强的表示能力，但平行采样仍表现出更优性能。作者提出三个假设，包括聚合操作、上下文长度和探索不足的影响。通过多个模型和任务的实验验证，发现上下文长度和聚合操作并非主要原因，而探索不足是导致性能差距的主要因素。

item.title: 面向智能体的奖励模型评估基准Plan-RewardBench
item.content:encoded:
提出Plan-RewardBench评估智能体轨迹偏好能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了Plan-RewardBench，这是一个用于评估奖励模型在工具集成环境中的轨迹偏好判断能力的基准。该基准覆盖了安全拒绝、工具无关性/不可用性、复杂规划和鲁棒错误恢复等任务类型，包含经过验证的正样本和混淆负样本。研究对生成式、判别式和基于大语言模型的奖励模型进行了统一评估，揭示了其在长时序轨迹上的性能下降问题，强调了针对智能体轨迹建模的专门训练需求。Plan-RewardBench不仅是一个评估工具，也为构建智能体规划偏好数据提供了可复用的蓝图。

item.content:encoded:
DAERT框架有效暴露VLA模型在语言变化下的安全漏洞。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的DAERT框架，用于检测视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在面对语言变化时的安全漏洞。传统强化学习方法在红队测试中容易陷入模式崩溃，无法全面揭示风险。DAERT通过评估统一策略，生成多样化的挑战性指令，显著提升了攻击效果，在物理模拟器中将任务成功率从93.33%降至5.85%，为VLA模型的安全性提供了更全面的测试手段。

item.title: 多语言推理特征分析与模型优化研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示多语言推理特征对模型性能的影响差异。



摘要： 本文探讨了多语言推理模型（LRMs）在不同语言间的性能差异，挑战了将英语推理模式直接应用于其他语言的假设。通过定义一系列可量化的推理特征，并利用逻辑回归分析这些特征与最终答案准确性的关联性，研究进一步使用稀疏自编码器发现潜在的推理概念。实验结果表明，尽管多数特征与准确性呈正相关，但其影响强度在不同语言间差异显著，甚至在某些语言中出现反向关系。这表明需要针对不同语言设计适应性的推理目标，以提升多语言推理模型的整体表现。

item.title: 基于语义引导的工业异常图像生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出GroundingAnomaly框架，提升工业异常检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对工业质量控制中异常样本稀缺的问题，提出一种新的少样本异常图像生成框架GroundingAnomaly。该框架通过引入空间条件模块和门控自注意力模块，实现对合成异常的精确空间控制和稳定适应。实验表明，GroundingAnomaly在MVTec AD和VisA数据集上表现优异，有效提升了异常检测、分割及实例级检测等任务的性能。

item.title: HingeMem：基于边界引导的长期记忆机制提升对话系统性能
item.content:encoded:
HingeMem通过边界触发机制提升对话系统的长期记忆与检索效率。



摘要： 本文提出HingeMem，一种基于事件分割理论的边界引导长期记忆机制，用于提升对话系统的持续交互能力。该方法通过边界触发的超边结构，对人物、时间、地点和主题四个元素进行索引，有效减少冗余操作并保留关键上下文。同时，HingeMem引入查询自适应检索机制，根据查询类型动态决定检索内容和深度，显著提升检索效率。实验表明，在LOCOMO数据集上，HingeMem在不指定查询类别的情况下相比基线模型提升了约20%，且计算成本降低了68%。该方法不仅推动了记忆建模的发展，还适用于需要高效可靠记忆的网络应用。

item.title: 基于证据理论的终身分类器在雷达脉冲识别中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出ELC模型提升雷达脉冲识别的可靠性与置信度。



摘要： 本文研究了在电磁战中雷达脉冲分类的重要性，提出一种结合不确定性量化和终身学习的证据终身分类器（ELC）。该方法利用证据理论建模认知不确定性，并通过Learn-Prune-Share机制实现持续学习。实验对比了ELC与贝叶斯终身分类器（BLC）在合成雷达和射频指纹数据集上的表现，结果表明基于证据不确定性的选择性预测在低信噪比条件下显著提升了召回率，证明了ELC在提高系统可信度方面的有效性。

item.content:encoded:
多模态模型在生存预测中校准不足，影响临床应用。



摘要： 本文首次系统评估了多模态全切片图像与基因组数据融合模型在癌症生存预测中的校准性能。研究发现，多个模型在多数测试中未能通过1-校准检验，表明其预测概率存在偏差。实验显示基于门控的融合方法比其他方法更优，而后处理方法如Platt缩放可改善校准但不影响判别能力。研究强调仅依靠C-index不足以评估临床适用性。

item.title: 推荐系统中训练数据规模对性能的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
更多训练数据持续提升推荐系统性能，无明显饱和点。



摘要： 本文研究了推荐系统中训练数据规模对离线推荐性能的影响，通过在多个大型数据集上进行实验，分析了不同样本量下的NDCG@10指标表现。结果表明，随着训练数据量的增加，推荐性能通常持续提升，未观察到明显的饱和点。通过对结果进行归一化处理，进一步验证了这一趋势。实验还发现，在数据集和算法的特殊情况下，性能提升可能较弱。总体而言，传统推荐系统在典型用户-物品交互数据上仍受益于更多的训练数据。

item.title: 基于时空感知的ADAS DNN故障注入框架研究
item.content:encoded:
STAFI框架有效识别ADAS中关键故障位，提升系统安全性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为SpatioTemporal-Aware Fault Injection (STAFI)的框架，用于在高级驾驶辅助系统（ADAS）的深度神经网络（DNN）中高效定位关键故障点。该框架包括空间上的Progressive Metric-guided Bit Search (PMBS)方法，用于识别对驾驶行为影响最大的权重位；以及时间上的Critical Fault Time Identification (CFTI)机制，用于确定触发故障的最佳时机，以最大化安全影响。实验表明，STAFI比现有最强基线方法多发现29.56倍的危险性故障。

item.title: LLMs能否重新发明计算机科学基础算法
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨LLMs是否能重新发明基础算法，发现其创新潜力有限。



摘要： 本文研究了大型语言模型（LLMs）是否具备重新发明计算机科学基础算法的能力。通过提出一种名为“Unlearn-and-Reinvent”的方法，研究人员从LLM的预训练知识中移除特定算法（如Dijkstra或欧几里得算法），并测试其在受控环境中重新发明这些算法的能力。实验结果显示，Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507模型在无提示情况下成功重新发明50%的算法，提示等级越高成功率越高，但复杂算法仍面临挑战。研究还发现，在推理过程中生成验证器对维持模型推理能力至关重要，有助于避免“思维崩溃”现象。该研究揭示了LLMs在创新思维方面的潜力与局限。

item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型LRM选择性遗忘框架，保护敏感信息同时保持推理能力。



摘要： 本文针对大型推理模型（LRMs）在推理过程中可能泄露敏感信息的问题，提出一种选择性遗忘方法。该方法通过多模型与检索增强生成技术分析推理链，识别并替换敏感内容，同时保留逻辑结构。引入特征替换遗忘损失函数，在抑制生成敏感内容的同时增强合法替代内容的生成。实验表明该方法在合成和医学数据集上均表现出色，有效平衡了知识遗忘与推理能力的保持。

item.title: 基于宇宙射线μ子成像的混凝土结构缺陷分割方法SA-DSVN
item.content:encoded:
SA-DSVN通过联合处理散射动力学和次级电磁簇射信息，提升混凝土缺陷检测精度。



摘要： 本文提出SA-DSVN，一种用于宇宙射线μ子成像中混凝土结构缺陷的体素级分割3D卷积架构。与传统方法不同，该方法同时处理散射动力学（9个通道）和次级电磁簇射多重性（40个通道），并通过交叉注意力机制融合。训练数据由Vega模拟生成，包含900个体积，涵盖四种缺陷类型。消融实验表明，簇射多重性信息对区分度贡献显著，使Dice系数从0.535提升至0.685。模型在60个独立验证体积上达到96.3%的体素准确率，各缺陷Dice分数为0.59-0.81，且在10毫秒内完成推理，具有高检测灵敏度。

item.title: 医学图像分类中语义漂移的分析与评估
item.content:encoded:
研究医学图像分类中模型推理稳定性变化。



摘要： 本文探讨了在医学图像分类任务中，迁移学习后微调过程中可能出现的语义漂移现象。尽管分类准确率保持稳定，但模型的视觉证据支持结构可能发生系统性变化。通过五类胸部X光图像任务，评估了DenseNet201、ResNet50V2和InceptionV3三种架构的模型表现，并使用无参考指标量化了特征图的空间定位和结构一致性。结果显示，粗略解剖定位保持稳定，但重叠IoU显示出明显的架构依赖性变化。此外，不同归因方法（如LayerCAM和GradCAM++）在预测性能稳定时可能表现出不同的稳定性排名，表明解释稳定性是架构、优化阶段和归因目标之间的相互作用结果。

item.title: 基于空间感知图神经网络的电力中断预测模型研究
item.content:encoded:
SA-HGNN提升极端天气导致的停电预测准确性。



摘要： 文章介绍了为应对气候变化带来的极端天气事件对电力系统的影响，研究团队开发了空间感知混合图神经网络（SA-HGNN）以提高停电预测模型（OPM）的准确性。该模型通过编码静态特征和动态天气特征的空间关系，并结合对比学习解决不同天气事件的数据不平衡问题，从而生成位置特定的嵌入表示。在四个地区进行的实验表明，SA-HGNN在停电预测任务中表现优异，具有广泛应用前景。

item.title: GenomeQA：评估通用大语言模型在基因组序列推理任务中的基准
item.content:encoded:
GenomeQA评估LLMs在基因组序列推理任务中的表现。



摘要： 文章介绍了GenomeQA，一个用于评估通用大语言模型（LLMs）在基于序列的基因组推理任务中的基准。该基准包含5200个样本，涵盖六类任务，如增强子和启动子识别、剪接位点识别等。研究发现，LLMs在局部序列信号上表现良好，但在需要多步推理的任务中性能下降。GenomeQA为研究和改进LLMs在原始基因组序列上的应用提供了诊断性基准。

item.title: 探索转向向量在大语言模型中的作用机制
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示转向向量通过注意力机制影响模型输出。



摘要： 本文研究了转向向量在大语言模型中的作用机制，发现其主要通过OV电路影响注意力机制，而对QK电路影响较小。通过多标记激活补丁框架，研究人员发现不同转向方法在相同层使用功能可互换的电路。数学分解进一步揭示了语义可解释的概念，即使转向向量本身不具语义。研究还表明，转向向量可被稀疏化90%-99%而不显著影响性能，且不同方法在部分关键维度上达成一致。

item.title: WorldMAP：利用世界模型生成结构化监督的导航方法
item.content:encoded:
WorldMAP通过世界模型生成结构化监督，提升导航轨迹预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出WorldMAP，一种基于教师-学生框架的方法，将世界模型生成的未来视图转化为持久的语义-空间结构和规划衍生的监督信号。教师模块从生成视频中构建语义空间记忆，定位任务相关目标与障碍，并通过显式规划生成轨迹伪标签。学生模型则直接从视觉-语言输入预测导航轨迹。在Target-Bench基准测试中，WorldMAP在ADE和FDE指标上优于现有方法，显著提升了导航性能，表明世界模型在具身导航中的价值可能在于提供结构化监督而非直接提供行动证据。

item.title: CODE-GEN：基于RAG的AI系统生成代码理解题
item.content:encoded:
CODE-GEN通过AI生成代码理解题，提升学生编程能力。



摘要： CODE-GEN是一种结合人类反馈和检索增强生成（RAG）技术的智能系统，用于生成与课程目标一致的多选编程题，以提高学生的代码推理和理解能力。该系统由生成器和验证器两个代理组成，分别负责生成题目和评估内容质量。通过六位专家对288道AI生成题目的评估，结果显示系统在多个教学维度上表现优异，成功率达79.9%至98.6%。尽管AI在明确标准的维度上表现出色，但在需要深入教学判断的方面仍需人类参与，如设计有意义的干扰项和提供高质量反馈。

item.title: Q-Zoom：一种高效的查询感知高分辨率视觉感知框架
item.content:encoded:
Q-Zoom提升高分辨率任务的推理效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出Q-Zoom，一种基于查询感知的自适应高分辨率视觉感知框架，旨在解决现有模型在处理高分辨率任务时因冗余视觉令牌导致的推理瓶颈问题。Q-Zoom通过轻量级动态门控网络和自蒸馏区域建议网络，在保证精度的同时显著提升推理速度。实验表明，Q-Zoom在文档识别和高分辨率场景中分别提升了2.52倍和4.39倍的推理速度，同时保持或超越了基线模型的性能。该方法在多个模型上均表现出色，具有广泛的应用前景。

item.title: 基于FDSOI-FeFET的贝叶斯决策树硬件平台设计
item.content:encoded:
新型硬件平台提升贝叶斯决策树性能与能效。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于FDSOI-FeFET的单片硬件平台，支持ACAM和GRNG功能，用于提升贝叶斯决策树在自动驾驶、医疗诊断等场景中的性能。该平台利用铁电FET的极化特性实现高效多比特存储，并通过漏源重叠区的隧穿效应生成高质量熵源。系统评估表明，该架构在MNIST数据集上比传统决策树提高40%以上分类准确率，且相比CPU和GPU在速度和能效方面分别提升两个和四个数量级，适用于资源受限和安全关键环境。

item.title: Efficient Onboard Spacecraft Pose Estimation with Event Cameras and Neuromorphic Hardware
item.content:encoded: Reliable relative pose estimation is a key enabler for autonomous rendezvous and proximity operations, yet space imagery is notoriously challenging due to extreme illumination, high contrast, and fast target motion. Event cameras provide asynchronous, change-driven measurements that can remain informative when frame-based imagery saturates or blurs, while neuromorphic processors can exploit sparse activations for low-latency, energy-efficient inferences. This paper presents a spacecraft 6-DoF pose-estimation pipeline that couples event-based vision with the BrainChip Akida neuromorphic processor. Using the SPADES dataset, we train compact MobileNet-style keypoint regression networks on lightweight event-frame representations, apply quantization-aware training (8/4-bit), and convert the models to Akida-compatible spiking neural networks. We benchmark three event representations and demonstrate real-time, low-power inference on Akida V1 hardware. We additionally design a heatmap-based model targeting Akida V2 and evaluate it on Akida Cloud, yielding improved pose accuracy. To our knowledge, this is the first end-to-end demonstration of spacecraft pose estimation running on Akida hardware, highlighting a practical route to low-latency, low-power perception for future autonomous space missions.

item.title: Plasma GraphRAG: Physics-Grounded Parameter Selection for Gyrokinetic Simulations
item.content:encoded: Accurate parameter selection is fundamental to gyrokinetic plasma simulations, yet current practices rely heavily on manual literature reviews, leading to inefficiencies and inconsistencies. We introduce Plasma GraphRAG, a novel framework that integrates Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) with large language models (LLMs) for automated, physics-grounded parameter range identification. By constructing a domain-specific knowledge graph from curated plasma literature and enabling structured retrieval over graph-anchored entities and relations, Plasma GraphRAG enables LLMs to generate accurate, context-aware recommendations. Extensive evaluations across five metrics, comprehensiveness, diversity, grounding, hallucination, and empowerment, demonstrate that Plasma GraphRAG outperforms vanilla RAG by over $10\%$ in overall quality and reduces hallucination rates by up to $25\%$. {Beyond enhancing simulation reliability, Plasma GraphRAG offers a methodology for accelerating scientific discovery across complex, data-rich domains.

item.title: Visual prompting reimagined: The power of the Activation Prompts
item.content:encoded: Visual prompting (VP) has emerged as a popular method to repurpose pretrained vision models for adaptation to downstream tasks. Unlike conventional model fine-tuning techniques, VP introduces a universal perturbation directly into the input data to facilitate task-specific fine-tuning rather than modifying model parameters. However, there exists a noticeable performance gap between VP and conventional fine-tuning methods, highlighting an unexplored realm in theory and practice to understand and advance the input-level VP to reduce its current performance gap. Towards this end, we introduce a generalized concept, termed activation prompt (AP), which extends the scope of the input-level VP by enabling universal perturbations to be applied to activation maps within the intermediate layers of the model. By using AP to revisit the problem of VP and employing it as an analytical tool, we demonstrate the intrinsic limitations of VP in both performance and efficiency, revealing why input-level prompting may lack effectiveness compared to AP, which exhibits a model-dependent layer preference. We show that AP is closely related to normalization tuning in convolutional neural networks and vision transformers, although each model type has distinct layer preferences for prompting. We also theoretically elucidate the rationale behind such a preference by analyzing global features across layers. Through extensive experiments across 29 datasets and various model architectures, we provide a comprehensive performance analysis of AP, comparing it with VP and parameter-efficient fine-tuning baselines. Our results demonstrate AP's superiority in both accuracy and efficiency, considering factors such as time, parameters, memory usage, and throughput.

item.title: DataSTORM: Deep Research on Large-Scale Databases using Exploratory Data Analysis and Data Storytelling
item.content:encoded: Deep research with Large Language Model (LLM) agents is emerging as a powerful paradigm for multi-step information discovery, synthesis, and analysis. However, existing approaches primarily focus on unstructured web data, while the challenges of conducting deep research over large-scale structured databases remain relatively underexplored. Unlike web-based research, effective data-centric research requires more than retrieval and summarization and demands iterative hypothesis generation, quantitative reasoning over structured schemas, and convergence toward a coherent analytical narrative. In this paper, we present DataSTORM, an LLM-based agentic system capable of autonomously conducting research across both large-scale structured databases and internet sources. Grounded in principles from Exploratory Data Analysis and Data Storytelling, DataSTORM reframes deep research over structured data as a thesis-driven analytical process: discovering candidate theses from data, validating them through iterative cross-source investigation, and developing them into coherent analytical narratives. We evaluate DataSTORM on InsightBench, where it achieves a new state-of-the-art result with a 19.4% relative improvement in insight-level recall and 7.2% in summary-level score. We further introduce a new dataset built on ACLED, a real-world complex database, and demonstrate that DataSTORM outperforms proprietary systems such as ChatGPT Deep Research across both automated metrics and human evaluations.

item.title: Stochastic Auto-conditioned Fast Gradient Methods with Optimal Rates
item.content:encoded: Achieving optimal rates for stochastic composite convex optimization without prior knowledge of problem parameters remains a central challenge. In the deterministic setting, the auto-conditioned fast gradient method has recently been proposed to attain optimal accelerated rates without line-search procedures or prior knowledge of the Lipschitz smoothness constant, providing a natural prototype for parameter-free acceleration. However, extending this approach to the stochastic setting has proven technically challenging and remains open. Existing parameter-free stochastic methods either fail to achieve accelerated rates or rely on restrictive assumptions, such as bounded domains, bounded gradients, prior knowledge of the iteration horizon, or strictly sub-Gaussian noise. To address these limitations, we propose a stochastic variant of the auto-conditioned fast gradient method, referred to as stochastic AC-FGM. The proposed method is fully adaptive to the Lipschitz constant, the iteration horizon, and the noise level, enabling both adaptive stepsize selection and adaptive mini-batch sizing without line-search procedures. Under standard bounded conditional variance assumptions, we show that stochastic AC-FGM achieves the optimal iteration complexity of $O(1/\sqrt{\varepsilon})$ and the optimal sample complexity of $O(1/\varepsilon^2)$.

item.title: Select-then-Solve: Paradigm Routing as Inference-Time Optimization for LLM Agents
item.content:encoded: When an LLM-based agent improves on a task, is the gain from the model itself or from the reasoning paradigm wrapped around it? We study this question by comparing six inference-time paradigms, namely Direct, CoT, ReAct, Plan-Execute, Reflection, and ReCode, across four frontier LLMs and ten benchmarks, yielding roughly 18,000 runs. We find that reasoning structure helps dramatically on some tasks but hurts on others: ReAct improves over Direct by 44pp on GAIA, while CoT degrades performance by 15pp on HumanEval. No single paradigm dominates, and oracle per-task selection beats the best fixed paradigm by 17.1pp on average. Motivated by this complementarity, we propose a select-then-solve approach: before answering each task, a lightweight embedding-based router selects the most suitable paradigm. Across four models, the router improves average accuracy from 47.6% to 53.1%, outperforming the best fixed paradigm at 50.3% by 2.8pp and recovering up to 37% of the oracle gap. In contrast, zero-shot self-routing only works for GPT-5 at 67.1% and fails for weaker models, all trailing the learned router. Our results argue that reasoning paradigm selection should be a per-task decision made by a learned router, not a fixed architectural choice.

item.title: Emergent social transmission of model-based representations without inference
item.content:encoded: How do people acquire rich, flexible knowledge about their environment from others despite limited cognitive capacity? Humans are often thought to rely on computationally costly mentalizing, such as inferring others' beliefs. In contrast, cultural evolution emphasizes that behavioral transmission can be supported by simple social cues. Using reinforcement learning simulations, we show how minimal social learning can indirectly transmit higher-level representations. We simulate a naïve agent searching for rewards in a reconfigurable environment, learning either alone or by observing an expert - crucially, without inferring mental states. Instead, the learner heuristically selects actions or boosts value representations based on observed actions. Our results demonstrate that these cues bias the learner's experience, causing its representation to converge toward the expert's. Model-based learners benefit most from social exposure, showing faster learning and more expert-like representations. These findings show how cultural transmission can arise from simple, non-mentalizing processes exploiting asocial learning mechanisms.

item.title: PCA-Driven Adaptive Sensor Triage for Edge AI Inference
item.content:encoded: Multi-channel sensor networks in industrial IoT often exceed available bandwidth. We propose PCA-Triage, a streaming algorithm that converts incremental PCA loadings into proportional per-channel sampling rates under a bandwidth budget. PCA-Triage runs in O(wdk) time with zero trainable parameters (0.67 ms per decision). We evaluate on 7 benchmarks (8--82 channels) against 9 baselines. PCA-Triage is the best unsupervised method on 3 of 6 datasets at 50% bandwidth, winning 5 of 6 against every baseline with large effect sizes (r = 0.71--0.91). On TEP, it achieves F1 = 0.961 +/- 0.001 -- within 0.1% of full-data performance -- while maintaining F1 > 0.90 at 30% budget. Targeted extensions push F1 to 0.970. The algorithm is robust to packet loss and sensor noise (3.7--4.8% degradation under combined worst-case).

item.title: The Theory and Practice of Highly Scalable Gaussian Process Regression with Nearest Neighbours
item.content:encoded: Gaussian process ($GP$) regression is a widely used non-parametric modeling tool, but its cubic complexity in the training size limits its use on massive data sets. A practical remedy is to predict using only the nearest neighbours of each test point, as in Nearest Neighbour Gaussian Process ($NNGP$) regression for geospatial problems and the related scalable $GPnn$ method for more general machine-learning applications. Despite their strong empirical performance, the large-$n$ theory of $NNGP/GPnn$ remains incomplete. We develop a theoretical framework for $NNGP$ and $GPnn$ regression. Under mild regularity assumptions, we derive almost sure pointwise limits for three key predictive criteria: mean squared error ($MSE$), calibration coefficient ($CAL$), and negative log-likelihood ($NLL$). We then study the $L_2$-risk, prove universal consistency, and show that the risk attains Stone's minimax rate $n^{-2α/(2p+d)}$, where $α$ and $p$ capture regularity of the regression problem. We also prove uniform convergence of $MSE$ over compact hyper-parameter sets and show that its derivatives with respect to lengthscale, kernel scale, and noise variance vanish asymptotically, with explicit rates. This explains the observed robustness of $GPnn$ to hyper-parameter tuning. These results provide a rigorous statistical foundation for $NNGP/GPnn$ as a highly scalable and principled alternative to full $GP$ models.

item.title: Unsupervised Multi-agent and Single-agent Perception from Cooperative Views
item.content:encoded: The LiDAR-based multi-agent and single-agent perception has shown promising performance in environmental understanding for robots and automated vehicles. However, there is no existing method that simultaneously solves both multi-agent and single-agent perception in an unsupervised way. By sharing sensor data between multiple agents via communication, this paper discovers two key insights: 1) Improved point cloud density after the data sharing from cooperative views could benefit unsupervised object classification, 2) Cooperative view of multiple agents can be used as unsupervised guidance for the 3D object detection in the single view. Based on these two discovered insights, we propose an Unsupervised Multi-agent and Single-agent (UMS) perception framework that leverages multi-agent cooperation without human annotations to simultaneously solve multi-agent and single-agent perception. UMS combines a learning-based Proposal Purifying Filter to better classify the candidate proposals after multi-agent point cloud density cooperation, followed by a Progressive Proposal Stabilizing module to yield reliable pseudo labels by the easy-to-hard curriculum learning. Furthermore, we design a Cross-View Consensus Learning to use multi-agent cooperative view to guide detection in single-agent view. Experimental results on two public datasets V2V4Real and OPV2V show that our UMS method achieved significantly higher 3D detection performance than the state-of-the-art methods on both multi-agent and single-agent perception tasks in an unsupervised setting.

item.title: Toward a Sustainable Software Architecture Community: Evaluating ICSA's Environmental Impact
item.content:encoded: Generative AI (GenAI) tools are increasingly integrated into software architecture research, yet the environmental impact of their computational usage remains largely undocumented. This study presents the first systematic audit of the carbon footprint of both the digital footprint from GenAI usage in research papers, and the traditional footprint from conference activities within the context of the IEEE International Conference on Software Architecture (ICSA). We report two separate carbon inventories relevant to the software architecture research community: i) an exploratory estimate of the footprint of GenAI inference usage associated with accepted papers within a research-artifact boundary, and ii) the conference attendance and operations footprint of ICSA 2025 (travel, accommodation, catering, venue energy, and materials) within the conference time boundary. These two inventories, with different system boundaries and completeness, support transparency and community reflection. We discuss implications for sustainable software architecture, including recommendations for transparency, greener conference planning, and improved energy efficiency in GenAI operations. Our work supports a more climate-conscious research culture within the ICSA community and beyond

item.title: LAA-X: Unified Localized Artifact Attention for Quality-Agnostic and Generalizable Face Forgery Detection
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we propose Localized Artifact Attention X (LAA-X), a novel deepfake detection framework that is both robust to high-quality forgeries and capable of generalizing to unseen manipulations. Existing approaches typically rely on binary classifiers coupled with implicit attention mechanisms, which often fail to generalize beyond known manipulations. In contrast, LAA-X introduces an explicit attention strategy based on a multi-task learning framework combined with blending-based data synthesis. Auxiliary tasks are designed to guide the model toward localized, artifact-prone (i.e., vulnerable) regions. The proposed framework is compatible with both CNN and transformer backbones, resulting in two different versions, namely, LAA-Net and LAA-Former, respectively. Despite being trained only on real and pseudo-fake samples, LAA-X competes with state-of-the-art methods across multiple benchmarks. Code and pre-trained weights for LAA-Net\footnote{https://github.com/10Ring/LAA-Net} and LAA-Former\footnote{https://github.com/10Ring/LAA-Former} are publicly available.

item.title: FORGE:Fine-grained Multimodal Evaluation for Manufacturing Scenarios
item.content:encoded: The manufacturing sector is increasingly adopting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to transition from simple perception to autonomous execution, yet current evaluations fail to reflect the rigorous demands of real-world manufacturing environments. Progress is hindered by data scarcity and a lack of fine-grained domain semantics in existing datasets. To bridge this gap, we introduce FORGE. Wefirst construct a high-quality multimodal dataset that combines real-world 2D images and 3D point clouds, annotated with fine-grained domain semantics (e.g., exact model numbers). We then evaluate 18 state-of-the-art MLLMs across three manufacturing tasks, namely workpiece verification, structural surface inspection, and assembly verification, revealing significant performance gaps. Counter to conventional understanding, the bottleneck analysis shows that visual grounding is not the primary limiting factor. Instead, insufficient domain-specific knowledge is the key bottleneck, setting a clear direction for future research. Beyond evaluation, we show that our structured annotations can serve as an actionable training resource: supervised fine-tuning of a compact 3B-parameter model on our data yields up to 90.8% relative improvement in accuracy on held-out manufacturing scenarios, providing preliminary evidence for a practical pathway toward domain-adapted manufacturing MLLMs. The code and datasets are available at https://ai4manufacturing.github.io/forge-web.

item.title: PRIME: Prototype-Driven Multimodal Pretraining for Cancer Prognosis with Missing Modalities
item.content:encoded: Multimodal self-supervised pretraining offers a promising route to cancer prognosis by integrating histopathology whole-slide images, gene expression, and pathology reports, yet most existing approaches require fully paired and complete inputs. In practice, clinical cohorts are fragmented and often miss one or more modalities, limiting both supervised fusion and scalable multimodal pretraining. We propose PRIME, a missing-aware multimodal self-supervised pretraining framework that learns robust and transferable representations from partially observed cohorts. PRIME maps heterogeneous modality embeddings into a unified token space and introduces a shared prototype memory bank for latent-space semantic imputation via patient-level consensus retrieval, producing structurally aligned tokens without reconstructing raw signals. Two complementary pretraining objectives: inter-modality alignment and post-fusion consistency under structured missingness augmentation, jointly learn representations that remain predictive under arbitrary modality subsets. We evaluate PRIME on The Cancer Genome Atlas with label-free pretraining on 32 cancer types and downstream 5-fold evaluation on five cohorts across overall survival prediction, 3-year mortality classification, and 3-year recurrence classification. PRIME achieves the best macro-average performance among all compared methods, reaching 0.653 C-index, 0.689 AUROC, and 0.637 AUROC on the three tasks, respectively, while improving robustness under test-time missingness and supporting parameter-efficient and label-efficient adaptation. These results support missing-aware multimodal pretraining as a practical strategy for prognosis modeling in fragmented clinical data settings.

item.title: 神经符号深度知识追踪提升教育AI的可解释性与适应性
item.content:encoded:
神经符号方法提升教育AI的性能与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出 Responsible-DKT，一种结合符号化教育知识的神经符号深度知识追踪模型，用于改进智能辅导系统的学习者建模。该方法在真实数学学习数据集上表现优于传统模型，具有更高的AUC值和更好的时间一致性。同时，其具备内在可解释性，能提供局部和全局的预测逻辑说明，并验证了教学假设，如重复错误响应对预测的影响。该研究为更负责任、以人为核心的教育AI提供了新方向。

item.title: SOLAR：一种高效的PEFT适配器压缩框架
item.content:encoded:
SOLAR通过子空间重构降低PEFT适配器通信成本。



摘要： 本文提出SOLAR（Subspace-Oriented Latent Adapter Reparameterization），一种用于参数高效微调（PEFT）适配器的后训练压缩框架。SOLAR通过将PEFT更新表示为基础模型奇异向量的线性组合，并引入受控随机扰动，显著降低了通信和存储成本。该方法利用基础模型与任务特定微调更新之间的子空间相似性，解耦适配器大小与PEFT结构，实现紧凑而富有表现力的表示。SOLAR具有模型无关性，兼容LoRA、AdaLoRA等现有PEFT方法。理论分析证明了重建误差的上界，实验表明其在语言和视觉任务中有效保持性能的同时大幅减少模型表示尺寸，适用于分布式系统和边缘设备部署。

item.title: MESA：一种有效缓解大型视觉语言模型幻觉的框架
item.content:encoded:
MESA通过选择性潜空间干预减少幻觉，同时保持生成行为。



摘要： 本文提出MESA，一个用于缓解大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）幻觉的高效框架。现有方法在抑制幻觉时会改变生成行为，导致输出变短且词元分布偏移。MESA通过有选择地干预潜空间，仅针对幻觉相关响应进行调整，从而在减少幻觉的同时保持原始生成行为。实验表明，MESA在多个生成和判别基准上均优于以往方法，有效提升了模型的准确性与稳定性。

item.title: 时间序列分类中的性能与资源平衡评估
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种评估框架，研究TSC模型的性能与资源消耗平衡。



摘要： 时间序列分类（TSC）在多个领域有重要应用，但缺乏对模型、数据集和硬件之间性能权衡的统一理解。本文引入一个全面的评估框架，探索预测性能与资源消耗之间的平衡。通过理论上有界的数据剪枝策略，作者对领先的混合分类器进行优化，提出了新的可剪枝模型Hydrant。在20个MONSTER数据集、13种方法和三种计算配置下进行了超过4000次实验，系统分析了模型设计、超参数和硬件选择对实际TSC性能的影响。结果表明，剪枝可以显著降低能耗达80%，同时保持接近95%的准确率。该方法、实验结果及配套软件推动了TSC向可持续和可复现的方向发展。

item.title: Tarot-SAM3：一种无需训练的参考表达分割框架
item.content:encoded:
Tarot-SAM3通过两个阶段提升参考表达分割性能。



摘要： 本文提出Tarot-SAM3，一种无需训练的参考表达分割框架，旨在解决现有方法在处理长或隐晦表达时的不足。该框架包含两个关键阶段：表达推理解释器（ERI）通过引入推理辅助提示选项，将任意查询转化为稳健的异构提示；掩码自精炼（MSR）阶段则通过DINOv3的特征关系优化分割结果，提升准确性。实验表明，Tarot-SAM3在显式和隐式参考表达分割任务以及开放世界场景中均表现出色。

item.title: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习方法在数学问题求解中的应用
item.content:encoded:
RLVR提升低k推理准确率，但需优化提示机制以提升性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于可验证奖励的强化学习方法（RLVR），用于提升数学问题求解的准确性。研究发现，虽然pass@1指标提升，但大k性能未必同步改善。现有基于提示的方法虽能提高训练可行性，但存在教师-学生分布不匹配和提示暴露过多的问题。为此，作者提出两种改进方法：分布对齐提示合成（DAHS）和反向提示退火（BHA）。通过在AIME24、AIME25和AIME26数据集上进行实验，结果表明该方法在Qwen3-1.7B-Base模型上显著提升了pass@1和pass@2048指标，在Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct模型上则主要提升大k性能。研究强调，合理使用提示机制有助于早期提升学习效果，并在无提示评估前逐步减少提示依赖。

item.title: 提升多模态推理模型的逻辑一致性与视觉基础
item.content:encoded:
FGRPO提升多模态推理模型的逻辑一致性和视觉基础，提高推理质量。



摘要： 本文研究了基于强化学习与可验证奖励训练的多模态推理模型（MRMs）在视觉推理基准测试中的表现。尽管这些模型在准确性上有所提升，但其生成的思维链（CoT）常常与最终答案不一致，且缺乏对视觉证据的有效支撑。作者通过七个空间推理基准测试发现这一问题广泛存在。为此，提出了Faithful GRPO（FGRPO），一种改进的GRPO方法，通过拉格朗日对偶上升法强制约束逻辑一致性和视觉基础。实验表明，FGRPO显著提升了推理质量，将不一致性从24.5%降至1.7%，并提高了视觉基础评分13%。同时，FGRPO还提升了最终答案的准确性，证明了忠实推理对提升回答质量的重要性。

item.title: SelfEvo：无需标注数据的自提升多视角重建框架
item.content:encoded:
SelfEvo通过自蒸馏提升多视角重建模型性能，无需标注数据。



摘要： 本文提出 SelfEvo，一个无需外部标注数据即可持续提升多视角重建模型的自改进框架。该方法利用时空上下文不对称性进行自蒸馏，有效提升了4D感知能力。研究系统分析了影响自改进效果的设计选择，包括损失信号、不对称形式和训练策略。在八个跨不同数据集和领域的基准测试中，SelfEvo均表现出色，尤其在动态场景中取得显著提升，视频深度估计和相机估计分别提升36.5%和20.1%。

item.title: Kathleen：基于频域处理的文本分类架构
item.content:encoded:
Kathleen通过频域处理实现高效文本分类，无需分词器和注意力机制。



摘要： Kathleen是一种新型文本分类架构，直接在原始UTF-8字节上进行频域处理，无需分词器或注意力机制，仅需733K参数。其创新包括RecurrentOscillatorBanks、FFT-Rotate Wavetable Encoder和PhaseHarmonics三个组件。实验表明，Kathleen在IMDB、AG News和SST-2数据集上的准确率分别为88.6%、92.3%和83.3%，优于具有16倍参数量的分词模型。Kathleen在时间复杂度和内存使用上优于传统Transformer模型，适用于长序列任务。

item.title: 基于坐标约束的自回归动作生成框架CDAMD
item.content:encoded:
提出CDAMD框架提升文本到动作生成的精度与语义一致性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Coordinate-based Dual-constrained Autoregressive Motion Generation (CDAMD) 的文本到动作生成框架，旨在解决扩散模型和自回归模型在运动生成中的不足。该框架采用运动坐标作为输入，结合自回归机制与扩散启发的多层感知机，提高生成动作的保真度。同时引入双约束因果掩码，通过将动作标记与文本编码拼接，引导生成过程。为验证效果，研究者建立了新的基准测试，实验结果表明CDAMD在生成质量和语义一致性方面均达到领先水平。

item.title: 开源网页代理MolmoWeb提升网络任务自动化能力
item.content:encoded:
MolmoWeb是首个完全开源的网页代理系统，性能优于同类模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了MolmoWeb，一个完全开源的多模态网页代理系统。该系统基于MolmoWebMix数据集，包含10万条合成任务轨迹和3万多人类示范数据，支持无需HTML或API的网页操作。MolmoWeb在多个基准测试中表现优异，超越了类似规模的开源模型，并在测试时通过并行推理实现了更高的成功率。研究团队还提供了模型、数据和评估工具，以促进开放研究。

item.title: 基于稀疏切片的三维组织分析方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究二维与三维采样对空间统计稳定性的影响，提出几何感知重建方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了二维与三维组织采样对空间统计稳定性的影响，指出平面采样虽能可靠恢复细胞类型丰度，但在局部统计如细胞聚类和相互作用方面存在高方差。针对这一问题，作者提出一种几何感知的重建模块，通过表型和距离约束连接相邻切片中的细胞投影，并利用细胞类型特异性形状先验恢复单细胞三维坐标。研究还分析了切片间距、覆盖范围和冗余之间的权衡，验证了该方法在真实数据集上的有效性，为二维与三维分析的选择提供了实用指导。

item.title: PSI：共享状态架构实现个人AI工具的协同整合
item.content:encoded:
PSI通过共享状态实现个人AI工具的集成与协作。



摘要： 文章介绍了PSI，一种共享状态架构，能够将独立生成的个人AI模块转化为连贯、可访问的工具。通过发布当前状态和写回功能到共享的个人上下文总线，PSI使模块之间能够进行跨模块推理和同步操作。研究通过三周的自我开发个人AI环境部署验证了PSI的有效性，表明后期生成的工具可通过相同协议自动集成。PSI识别出共享状态是将孤立的AI软件转变为统一个人计算环境的关键系统层。

item.title: 基于流模型的多智能体强化学习方法VGM$^2$P
item.content:encoded:
VGM$^2$P提升多智能体强化学习效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为VGM$^2$P的新型多智能体强化学习框架，旨在解决传统方法在离线数据中学习最优联合策略时的效率和分布偏移问题。该方法利用全局优势值引导智能体协作，并通过条件行为克隆实现高效的行动生成。同时，采用无分类器引导的均流模型提升策略表达能力和推理效率。实验表明，即使仅通过条件行为克隆训练，VGM$^2$P也能达到与先进方法相当的性能。

item.title: LogAct：一种基于共享日志的智能体执行保障机制
item.content:encoded:
LogAct通过共享日志提升智能体执行的可靠性和可控制性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为LogAct的新抽象机制，用于提升智能体在生产环境中的执行可靠性。LogAct将每个智能体视为一个分解状态机，在共享日志中运行。智能体的操作在执行前可见，并可通过可插拔的投票机制提前停止。该机制支持在智能体或环境故障时进行一致恢复，并允许智能体利用大语言模型分析自身执行历史，从而实现语义层面的恢复、健康检查和优化。评估结果显示，LogAct智能体能高效正确地从故障中恢复，自我调试性能，优化令牌使用，并在基准测试中有效阻止不良行为，仅导致3%的良性效用下降。

item.title: 基于频域的时移与拉伸不变非负矩阵分解方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型非负矩阵分解框架，用于处理动态神经影像数据中的时间延迟和拉伸问题。



摘要： 本文针对动态神经影像数据中常见的扩散特性导致的时间延迟、尺度差异和拉伸效应，提出了一种时移与拉伸不变的非负矩阵分解方法。该方法在频域中实现，通过相位调整模拟时间偏移，并利用零填充或截断处理时间拉伸。模型使用PyTorch实现，实验表明其能够更精确地描述脑组织结构，适用于合成数据和脑部发射断层扫描数据。

item.title: CausalVAE提升潜在世界模型的因果推理能力
item.content:encoded:
CausalVAE增强潜在世界模型的因果推理与预测性能。



摘要： 本文引入CausalVAE作为潜在世界模型的结构模块，将其与多种编码器-转移骨干网络结合。实验表明，添加该模块后，在多个基准测试中保持了竞争性的事实预测能力，并提升了干预感知的反事实检索效果，表明其在分布偏移和干预下具有更强的鲁棒性。在物理基准测试中，CF-H@1指标显著提升，从11.0提高到41.0，增幅达272.7%。通过因果分析，发现学习到的结构依赖关系能够恢复有意义的一阶物理交互趋势，支持了潜在因果结构的可解释性。

item.title: 基于机器学习的金融交易欺诈检测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种机器学习欺诈检测框架，提升金融交易安全。



摘要： 随着数字支付系统的扩展，网络金融交易规模和复杂性增加，使欺诈活动更易发生。本文基于PaySim合成数据集，采用CRISP-DM方法构建欺诈检测框架，通过特征工程、模型比较（如逻辑回归、随机森林、XGBoost等）和SMOTE处理数据不平衡问题，结合超参数调优提高模型性能，为金融科技系统提供有效的欺诈预防解决方案。

item.title: 基于语义空间传感器调度的智能系统优化
item.content:encoded:
提出S3框架提升智能系统感知效率与可靠性。



摘要： 本文探讨了由大规模传感器网络驱动的智能系统从预定义监控向意图驱动操作的转变，指出存在语义到物理映射的缺口。尽管大语言模型在语义理解上表现优异，但现有感知流程为事后处理，缺乏主动感知决策。为此，作者提出了语义-空间传感器调度（S3）概念，并引入空间轨迹图（STG）作为神经符号范式，将开放规划转化为可验证的图优化问题。基于此，构建了IoT-Brain系统和TopoSense-Bench基准测试平台，实验证明该系统任务成功率提升37.6%，运行速度提高近2倍，同时减少6.6倍提示词使用量。实际部署中，系统显著提升可靠性并降低4.1倍网络带宽消耗，为大语言模型与物理世界的交互提供高效可靠框架。

item.title: 改进量化方法中的残差误差建模以提升大语言模型压缩性能
item.content:encoded:
改进量化方法中的残差误差建模，提升大语言模型压缩性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种改进的量化方法，通过重新定义残差误差的目标，使量化模型的输出更精确地对齐原始全精度模型的输出。研究发现，现有方法在层内校准过程中仅对齐量化输出与补偿权重的输出，而非真实输出，导致次优结果。为此，作者引入了‘补偿感知误差’，并结合神经元分解技术，有效将该误差融入权重更新过程。实验表明，该方法显著提升了GPTQ和GPTAQ的量化性能，并已开源。

item.title: DMax：一种高效的扩散语言模型解码方法
item.content:encoded:
DMax提升扩散语言模型的解码效率与质量。



摘要： 本文介绍了DMax，一种用于高效扩散语言模型（dLLMs）的新范式。DMax通过缓解并行解码中的误差累积问题，实现了更激进的并行解码，同时保持生成质量。与传统掩码dLLMs不同，DMax将解码过程重新定义为从掩码嵌入到词元嵌入的逐步自我优化。核心方法是On-Policy Uniform Training，该策略统一了掩码和均匀dLLMs的训练，使模型能够从掩码输入或自身错误预测中恢复干净词元。此外，DMax还引入了Soft Parallel Decoding，通过在嵌入空间中迭代自我修正，提升解码效果。实验表明，DMax在多个基准测试中表现优异，显著提升了TPF指标。

item.title: AtomEval：一种更可靠的对抗性陈述重写评估框架
item.content:encoded:
AtomEval通过分解陈述检测事实错误，提升对抗性重写的评估准确性。



摘要： 本文提出AtomEval，一个基于原子有效性评分的对抗性陈述重写评估框架。该框架将陈述分解为SROM结构，并通过原子有效性评分检测事实错误，超越了传统指标的表面相似性评估。实验表明，AtomEval在FEVER数据集上提供了更可靠的评估信号。研究还发现，更强的语言模型在有效性评估下未必生成更有效的对抗性陈述，揭示了现有对抗评估方法的局限性。

item.title: 基于CQD的国际象棋残局验证方法
item.content:encoded:
CQD方法验证国际象棋残局WDL标签的正确性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为Capture-Quiet Decomposition (CQD) 的结构定理，用于验证国际象棋残局的Win-Draw-Loss (WDL) 标签。该定理将所有合法局面分解为三类：终局、捕获和静止，并证明WDL标签正确的条件包括：终局标签正确、捕获局面与较小棋子数的已验证子模型一致，以及静止局面在同一体系内满足回溯一致性。CQD通过将捕获局面与外部验证的子模型关联，避免了自洽性带来的虚假解。作者对35个三子和四子残局、110个五子残局和372个六子残局进行了全面验证，结果与全回溯基线一致。

item.title: 基于EEG的三维视觉信息解码方法Brain3D
item.content:encoded:
提出Brain3D模型实现从EEG信号到三维重建的多模态解码。



摘要： 本文提出Brain3D，一种基于EEG-to-图像解码的多模态架构，用于将脑电波信号转换为三维表示。该方法通过生成式推理逐步将神经表征转换为三维域，首先从EEG信号生成视觉语义图像，再利用多模态大语言模型提取结构化的三维感知描述，最终通过扩散模型生成三维网格。实验表明，该方法在语义对齐和几何保真度方面表现优异，达到85.4%的Top-1解码准确率和0.648的CLIPScore，验证了多模态EEG驱动三维重建的可行性。

item.title: 基于图像对的高效视频编辑框架ImVideoEdit
item.content:encoded:
ImVideoEdit通过图像对实现高效视频编辑，无需大量视频数据。



摘要： 当前视频编辑模型通常依赖昂贵的成对视频数据，限制了其实际应用。本文提出ImVideoEdit，一种完全基于图像对的高效视频编辑框架。该方法通过冻结预训练的3D注意力模块，并将图像视为单帧视频，实现了时空解耦。核心是Predict-Update Spatial Difference Attention模块，用于逐步提取和注入空间差异。此外，引入Text-Guided Dynamic Semantic Gating机制，实现自适应文本驱动的修改。尽管仅使用13K张图像对进行5次训练，ImVideoEdit在编辑保真度和时间一致性方面表现优异，计算成本极低。



使用 qwen-turbo 生成

查看原文

item.title: 基于论文自身验证的科学质量保证新方案
item.content:encoded:
提出通过分析论文本身进行质量评估的新方法。



摘要： 文章指出当前科学质量保证体系存在缺陷，传统同行评审和开放科学均无法有效确保论文质量。作者提出一种新方案——直接对论文本身进行验证，开发了名为 sciwrite-lint 的开源工具，可在本地运行，无需上传论文。该工具检查引用文献的真实性、重印状态及与论文内容的一致性，并为每篇参考文献生成可靠性评分。此外，还引入 SciLint Score，结合科学贡献评估框架，进一步提升质量评估能力。

查看原文

item.title: 基于合成轨迹生成的本地化网页代理框架
item.content:encoded:
通过模拟人类标注角色生成网页轨迹，实现高效本地部署的网页代理模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Agent-as-Annotators的框架，通过模仿人类标注角色来结构化生成网页代理的合成轨迹。该框架利用Gemini 3 Pro作为教师模型，生成3000条轨迹，并通过监督学习微调一个9B参数的学生模型。在WebArena测试中，该模型达到41.5%的准确率，优于多个闭源模型。此外，模型在未见过的环境和多个基准测试中也表现出色。实验表明，每个模块组件都对性能提升有显著贡献。该研究证明，仅需一个前沿教师模型即可生成具有竞争力的本地部署网页代理。

item.title: 面向多模态深度伪造的通用检测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MAF框架提升多模态深度伪造检测泛化能力。



摘要： 随着生成式人工智能的发展，深度伪造攻击已从单一模态演变为复杂的多模态威胁。现有检测技术依赖于特定模态的表面特征，导致在面对未知模态时性能严重下降。本文提出一种新的多模态取证范式——模态无关伪造检测（MAF），通过解耦模态特有风格，提取跨模态的伪造知识。同时定义了两种泛化维度：对语义相关模态的迁移能力（弱MAF）和对孤立信号的鲁棒性（强MAF）。为验证该方法，构建了DeepModal-Bench基准测试平台，集成多种多模态检测算法并应用先进泛化学习方法。实验表明，MAF框架在未知模态上取得显著突破，为多模态防御提供了新路径。

item.title: 基于3D先验的开放世界机器人操作方法LAMP
item.content:encoded:
LAMP通过3D先验提升机器人操作的泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为LAMP的方法，用于解决开放世界中机器人操作的泛化问题。现有基于学习的方法在面对新任务和未知环境时表现不佳，而大语言模型和视觉语言模型由于缺乏3D感知能力，也难以进行精细操作。LAMP通过将图像编辑作为3D先验，提取物体间的3D变换，形成几何感知的连续表示，从而提供更精确的操作指导。实验表明，该方法在开放世界操作任务中表现出色，具备强大的零样本泛化能力。

item.title: 基于文本引导的视频行人重识别方法CG-CLIP
item.content:encoded:
提出CG-CLIP框架提升视频行人重识别性能。



摘要： 本文针对视频行人重识别（ReID）在高难度场景下的挑战，如运动中的多人穿着相似服装，提出CG-CLIP框架。该方法结合文本描述和可学习标记，包含Caption-guided Memory Refinement（CMR）和Token-based Feature Extraction（TFE）两个关键组件。CMR通过多模态大语言模型生成的描述优化身份特征，TFE则利用交叉注意力机制高效聚合时空特征。实验在多个数据集上验证了该方法的有效性，结果表明其优于现有方法。
