item.title: Feature Weighting Improves Pool-Based Sequential Active Learning for Regression
item.content:encoded: Pool-based sequential active learning for regression (ALR) optimally selects a small number of samples sequentially from a large pool of unlabeled samples to label, so that a more accurate regression model can be constructed under a given labeling budget. Representativeness and diversity, which involve computing the distances among different samples, are important considerations in ALR. However, previous ALR approaches do not incorporate the importance of different features in inter-sample distance computation, resulting in sub-optimal sample selection. This paper proposes three feature weighted single-task ALR approaches and two feature weighted multi-task ALR approaches, where the ridge regression coefficients trained from a small amount of previously labeled samples are used to weight the corresponding features in inter-sample distance computation. Experiments showed that this easy-to-implement enhancement almost always improves the performance of four existing ALR approaches, in both single-task and multi-task regression problems. The feature weighting strategy may also be easily extended to stream-based ALR, and classification algorithms.

item.title: EventHub: Data Factory for Generalizable Event-Based Stereo Networks without Active Sensors
item.content:encoded: We propose EventHub, a novel framework for training deep-event stereo networks without ground truth annotations from costly active sensors, relying instead on standard color images. From these images, we derive either proxy annotations and proxy events through state-of-the-art novel view synthesis techniques, or simply proxy annotations when images are already paired with event data. Using the training set generated by our data factory, we repurpose state-of-the-art stereo models from RGB literature to process event data, obtaining new event stereo models with unprecedented generalization capabilities. Experiments on widely used event stereo datasets support the effectiveness of EventHub and show how the same data distillation mechanism can improve the accuracy of RGB stereo foundation models in challenging conditions such as nighttime scenes.

item.title: Towards Minimal Focal Stack in Shape from Focus
item.content:encoded: Shape from Focus (SFF) is a depth reconstruction technique that estimates scene structure from focus variations observed across a focal stack, that is, a sequence of images captured at different focus settings. A key limitation of SFF methods is their reliance on densely sampled, large focal stacks, which limits their practical applicability. In this study, we propose a focal stack augmentation that enables SFF methods to estimate depth using a reduced stack of just two images, without sacrificing precision. We introduce a simple yet effective physics-based focal stack augmentation that enriches the stack with two auxiliary cues: an all-in-focus (AiF) image estimated from two input images, and Energy-of-Difference (EOD) maps, computed as the energy of differences between the AiF and input images. Furthermore, we propose a deep network that computes a deep focus volume from the augmented focal stacks and iteratively refines depth using convolutional Gated Recurrent Units (ConvGRUs) at multiple scales. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed augmentation benefits existing state-of-the-art SFF models, enabling them to achieve comparable accuracy. The results also show that our approach maintains state-of-the-art performance with a minimal stack size.

item.title: Attention at Rest Stays at Rest: Breaking Visual Inertia for Cognitive Hallucination Mitigation
item.content:encoded: Like a body at rest that stays at rest, we find that visual attention in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) exhibits pronounced inertia, remaining largely static once settled during early decoding steps and failing to support the compositional understanding required for cognitive inference. While existing hallucination mitigation methods mainly target perceptual hallucinations concerning object existence or attributes, they remain inadequate for such cognitive hallucinations that require inter-object relational deduction. Through token-wise attention analysis, we identify this visual inertia as a key factor: attention to semantically critical regions remains persistently focused and fails to dynamically support relational inference. We thereby propose a training-free Inertia-aware Visual Excitation (IVE) method that breaks this inertial pattern by modeling cognitive inference as the dynamic responsiveness of visual attention. Specifically, IVE selects visual tokens that are dynamically emerging relative to historical attention trends while distinguishing tokens exhibiting inertial behavior. To further facilitate compositional inference, IVE introduces an inertia-aware penalty that discourages over-concentration and limits the persistence of attention within localized regions. Extensive experiments show that IVE is effective across various base MLLMs and multiple hallucination benchmarks, particularly for cognitive hallucinations.

item.title: ImplicitBBQ: Benchmarking Implicit Bias in Large Language Models through Characteristic Based Cues
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models increasingly suppress biased outputs when demographic identity is stated explicitly, yet may still exhibit implicit biases when identity is conveyed indirectly. Existing benchmarks use name based proxies to detect implicit biases, which carry weak associations with many social demographics and cannot extend to dimensions like age or socioeconomic status. We introduce ImplicitBBQ, a QA benchmark that evaluates implicit bias through characteristic based cues, culturally associated attributes that signal implicitly, across age, gender, region, religion, caste, and socioeconomic status. Evaluating 11 models, we find that implicit bias in ambiguous contexts is over six times higher than explicit bias in open weight models. Safety prompting and chain-of-thought reasoning fail to substantially close this gap; even few-shot prompting, which reduces implicit bias by 84%, leaves caste bias at four times the level of any other dimension. These findings indicate that current alignment and prompting strategies address the surface of bias evaluation while leaving culturally grounded stereotypic associations largely unresolved. We publicly release our code and dataset for model providers and researchers to benchmark potential mitigation techniques.

item.title: Ouroboros: Dynamic Weight Generation for Recursive Transformers via Input-Conditioned LoRA Modulation
item.content:encoded: Recursive transformers reuse a shared weight block across multiple depth steps, trading parameters for compute. A core limitation: every step applies the same transformation, preventing the model from composing distinct operations across depth. We present Ouroboros, a system that attaches a compact Controller hypernetwork to a recursive transformer block. The Controller observes the current hidden state, produces a per-step diagonal modulation vector, and applies it to frozen SVD-initialized LoRA bases, making each recurrence step input-dependent. We combine this with gated recurrence (bias-initialized to 88% retention) and per-step LayerNorm for stable deep iteration. On Qwen2.5-3B split into a Prelude/Recurrent/Coda architecture (17 of 36 layers retained), Ouroboros reduces training loss by 43.4% over the unmodified 17-layer baseline, recovering 51.3% of the performance gap caused by layer removal. The full system adds only 9.2M trainable parameters (Controller, gate, and per-step norms) yet outperforms equivalently-sized static per-step LoRA by 1.44 loss points at depth 1 and remains ahead across all tested depths (1, 4, 8, 16) and ranks (8, 32, 64). We also find that gated recurrence is essential: without it, recursive layer application makes the model strictly worse. These gains are measured on the training distribution; on held-out text, the Controller does not yet improve over the baseline, a limitation we attribute to frozen downstream layers and discuss in detail. Code: https://github.com/RightNow-AI/ouroboros

item.title: What Do Claim Verification Datasets Actually Test? A Reasoning Trace Analysis
item.content:encoded: Despite rapid progress in claim verification, we lack a systematic understanding of what reasoning these benchmarks actually exercise. We generate structured reasoning traces for 24K claim-verification examples across 9 datasets using GPT-4o-mini and find that direct evidence extraction dominates, while multi-sentence synthesis and numerical reasoning are severely under-represented. A dataset-level breakdown reveals stark biases: some datasets almost exclusively test lexical matching, while others require information synthesis in roughly half of cases. Using a compact 1B-parameter reasoning verifier, we further characterize five error types and show that error profiles vary dramatically by domain -- general-domain verification is dominated by lexical overlap bias, scientific verification by overcautiousness, and mathematical verification by arithmetic reasoning failures. Our findings suggest that high benchmark scores primarily reflect retrieval-plus-entailment ability. We outline recommendations for building more challenging evaluation suites that better test the reasoning capabilities verification systems need.

item.title: Causal Scene Narration with Runtime Safety Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Driving
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for autonomous driving must integrate diverse textual inputs, including navigation commands, hazard warnings, and traffic state descriptions, yet current systems often present these as disconnected fragments, forcing the model to discover on its own which environmental constraints are relevant to the current maneuver. We introduce Causal Scene Narration (CSN), which restructures VLA text inputs through intent-constraint alignment, quantitative grounding, and structured separation, at inference time with zero GPU cost. We complement CSN with Simplex-based runtime safety supervision and training-time alignment via Plackett-Luce DPO with negative log-likelihood (NLL) regularization. A multi-town closed-loop CARLA evaluation shows that CSN improves Driving Score by +31.1% on original LMDrive and +24.5% on the preference-aligned variant. A controlled ablation reveals that causal structure accounts for 39.1% of this gain, with the remainder attributable to information content alone. A perception noise ablation confirms that CSN's benefit is robust to realistic sensing errors. Semantic safety supervision improves Infraction Score, while reactive Time-To-Collision monitoring degrades performance, demonstrating that intent-aware monitoring is needed for VLA systems.

item.title: Interactive Tracking: A Human-in-the-Loop Paradigm with Memory-Augmented Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Existing visual trackers mainly operate in a non-interactive, fire-and-forget manner, making them impractical for real-world scenarios that require human-in-the-loop adaptation. To overcome this limitation, we introduce Interactive Tracking, a new paradigm that allows users to guide the tracker at any time using natural language commands. To support research in this direction, we make three main contributions. First, we present InteractTrack, the first large-scale benchmark for interactive tracking, containing 150 videos with dense bounding box annotations and timestamped language instructions. Second, we propose a comprehensive evaluation protocol and evaluate 25 representative trackers, showing that state-of-the-art methods fail in interactive scenarios; strong performance on conventional benchmarks does not transfer. Third, we introduce Interactive Memory-Augmented Tracking (IMAT), a new baseline that employs a dynamic memory mechanism to learn from user feedback and update tracking behavior accordingly. Our benchmark, protocol, and baseline establish a foundation for developing more intelligent, adaptive, and collaborative tracking systems, bridging the gap between automated perception and human guidance. The full benchmark, tracking results, and analysis are available at https://github.com/NorahGreen/InteractTrack.git.

item.title: Demographic Parity Tails for Regression
item.content:encoded: Demographic parity (DP) is a widely studied fairness criterion in regression, enforcing independence between the predictions and sensitive attributes. However, constraining the entire distribution can degrade predictive accuracy and may be unnecessary for many applications, where fairness concerns are localized to specific regions of the distribution. To overcome this issue, we propose a new framework for regression under DP that focuses on the tails of target distribution across sensitive groups. Our methodology builds on optimal transport theory. By enforcing fairness constraints only over targeted regions of the distribution, our approach enables more nuanced and context-sensitive interventions. Leveraging recent advances, we develop an interpretable and flexible algorithm that leverages the geometric structure of optimal transport. We provide theoretical guarantees, including risk bounds and fairness properties, and validate the method through experiments in regression settings.

item.title: HOT: Harmonic-Constrained Optimal Transport for Remote Photoplethysmography Domain Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) enables non-contact physiological measurement from facial videos; however, its practical deployment is often hindered by substantial performance degradation under domain shift. While recent deep learning-based rPPG methods have achieved strong performance on individual datasets, they frequently overfit to appearance-related factors, such as illumination, camera characteristics, and color response, that vary significantly across domains. To address this limitation, we introduce frequency domain adaptation (FDA) as a principled strategy for modeling appearance variation in rPPG. By transferring low-frequency spectral components that encode domain-dependent appearance characteristics, FDA encourages rPPG models to learn invariance to appearance variations while retaining cardiac-induced signals. To further support physiologically consistent alignment under such appearance variation, we propose Harmonic-Constrained Optimal Transport (HOT), which leverages the harmonic property of cardiac signals to guide alignment between original and FDA-transferred representations. Extensive cross-dataset experiments demonstrate that the proposed FDA and HOT framework effectively enhances the robustness and generalization of rPPG models across diverse datasets.

item.title: Ultrasound-CLIP: Semantic-Aware Contrastive Pre-training for Ultrasound Image-Text Understanding
item.content:encoded: Ultrasound imaging is widely used in clinical diagnostics due to its real-time capability and radiation-free nature. However, existing vision-language pre-training models, such as CLIP, are primarily designed for other modalities, and are difficult to directly apply to ultrasound data, which exhibit heterogeneous anatomical structures and diverse diagnostic attributes. To bridge this gap, we construct US-365K, a large-scale ultrasound image-text dataset containing 365k paired samples across 52 anatomical categories. We establish Ultrasonographic Diagnostic Taxonomy (UDT) containing two hierarchical knowledge frameworks. Ultrasonographic Hierarchical Anatomical Taxonomy standardizes anatomical organization, and Ultrasonographic Diagnostic Attribute Framework formalizes nine diagnostic dimensions, including body system, organ, diagnosis, shape, margins, echogenicity, internal characteristics, posterior acoustic phenomena, and vascularity. Building upon these foundations, we propose Ultrasound-CLIP, a semantic-aware contrastive learning framework that introduces semantic soft labels and semantic loss to refine sample discrimination. Moreover, we construct a heterogeneous graph modality derived from UDAF's textual representations, enabling structured reasoning over lesion-attribute relations. Extensive experiments with patient-level data splitting demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on classification and retrieval benchmarks, while also delivering strong generalization to zero-shot, linear probing, and fine-tuning tasks.

item.title: The Self Driving Portfolio: Agentic Architecture for Institutional Asset Management
item.content:encoded: Agentic AI shifts the investor's role from analytical execution to oversight. We present an agentic strategic asset allocation pipeline in which approximately 50 specialized agents produce capital market assumptions, construct portfolios using over 20 competing methods, and critique and vote on each other's output. A researcher agent proposes new portfolio construction methods not yet represented, and a meta-agent compares past forecasts against realized returns and rewrites agent code and prompts to improve future performance. The entire pipeline is governed by the Investment Policy Statement--the same document that guides human portfolio managers can now constrain and direct autonomous agents.

item.title: Light-ResKAN: A Parameter-Sharing Lightweight KAN with Gram Polynomials for Efficient SAR Image Recognition
item.content:encoded: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) image recognition is vital for disaster monitoring, military reconnaissance, and ocean observation. However, large SAR image sizes hinder deep learning deployment on resource-constrained edge devices, and existing lightweight models struggle to balance high-precision feature extraction with low computational requirements. The emerging Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) enhances fitting by replacing fixed activations with learnable ones, reducing parameters and computation. Inspired by KAN, we propose Light-ResKAN to achieve a better balance between precision and efficiency. First, Light-ResKAN modifies ResNet by replacing convolutions with KAN convolutions, enabling adaptive feature extraction for SAR images. Second, we use Gram Polynomials as activations, which are well-suited for SAR data to capture complex non-linear relationships. Third, we employ a parameter-sharing strategy: each kernel shares parameters per channel, preserving unique features while reducing parameters and FLOPs. Our model achieves 99.09%, 93.01%, and 97.26% accuracy on MSTAR, FUSAR-Ship, and SAR-ACD datasets, respectively. Experiments on MSTAR resized to $1024 \times 1024$ show that compared to VGG16, our model reduces FLOPs by $82.90 \times$ and parameters by $163.78 \times$. This work establishes an efficient solution for edge SAR image recognition.

item.title: MM-ReCoder: Advancing Chart-to-Code Generation with Reinforcement Learning and Self-Correction
item.content:encoded: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have recently demonstrated promising capabilities in multimodal coding tasks such as chart-to-code generation. However, existing methods primarily rely on supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which requires the model to learn code patterns through chart-code pairs but does not expose the model to a code execution environment. Moreover, while self-correction through execution feedback offers a potential route to improve coding quality, even state-of-the-art MLLMs have been shown to struggle with effective self-correction. In this work, we introduce MM-ReCoder, a chart-to-code generation model trained with reinforcement learning (RL) and equipped with self-correction ability. We propose a two-stage multi-turn self-correction RL strategy based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). The first stage enhances the model's self-correction ability via rolling out a shared first turn, while the second stage improves the coding capability with full-trajectory optimization. MM-ReCoder learns to produce more accurate and executable code through the interaction with the environment and by iteratively correcting its own outputs. Our results on three chart-to-code benchmarks demonstrate the state-of-the-art performance of MM-ReCoder.

item.title: World Action Verifier: Self-Improving World Models via Forward-Inverse Asymmetry
item.content:encoded: General-purpose world models promise scalable policy evaluation, optimization, and planning, yet achieving the required level of robustness remains challenging. Unlike policy learning, which primarily focuses on optimal actions, a world model must be reliable over a much broader range of suboptimal actions, which are often insufficiently covered by action-labeled interaction data. To address this challenge, we propose World Action Verifier (WAV), a framework that enables world models to identify their own prediction errors and self-improve. The key idea is to decompose action-conditioned state prediction into two factors -- state plausibility and action reachability -- and verify each separately. We show that these verification problems can be substantially easier than predicting future states due to two underlying asymmetries: the broader availability of action-free data and the lower dimensionality of action-relevant features. Leveraging these asymmetries, we augment a world model with (i) a diverse subgoal generator obtained from video corpora and (ii) a sparse inverse model that infers actions from a subset of state features. By enforcing cycle consistency among generated subgoals, inferred actions, and forward rollouts, WAV provides an effective verification mechanism in under-explored regimes, where existing methods typically fail. Across nine tasks spanning MiniGrid, RoboMimic, and ManiSkill, our method achieves 2x higher sample efficiency while improving downstream policy performance by 18%.

item.title: From High-Dimensional Spaces to Verifiable ODD Coverage for Safety-Critical AI-based Systems
item.content:encoded: While Artificial Intelligence (AI) offers transformative potential for operational performance, its deployment in safety-critical domains such as aviation requires strict adherence to rigorous certification standards. Current EASA guidelines mandate demonstrating complete coverage of the AI/ML constituent's Operational Design Domain (ODD) -- a requirement that demands proof that no critical gaps exist within defined operational boundaries. However, as systems operate within high-dimensional parameter spaces, existing methods struggle to provide the scalability and formal grounding necessary to satisfy the completeness criterion. Currently, no standardized engineering method exists to bridge the gap between abstract ODD definitions and verifiable evidence. This paper addresses this void by proposing a method that integrates parameter discretization, constraint-based filtering, and criticality-based dimension reduction into a structured, multi-step ODD coverage verification process. Grounded in gathered simulation data from prior research on AI-based mid-air collision avoidance research, this work demonstrates a systematic engineering approach to defining and achieving coverage metrics that satisfy EASA's demand for completeness. Ultimately, this method enables the validation of ODD coverage in higher dimensions, advancing a Safety-by-Design approach while complying with EASA's standards.

item.title: Large-scale Codec Avatars: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Large-scale Avatar Pretraining
item.content:encoded: High-quality 3D avatar modeling faces a critical trade-off between fidelity and generalization. On the one hand, multi-view studio data enables high-fidelity modeling of humans with precise control over expressions and poses, but it struggles to generalize to real-world data due to limited scale and the domain gap between the studio environment and the real world. On the other hand, recent large-scale avatar models trained on millions of in-the-wild samples show promise for generalization across a wide range of identities, yet the resulting avatars are often of low-quality due to inherent 3D ambiguities. To address this, we present Large-Scale Codec Avatars (LCA), a high-fidelity, full-body 3D avatar model that generalizes to world-scale populations in a feedforward manner, enabling efficient inference. Inspired by the success of large language models and vision foundation models, we present, for the first time, a pre/post-training paradigm for 3D avatar modeling at scale: we pretrain on 1M in-the-wild videos to learn broad priors over appearance and geometry, then post-train on high-quality curated data to enhance expressivity and fidelity. LCA generalizes across hair styles, clothing, and demographics while providing precise, fine-grained facial expressions and finger-level articulation control, with strong identity preservation. Notably, we observe emergent generalization to relightability and loose garment support to unconstrained inputs, and zero-shot robustness to stylized imagery, despite the absence of direct supervision.

item.title: GeoAI Agency Primitives
item.content:encoded: We present ongoing research on agency primitives for GeoAI assistants -- core capabilities that connect Foundation models to the artifact-centric, human-in-the-loop workflows where GIS practitioners actually work. Despite advances in satellite image captioning, visual question answering, and promptable segmentation, these capabilities have not translated into productivity gains for practitioners who spend most of their time producing vector layers, raster maps, and cartographic products. The gap is not model capability alone but the absence of an agency layer that supports iterative collaboration. We propose a vocabulary of $9$ primitives for such a layer -- including navigation, perception, geo-referenced memory, and dual modeling -- along with a benchmark that measures human productivity. Our goal is a vocabulary that makes agentic assistance in GIS implementable, testable, and comparable.

item.title: Matching Accuracy, Different Geometry: Evolution Strategies vs GRPO in LLM Post-Training
item.content:encoded: Evolution Strategies (ES) have emerged as a scalable gradient-free alternative to reinforcement learning based LLM fine-tuning, but it remains unclear whether comparable task performance implies comparable solutions in parameter space. We compare ES and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) across four tasks in both single-task and sequential continual-learning settings. ES matches or exceeds GRPO in single-task accuracy and remains competitive sequentially when its iteration budget is controlled. Despite this similarity in task performance, the two methods produce markedly different model updates: ES makes much larger changes and induces broader off-task KL drift, whereas GRPO makes smaller, more localized updates. Strikingly, the ES and GRPO solutions are linearly connected with no loss barrier, even though their update directions are nearly orthogonal. We develop an analytical theory of ES that explains all these phenomena within a unified framework, showing how ES can accumulate large off-task movement on weakly informative directions while still making enough progress on the task to match gradient-based RL in downstream accuracy. These results show that gradient-free and gradient-based fine-tuning can reach similarly accurate yet geometrically distinct solutions, with important consequences for forgetting and knowledge preservation. The source code is publicly available: https://github.com/Bhoy1/ESvsGRPO.

item.title: 基于V-NSDE模型的奥迪沙州社会经济数据建模研究
item.content:encoded:
V-NSDE模型有效捕捉社会经济数据中的复杂模式。



摘要： 本研究探讨了在时间维度上对复杂且噪声较大的社会经济数据进行建模的挑战，特别是针对印度奥迪沙州不同地区的数据。传统时间序列模型难以同时捕捉趋势和变化。为此，研究提出了一种结合神经随机微分方程（Neural SDEs）与变分自编码器（VAEs）的V-NSDE模型。该模型通过编码器将初始观测值和区域嵌入转换为高斯分布，再通过神经SDE生成连续时间潜变量动态，并利用解码器重构观测值。通过引入KL散度正则化项优化证据下界（ELBO）损失函数，模型能够有效学习复杂的时间模式，生成包含明确趋势和随机波动的现实结果。

item.title: 开放集监督的3D异常检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究开放集监督的3D异常检测方法，提升工业场景中的检测效果。



摘要： 本文针对3D异常检测问题，提出一种基于少量已知异常样本和大量正常样本的开放集监督方法。通过构建高质量的工业数据集Open-Industry，并改进通用异常检测方法以适应点云输入。进一步提出Open3D-AD方法，利用正常样本、模拟异常和部分真实异常来建模正常与异常数据的概率分布。同时引入对应分布子采样技术，减少正常与非正常分布之间的重叠，增强双分布建模能力。实验在Open-Industry、Real3D-AD和Anomaly-ShapeNet等数据集上验证了该方法的有效性。

item.title: 基于STEM的人机协作纳米分析流程框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种可调的人机协作STEM分析框架，实现半导体材料的精准表征。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种可调的人机协作工作流程框架，用于扫描透射电子显微镜（STEM）图像的分析，以实现对多层薄膜结构的自动化层厚和界面粗糙度量化。该系统结合梯度峰值检测与交互式修正模块，在保持自动化执行的同时允许人工干预，适用于不同样品的分析需求。通过网页界面直接处理TEM/EMD文件，应用降噪和界面追踪算法，输出纳米级精度的统计结果。该架构展示了适应性强、可重复使用的计量工作流程方法，融合了人类洞察力与机器精确性，为半导体制造中的标准化分析提供了新思路。

item.title: AI在皮肤科诊断中的局限性及人类专家表现分析
item.content:encoded:
AI在皮肤图像诊断中存在系统性错误，人类专家也难以准确识别。



摘要： 本研究探讨了卷积神经网络（CNN）在皮肤镜图像诊断中的表现，发现部分图像被所有模型错误分类，且这些图像的视觉特征具有高度模糊性。通过对比专家医生的诊断结果，发现他们在这些困难图像上的判断一致性显著下降，表明AI的失败可能源于图像本身的复杂性而非算法偏差。研究强调了图像质量对AI和人类诊断的影响，并公开了所有数据和代码以提高透明度和可重复性。

item.title: CircuitProbe：高效定位Transformer模型中的推理电路
item.content:encoded:
CircuitProbe快速定位推理电路，提升模型推理效率。



摘要： 本文提出CircuitProbe，一种通过激活统计信息在CPU上快速预测Transformer模型中推理电路位置的方法。该方法相比传统方法提速数百倍，能有效识别早期层的稳定性电路和晚期层的幅度异常电路。实验验证覆盖9种模型，结果表明其预测准确率高，且适用于多种语言。此外，研究还发现模型参数量较小（3B以下）时复制层可提升性能，而大模型（7B以上）则可能下降，为小模型优化提供实用策略。

item.title: Activation Saturation and Floquet Spectrum Collapse in Neural ODEs
item.content:encoded: We prove that activation saturation imposes a structural dynamical limitation on autonomous Neural ODEs $\dot{h}=f_θ(h)$ with saturating activations ($\tanh$, sigmoid, etc.): if $q$ hidden layers of the MLP $f_θ$ satisfy $|σ'|\leδ$ on a region~$U$, the input Jacobian is attenuated as $\norm{Df_θ(x)}\le C(U)$ (for activations with $\sup_{x}|σ'(x)|\le 1$, e.g.\ $\tanh$ and sigmoid, this reduces to $C_Wδ^q$), forcing every Floquet (Lyapunov) exponen along any $T$-periodic orbit $γ\subset U$ into the interval $[-C(U),\;C(U)]$. This is a collapse of the Floquet spectrum: as saturation deepens ($δ\to 0$), all exponents are driven to zero, limiting both strong contraction and chaotic sensitivity. The obstruction is structural -- it constrains the learned vector field at inference time, independent of training quality. As a secondary contribution, for activations with $σ'>0$, a saturation-weighted spectral factorisation yields a refined bound $\widetilde{C}(U)\le C(U)$ whose improvement is amplified exponentially in~$T$ at the flow level. All results are numerically illustrated on the Stuart--Landau oscillator; the bounds provide a theoretical explanation for the empirically observed failure of $\tanh$-NODEs on the Morris--Lecar neuron model.

item.title: Lipschitz Dueling Bandits over Continuous Action Spaces
item.content:encoded: We study for the first time, stochastic dueling bandits over continuous action spaces with Lipschitz structure, where feedback is purely comparative. While dueling bandits and Lipschitz bandits have been studied separately, their combination has remained unexplored. We propose the first algorithm for Lipschitz dueling bandits, using round-based exploration and recursive region elimination guided by an adaptive reference arm. We develop new analytical tools for relative feedback and prove a regret bound of $\tilde O\left(T^{\frac{d_z+1}{d_z+2}}\right)$, where $d_z$ is the zooming dimension of the near-optimal region. Further, our algorithm takes only logarithmic space in terms of the total time horizon, best achievable by any bandit algorithm over a continuous action space.

item.title: 神经元激活中的词形混淆对概念压缩的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示词形混淆导致神经元激活重叠，影响概念压缩效果。



摘要： 该研究探讨了神经元在处理不同概念时的激活重叠现象，发现这种重叠可能并非源于概念压缩，而是由于共享词形（如“bank”）引起的词形混淆。通过2x2因子分解分析，研究显示词形相同但意义不同的情况比词义相同但词形不同的情况更常见。这一混淆现象在不同参数规模的模型中普遍存在，并影响了下游任务的表现，如词义消歧和知识编辑。去除该混淆后，任务性能得到提升。

item.title: 基于MIMIC-DOS数据集的医疗决策多阶段隐私保护框架研究
item.content:encoded:
CARE框架提升医疗决策中矛盾证据处理能力。



摘要： 本文针对医疗场景中患者主诉与体征不一致导致的决策难题，提出基于MIMIC-DOS数据集的CARE框架。该框架通过多阶段推理机制，在保护患者隐私的前提下，由远程大模型生成结构化分类和状态转移，辅助本地模型进行证据收集和最终决策。实验表明，CARE在多个关键指标上优于现有方法，能够更稳健地处理临床矛盾证据。

item.title: AI科学家新架构：BloClaw解决生命科学中的AI部署难题
item.content:encoded:
BloClaw提升AI在生命科学中的部署稳定性与交互能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了BloClaw，一个专为人工智能在生命科学（AI4S）中应用设计的统一多模态操作系统。该系统通过三项创新技术解决了当前AI研究环境中的关键问题，包括低效的JSON工具调用协议、执行沙箱中图形输出丢失以及不适应高维数据的对话界面。BloClaw采用XML-Regex双轨路由协议、运行时状态拦截沙箱和状态驱动动态视口用户界面，显著提升了AI研究助手的稳定性和自适应性，并在多个生命科学任务中进行了全面测试。

item.title: 3DGS与鱼眼相机融合提升三维场景重建质量
item.content:encoded:
鱼眼相机与3DGS结合，提升三维重建精度与细节表现。



摘要： 本文提出将鱼眼相机模型直接集成到3D Gaussian Splatting（3DGS）框架中，实现无需预处理的原生鱼眼图像输入。传统方法通过去畸变处理导致信息丢失和细节模糊，而新方法通过特征重叠驱动的跨视图联合优化策略，有效解决边缘浮点问题，提升重建质量。实验表明，DirectFisheye-GS在公开数据集上达到或超越当前最佳性能。

item.title: 基于街景图像与机器学习的区域建筑高程数据生成方法
item.content:encoded:
AI分析街景图像结合机器学习填补缺失数据，提升区域洪水风险评估精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种利用AI分析街景图像并结合机器学习填补缺失数据的方法，用于生成区域尺度的建筑高程数据，以支持洪水风险评估。研究在德克萨斯州18个区域应用了三阶段流程：第一阶段通过Elev-Vision框架从Google街景图像中提取楼面高程（LFE）和街道高程与最低楼层高度差（HDSL）；第二阶段使用随机森林和梯度提升模型，基于地形、水文、地理和洪水暴露特征填补缺失的HDSL数据；第三阶段将生成的高程数据与Fathom百年一遇淹没图和USACE深度-损失函数结合，估算每栋建筑内部洪水深度和预期损失。结果显示，该方法虽不能覆盖所有房产，但能显著提升区域洪水风险评估的准确性，为缺乏完整高程证书的地区提供可复制的解决方案。

item.title: 图神经网络与大语言模型在图问答中的融合研究
item.content:encoded:
融合GNN与LLM提升图问答性能，优化信息编码方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了将图神经网络（GNN）与大语言模型（LLM）结合用于图问答（GraphQA）的潜力。针对现有方法在结构信息编码上的局限性，提出通过多令牌池化和全局注意力机制来增强图到LLM的信息传输效率。实验对比了多种分层剪枝和聚类池化方法，并验证了LoRA在稳定特定投影中的有效性。结果表明，压缩表示可达到接近全图基线的性能。此外，研究还揭示了GraphQA基准数据集存在表征饱和问题，目标答案常与孤立节点特征高度相关。代码已开源。

item.title: KUTED数据集与中央库尔德语语音到文本翻译研究
item.content:encoded:
KUTED数据集提升中央库尔德语语音翻译性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了KUTED，一个用于中央库尔德语语音到文本翻译的大型数据集，来源于TED和TEDx演讲。该数据集包含91,000句对，涵盖170小时英语音频及对应的中央库尔德语文本。研究发现，拼写差异会显著影响翻译效果，为此提出了一种系统性的文本标准化方法，有效提升了翻译质量。实验表明，在独立测试集上，微调后的Seamless模型取得了15.18的BLEU分数，并在FLEURS基准上提升了3.0 BLEU。此外，还训练了一个Transformer模型并评估了Seamless（ASR）与NLLB（MT）结合的级联系统。

item.title: LLM生成工具库的软件质量评估研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM生成工具库需关注软件质量而非仅任务完成度。



摘要： 现代大型语言模型代理在运行时创建自己的工具，但现有基准主要评估任务完成情况，忽视了软件质量。本文提出EvolveTool-Bench，用于评估LLM生成工具库的软件质量，包括重用性、冗余性、组合成功率、回归稳定性和安全性。通过三个实际领域测试，发现任务完成率相近的系统在库健康度上存在显著差异，表明仅依赖任务评估存在风险。研究强调应将LLM生成的工具库视为第一类软件资产进行评估和治理。

item.title: ORCA：一种基于在线推理校准的高效语言模型优化方法
item.content:encoded:
ORCA提升语言模型效率与泛化能力，降低计算成本。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Online Reasoning Calibration (ORCA) 的框架，用于在测试阶段对语言模型进行采样校准，结合了符合性预测和测试时训练。ORCA通过元学习机制为每个输入更新校准模块，从而在分布偏移情况下提供有效的置信度估计。实验表明，在风险水平δ=0.1下，ORCA在Qwen2.5-32B模型上提升了47.5%的效率（使用监督标签）和40.7%（使用自洽标签）。在零样本跨域设置中，MATH-500任务的效率提升从24.8%提高到67%，同时保持低误差率。该方法在多个模型家族和下游基准上均表现出色。

item.title: 基于多智能体的住房咨询系统HabitatAgent研究
item.content:encoded:
HabitatAgent提升住房咨询准确率至95%



摘要： 本文提出HabitatAgent，一个基于大语言模型的多智能体架构，用于端到端的住房咨询。该系统包含记忆、检索、生成和验证四个专业代理，分别负责用户信息管理、混合向量-图检索、证据支持的推荐生成以及多层级验证。在100个真实用户咨询场景中，HabitatAgent的准确率达到了95%，显著高于单一阶段基线模型的75%。

item.title: 基于轻量探测器的大型语言模型训练中下游性能评估方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效评估LLM下游性能的方法，提升模型开发效率。



摘要： 本文针对大规模语言模型（LLM）在训练过程中下游任务性能评估成本过高的问题，提出了一种基于轻量探测器的评估方法。该方法利用模型检查点的内部表示，直接预测其在下游任务上的表现，避免了传统生成式评估的高昂计算成本。实验表明，该方法在多个任务上具有较高的预测准确性（平均AUROC>0.75），且具备良好的跨检查点泛化能力，将计算延迟从约1小时缩短至约3分钟，显著提升了LLM开发的效率和灵活性。

item.title: 并行执行范式提升代码生成效率
item.content:encoded:
通过并行执行减少代码生成与执行的延迟。



摘要： 当前基于大语言模型的代码生成代理采用串行执行方式，导致生成与执行阶段存在空闲，增加整体延迟。本文提出一种并行执行范式，将其建模为生成、检测和执行三阶段流水线，并推导出其速度提升和运行范围的理论边界。随后，作者实现了Eager系统，采用AST分块、动态批处理和早期错误中断等技术。在四个基准测试、七种大语言模型和三种执行环境中评估显示，Eager可减少高达99.9%的非重叠执行延迟和55%的端到端延迟。

item.title: AutoMIA：一种自动化的成员推理攻击框架
item.content:encoded:
AutoMIA通过自探索和策略进化提升成员推理攻击效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为AutoMIA的自动化成员推理攻击框架，旨在解决传统方法依赖静态启发式策略、适应性差的问题。AutoMIA通过生成可执行的日志级别策略并进行闭环评估反馈，实现攻击空间的自我探索与优化。该框架将抽象策略推理与低层执行解耦，支持模型无关的攻击搜索空间遍历。实验表明，AutoMIA在性能上优于现有最佳基线，同时无需人工特征工程。

item.title: 基于机器学习的天气预报超分辨率方法研究
item.content:encoded:
机器学习天气模型通过超分辨率技术提升预测精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种模块化框架，通过生成式超分辨率技术在后处理阶段提升天气预报的空间分辨率。该方法将预报与分辨率解耦，利用流匹配算法在再分析数据上训练模型，并应用于全球中程预报。评估结果显示，超分辨率方法在重新粗化后仍能保持大尺度结构和方差，引入物理一致的小尺度变化，在0.25°分辨率下达到与操作性集合预报相当的概率预测技能，且训练成本仅略有增加。

item.title: 从创始人职业数据预测初创公司成功性的模型研究
item.content:encoded:
基于创始人职业数据的初创公司成功预测模型研究。



摘要： 本文探讨了如何通过创始人职业数据预测初创公司的成功性，发现信号微弱且标签稀缺。研究者提取了28个结构化特征，并结合规则层和XGBoost模型，取得了优于零样本LLM基线的性能提升。进一步实验表明，从非结构化文本中提取的特征虽然占模型重要性的26.4%，但未带来显著的交叉验证改进，原因在于该文本是原始JSON数据的低质量重编码，而非更丰富的信息源。研究结果表明，数据集的信息量限制了模型上限，为未来数据增强提供了方向。

item.title: 基于潜在空间几何的神经网络标签预测方法
item.content:encoded:
利用潜在空间几何优化标签预测，提升计算效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经网络潜在空间几何特性的标签预测方法，通过将标签预测转化为向量系统中最近簇中心的搜索，从而将复杂度从O(n)降低到O(1)。该方法仅需查找嵌入向量中的最大值和最小值索引，计算效率高，且不影响模型训练精度。实验表明，该方法在多个数据集上可实现最高11.6倍的加速效果，并具备预测新类别的能力。

item.title: 基于图像级监督的人-物交互检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RegFormer模型提升人-物交互检测效率与准确性。



摘要： 弱监督下的人-物交互检测对场景理解至关重要，但传统方法依赖外部检测器生成候选对，计算成本高且易产生误检。为此，本文提出Relational Grounding Transformer (RegFormer)，在图像级监督下利用空间定位信号进行交互推理，提升局部感知能力，实现高效准确的实例级交互识别，无需额外训练即可直接迁移。实验表明，该方法在保持高效的同时，性能接近全监督模型。



使用 qwen-turbo 生成

查看原文

item.title: 大型语言模型训练中隐性数据损坏的影响与检测方法
item.content:encoded:
研究隐性数据损坏对LLM预训练的影响及检测方案。



摘要： 随着大型语言模型规模扩大，训练过程中出现的隐性数据损坏（SDC）问题愈发严重。本文通过在GPU矩阵乘法指令层面注入故障，研究不同位位置、核函数和执行阶段对SDC的敏感性。实验发现，局部故障可能导致NaN传播、损失波动、梯度异常和参数偏差等影响。基于这些特征，作者提出了一种轻量级检测方法，并在LLaMA模型上验证了重新计算最新训练步骤可有效缓解此类问题。

查看原文

item.title: 基于思维链的自动语音识别方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出CoT-ASR提升语音识别效果。



摘要： 本文针对传统自动语音识别（ASR）难以充分利用大语言模型（LLM）的知识和上下文理解能力的问题，提出了一种基于思维链的语音识别方法（CoT-ASR）。该方法通过构建推理链，使LLM先对输入语音进行分析并生成上下文解释，再进行更精准的语音识别，实现一次完成推理与转录。同时，CoT-ASR支持用户引导式转录，可结合用户提供的上下文信息以增强功能。为减少模态差异，研究引入了CTC引导的模态适配器，有效对齐语音编码输出与LLM的文本潜在空间。实验表明，CoT-ASR在词错误率（WER）和实体错误率（EER）上分别降低了8.7%和16.9%。

item.title: 基于视觉-几何-动作的自动驾驶新范式
item.content:encoded:
提出VGA范式，提升自动驾驶决策效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的视觉-几何-动作（VGA）自动驾驶范式，强调利用密集3D几何信息进行决策，以提升自动驾驶系统的性能。传统方法依赖稀疏感知和语言描述，而VGA范式通过在线处理输入并联合输出几何信息和轨迹规划，提高了实时性和效率。研究引入了流式驾驶视觉几何变换器（DVGT-2），结合时间因果注意力和历史特征缓存，实现高效的在线推理。该模型在多个数据集上表现优异，并可在不同摄像头配置下直接用于规划任务，无需微调。

item.title: 基于流的分布强化学习算法FP-DRL
item.content:encoded:
FP-DRL通过流匹配和分布强化学习提升多模态策略性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为FP-DRL的强化学习算法，旨在解决传统RL算法中策略参数化为对角高斯分布所导致的多模态分布建模不足问题。该算法采用流匹配技术建模策略，具备计算效率和复杂分布拟合能力，并结合分布强化学习优化整个回报分布，从而更有效地指导多模态策略更新，提升智能体性能。实验结果显示，FP-DRL在MuJoCo基准测试中表现优异，具有更强的流策略表征能力。

item.title: 基于主动共享感知的梯度对齐框架GRASP
item.content:encoded:
GRASP通过主动感知优化多智能体协作，提升系统收敛速度。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为GRASP（Gradient Realignment via Active Shared Perception）的新框架，用于解决多智能体系统中由于策略更新导致的非平稳性问题。传统方法如CTDE和顺序更新虽能缓解该问题，但因依赖环境交互数据进行其他智能体策略的感知，导致系统处于被动状态，引发平衡波动并降低收敛速度。GRASP通过独立梯度推导共识梯度，使智能体能够主动感知策略更新，从而优化团队协作。理论分析基于Kakutani不动点定理证明了均衡的可达性。实验在StarCraft II和Google Research Football平台上验证了该框架的可扩展性和性能优势。

item.title: MoonAnything：面向月球表面感知的统一基准数据集
item.content:encoded:
提出MoonAnything，首个提供几何与光度监督的月球数据集。



摘要： 本文介绍了MoonAnything，一个基于真实月球地形的统一基准数据集，通过物理渲染技术提供全面的几何和光度监督。该数据集包含两个互补子集：LunarGeo提供带有密集深度图的立体图像，支持三维重建和姿态估计；LunarPhoto则生成具有空间变化BRDF模型的逼真图像，并包含多种光照条件下的渲染结果，支持反射率估计和光照鲁棒感知。整体包含超过13万样本，适用于月球及其他无大气天体的感知研究。作者还提供了最先进的基线方法和完整数据集，以促进社区扩展。

item.title: Lightweight Prompt-Guided CLIP Adaptation for Monocular Depth Estimation
item.content:encoded: Leveraging the rich semantic features of vision-language models (VLMs) like CLIP for monocular depth estimation tasks is a promising direction, yet often requires extensive fine-tuning or lacks geometric precision. We present a parameter-efficient framework, named MoA-DepthCLIP, that adapts pretrained CLIP representations for monocular depth estimation with minimal supervision. Our method integrates a lightweight Mixture-of-Adapters (MoA) module into the pretrained Vision Transformer (ViT-B/32) backbone combined with selective fine-tuning of the final layers. This design enables spatially-aware adaptation, guided by a global semantic context vector and a hybrid prediction architecture that synergizes depth bin classification with direct regression. To enhance structural accuracy, we employ a composite loss function that enforces geometric constraints. On the NYU Depth V2 benchmark, MoA-DepthCLIP achieves competitive results, significantly outperforming the DepthCLIP baseline by improving the $δ_1$ accuracy from 0.390 to 0.745 and reducing the RMSE from 1.176 to 0.520. These results are achieved while requiring substantially few trainable parameters, demonstrating that lightweight, prompt-guided MoA is a highly effective strategy for transferring VLM knowledge to fine-grained monocular depth estimation tasks.

item.title: RefineRL: Advancing Competitive Programming with Self-Refinement Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: While large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance on complex reasoning tasks such as competitive programming (CP), existing methods predominantly focus on single-attempt settings, overlooking their capacity for iterative refinement. In this paper, we present RefineRL, a novel approach designed to unleash the self-refinement capabilities of LLMs for CP problem solving. RefineRL introduces two key innovations: (1) Skeptical-Agent, an iterative self-refinement agent equipped with local execution tools to validate generated solutions against public test cases of CP problems. This agent always maintains a skeptical attitude towards its own outputs and thereby enforces rigorous self-refinement even when validation suggests correctness. (2) A reinforcement learning (RL) solution to incentivize LLMs to self-refine with only standard RLVR data (i.e., problems paired with their verifiable answers). Extensive experiments on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-4B-2507 demonstrate that our method yields substantial gains: after our RL training, these compact 4B models integrated with the Skeptical-Agent not only outperform much larger 32B models but also approach the single-attempt performance of 235B models. These findings suggest that self-refinement holds considerable promise for scaling LLM reasoning, with significant potential for further advancement.

item.title: TF-SSD: A Strong Pipeline via Synergic Mask Filter for Training-free Co-salient Object Detection
item.content:encoded: Co-salient Object Detection (CoSOD) aims to segment salient objects that consistently appear across a group of related images. Despite the notable progress achieved by recent training-based approaches, they still remain constrained by the closed-set datasets and exhibit limited generalization. However, few studies explore the potential of Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) to address CoSOD, which demonstrate a strong generalized ability and robust saliency understanding. In this paper, we investigate and leverage VFMs for CoSOD, and further propose a novel training-free method, TF-SSD, through the synergy between SAM and DINO. Specifically, we first utilize SAM to generate comprehensive raw proposals, which serve as a candidate mask pool. Then, we introduce a quality mask generator to filter out redundant masks, thereby acquiring a refined mask set. Since this generator is built upon SAM, it inherently lacks semantic understanding of saliency. To this end, we adopt an intra-image saliency filter that employs DINO's attention maps to identify visually salient masks within individual images. Moreover, to extend saliency understanding across group images, we propose an inter-image prototype selector, which computes similarity scores among cross-image prototypes to select masks with the highest score. These selected masks serve as final predictions for CoSOD. Extensive experiments show that our TF-SSD outperforms existing methods (e.g., 13.7\% gains over the recent training-free method). Codes are available at https://github.com/hzz-yy/TF-SSD.

item.title: Rapid mixing in positively weighted restricted Boltzmann machines
item.content:encoded: We show polylogarithmic mixing time bounds for the alternating-scan sampler for positively weighted restricted Boltzmann machines. This is done via analysing the same chain and the Glauber dynamics for ferromagnetic two-spin systems, where we obtain new mixing time bounds up to the critical thresholds.

item.title: PHASOR: Anatomy- and Phase-Consistent Volumetric Diffusion for CT Virtual Contrast Enhancement
item.content:encoded: Contrast-enhanced computed tomography (CECT) is pivotal for highlighting tissue perfusion and vascularity, yet its clinical ubiquity is impeded by the invasive nature of contrast agents and radiation risks. While virtual contrast enhancement (VCE) offers an alternative to synthesizing CECT from non-contrast CT (NCCT), existing methods struggle with anatomical heterogeneity and spatial misalignment, leading to inconsistent enhancement patterns and incorrect details. This paper introduces PHASOR, a volumetric diffusion framework for high-fidelity CT VCE. By treating CT volumes as coherent sequences, we leverage a video diffusion model to enhance structural coherence and volumetric accuracy. To ensure anatomy-phase consistent synthesis, we introduce two complementary modules. First, anatomy-routed mixture-of-experts (AR-MoE) anchors distinct enhancement patterns to anatomical semantics, with organ-specific memory to capture salient details. Second, intensity-phase aware representation alignment (IP-REPA) highlights intricate contrast signals while mitigating the impact of imperfect spatial alignment. Extensive experiments across three datasets demonstrate that PHASOR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both synthesis quality and enhancement accuracy.

item.title: EmbedPart: Embedding-Driven Graph Partitioning for Scalable Graph Neural Network Training
item.content:encoded: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are widely used for learning on graph-structured data, but scaling GNN training to massive graphs remains challenging. To enable scalable distributed training, graphs are divided into smaller partitions that are distributed across multiple machines such that inter-machine communication is minimized and computational load is balanced. In practice, existing partitioning approaches face a fundamental trade-off between partitioning overhead and partitioning quality. We propose EmbedPart, an embedding-driven partitioning approach that achieves both speed and quality. Instead of operating directly on irregular graph structures, EmbedPart leverages node embeddings produced during the actual GNN training workload and clusters these dense embeddings to derive a partitioning. EmbedPart achieves more than 100x speedup over Metis while maintaining competitive partitioning quality and accelerating distributed GNN training. Moreover, EmbedPart naturally supports graph updates and fast repartitioning, and can be applied to graph reordering to improve data locality and accelerate single-machine GNN training. By shifting partitioning from irregular graph structures to dense embeddings, EmbedPart enables scalable and high-quality graph data optimization.

item.title: Predicting Dynamics of Ultra-Large Complex Systems by Inferring Governing Equations
item.content:encoded: Predicting the behavior of ultra-large complex systems, from climate to biological and technological networks, is a central unsolved challenge. Existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off: equation discovery methods provide interpretability but fail to scale, while neural networks scale but operate as black boxes and often lose reliability over long times. Here, we introduce the Sparse Identification Graph Neural Network, a framework that overcome this divide by allowing to infer the governing equations of large networked systems from data. By defining symbolic discovery as edge-level information, SIGN decouples the scalability of sparse identification from network size, enabling efficient equation discovery even in large systems. SIGN allows to study networks with over 100,000 nodes while remaining robust to noise, sparse sampling, and missing data. Across diverse benchmark systems, including coupled chaotic oscillators, neural dynamics, and epidemic spreading, it recovers governing equations with high precision and sustains accurate long-term predictions. Applied to a data set of time series of temperature measurements in 71,987 sea surface positions, SIGN identifies a compact predictive network model and captures large-scale sea surface temperature conditions up to two years in advance. By enabling equation discovery at previously inaccessible scales, SIGN opens a path toward interpretable and reliable prediction of real-world complex systems.

item.title: Dual Optimal: Make Your LLM Peer-like with Dignity
item.content:encoded: Current aligned language models exhibit a dual failure mode we term the Evasive Servant: they sycophantically validate flawed user beliefs while deflecting responsibility with boilerplate disclaimers. We propose the Dignified Peer framework, which counters servility with anti-sycophancy and trustworthiness, and mitigates evasiveness through empathy and creativity. Realizing this agent requires overcoming significant challenges in data supervision, objective collapse, and evaluation bias. We address these issues by introducing the PersonaKnob dataset which features a compositional partial order structure of multiple persona preference. This data is utilized alongside a tolerant constrained Lagrangian DPO algorithm that dynamically balances all persona dimensions to prevent behavioral collapse. Additionally, we employ a psychometrically calibrated Item Response Theory evaluation protocol to disentangle latent model persona capability from confounders like judge biases. Extensive empirical studies demonstrate that our approach successfully build a LLM agent with both dignity and peer.

item.title: Reasoning Shift: How Context Silently Shortens LLM Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) exhibiting test-time scaling behavior, such as extended reasoning traces and self-verification, have demonstrated remarkable performance on complex, long-term reasoning tasks. However, the robustness of these reasoning behaviors remains underexplored. To investigate this, we conduct a systematic evaluation of multiple reasoning models across three scenarios: (1) problems augmented with lengthy, irrelevant context; (2) multi-turn conversational settings with independent tasks; and (3) problems presented as a subtask within a complex task. We observe an interesting phenomenon: reasoning models tend to produce much shorter reasoning traces (up to 50%) for the same problem under different context conditions compared to the traces produced when the problem is presented in isolation. A finer-grained analysis reveals that this compression is associated with a decrease in self-verification and uncertainty management behaviors, such as double-checking. While this behavioral shift does not compromise performance on straightforward problems, it might affect performance on more challenging tasks. We hope our findings draw additional attention to both the robustness of reasoning models and the problem of context management for LLMs and LLM-based agents.

item.title: MIRANDA: MId-feature RANk-adversarial Domain Adaptation toward climate change-robust ecological forecasting with deep learning
item.content:encoded: Plant phenology modelling aims to predict the timing of seasonal phases, such as leaf-out or flowering, from meteorological time series. Reliable predictions are crucial for anticipating ecosystem responses to climate change. While phenology modelling has traditionally relied on mechanistic approaches, deep learning methods have recently been proposed as flexible, data-driven alternatives with often superior performance. However, mechanistic models tend to outperform deep networks when data distribution shifts are induced by climate change. Domain Adaptation (DA) techniques could help address this limitation. Yet, unlike standard DA settings, climate change induces a temporal continuum of domains and involves both a covariate and label shift, with warmer records and earlier start of spring. To tackle this challenge, we introduce Mid-feature Rank-adversarial Domain Adaptation (MIRANDA). Whereas conventional adversarial methods enforce domain invariance on final latent representations, an approach that does not explicitly address label shift, we apply adversarial regularization to intermediate features. Moreover, instead of a binary domain-classification objective, we employ a rank-based objective that enforces year-invariance in the learned meteorological representations. On a country-scale dataset spanning 70 years and comprising 67,800 phenological observations of 5 tree species, we demonstrate that, unlike conventional DA approaches, MIRANDA improves robustness to climatic distribution shifts and narrows the performance gap with mechanistic models.

item.title: Scalable Pretraining of Large Mixture of Experts Language Models on Aurora Super Computer
item.content:encoded: Pretraining Large Language Models (LLMs) from scratch requires massive amount of compute. Aurora super computer is an ExaScale machine with 127,488 Intel PVC (Ponte Vechio) GPU tiles. In this work, we showcase LLM pretraining on Aurora at the scale of 1000s of GPU tiles. Towards this effort, we developed Optimus, an inhouse training library with support for standard large model training techniques. Using Optimus, we first pretrained Mula-1B, a 1 Billion dense model and Mula-7B-A1B, a 7 Billion Mixture of Experts (MoE) model from scratch on 3072 GPU tiles for the full 4 trillion tokens of the OLMoE-mix-0924 dataset. We then demonstrated model scaling by pretraining three large MoE models Mula-20B-A2B, Mula-100B-A7B, and Mula-220B-A10B till 100 Billion tokens on the same dataset. On our largest model Mula-220B-A10B, we pushed the compute scaling from 384 to 12288 GPU tiles and observed scaling efficiency of around 90% at 12288 GPU tiles. We significantly improved the runtime performance of MoE models using custom GPU kernels for expert computation, and a novel EP-Aware sharded optimizer resulting in training speedups up to 1.71x. As part of the Optimus library, we also developed a robust set of reliability and fault tolerant features to improve training stability and continuity at scale.

item.title: LinguDistill: Recovering Linguistic Ability in Vision- Language Models via Selective Cross-Modal Distillation
item.content:encoded: Adapting pretrained language models (LMs) into vision-language models (VLMs) can degrade their native linguistic capability due to representation shift and cross-modal interference introduced during multimodal adaptation. Such loss is difficult to recover, even with targeted task-specific fine-tuning using standard objectives. Prior recovery approaches typically introduce additional modules that act as intermediate alignment layers to maintain or isolate modality-specific subspaces, which increases architectural complexity, adds parameters at inference time, and limits flexibility across models and settings. We propose LinguDistill, an adapter-free distillation method that restores linguistic capability by utilizing the original frozen LM as a teacher. We overcome the key challenge of enabling vision-conditioned teacher supervision by introducing layer-wise KV-cache sharing, which exposes the teacher to the student's multimodal representations without modifying the architecture of either model. We then selectively distill the teacher's strong linguistic signal on language-intensive data to recover language capability, while preserving the student's visual grounding on multimodal tasks. As a result, LinguDistill recovers $\sim$10% of the performance lost on language and knowledge benchmarks, while maintaining comparable performance on vision-heavy tasks. Our findings demonstrate that linguistic capability can be recovered without additional modules, providing an efficient and practical solution to modality-specific degradation in multimodal models.

item.title: An Approach to Enriching Surgical Video Datasets for Fine-Grained Spatial-Temporal Understanding of Vision-Language Models
item.content:encoded: Surgical video understanding is a crucial prerequisite for advancing Computer-Assisted Surgery. While vision-language models (VLMs) have recently been applied to the surgical domain, existing surgical vision-language datasets lack in capturing and evaluating complex, interleaved spatial-temporal dynamics. Creating large scale datasets that accurately represent fine-grained spatial-temporal relationships in surgical videos is challenging due to costly manual annotations or error-prone generation using large language models. To address this gap, we introduce the SurgSTU-Pipeline, a deterministic generation pipeline featuring temporal and spatial continuity filtering to reliably create surgical datasets for fine-grained spatial-temporal multimodal understanding. Applying this pipeline to publicly available surgical datasets, we create the SurgSTU dataset, comprising 7515 video clips densely extended with 150k fine-grained spatial-temporal question-answer samples. Our comprehensive evaluation shows that while state-of-the-art generalist VLMs struggle in zero-shot settings, their spatial-temporal capabilities can be improved through in-context learning. A fine-tuned VLM on the SurgSTU training dataset achieves highest performance among all spatial-temporal tasks, validating the dataset's efficacy to improve spatial-temporal understanding of VLMs in surgical videos. Code will be made publicly available.

item.title: MF-QAT: Multi-Format Quantization-Aware Training for Elastic Inference
item.content:encoded: Quantization-aware training (QAT) is typically performed for a single target numeric format, while practical deployments often need to choose numerical precision at inference time based on hardware support or runtime constraints. We study multi-format QAT, where a single model is trained to be robust across multiple quantization formats. We find that multi-format QAT can match single-format QAT at each target precision, yielding one model that performs well overall across different formats, even formats that were not seen during training. To enable practical deployment, we propose the Slice-and-Scale conversion procedure for both MXINT and MXFP that converts a high-precision representation into lower-precision formats without re-training. Building on this, we introduce a pipeline that (i) trains a model with multi-format QAT, (ii) stores a single anchor format checkpoint (MXINT8/MXFP8), and (iii) allows on-the-fly conversion to lower MXINT or MXFP formats at runtime with negligible-or no-additional accuracy degradation. Together, these components provide a practical path to elastic precision scaling and allow selecting the runtime format at inference time across diverse deployment targets.

item.title: Proactive Agent Research Environment: Simulating Active Users to Evaluate Proactive Assistants
item.content:encoded: Proactive agents that anticipate user needs and autonomously execute tasks hold great promise as digital assistants, yet the lack of realistic user simulation frameworks hinders their development. Existing approaches model apps as flat tool-calling APIs, failing to capture the stateful and sequential nature of user interaction in digital environments and making realistic user simulation infeasible. We introduce Proactive Agent Research Environment (Pare), a framework for building and evaluating proactive agents in digital environments. Pare models applications as finite state machines with stateful navigation and state-dependent action space for the user simulator, enabling active user simulation. Building on this foundation, we present Pare-Bench, a benchmark of 143 diverse tasks spanning communication, productivity, scheduling, and lifestyle apps, designed to test context observation, goal inference, intervention timing, and multi-app orchestration.

item.title: A CEFR-Inspired Classification Framework with Fuzzy C-Means To Automate Assessment of Programming Skills in Scratch
item.content:encoded: Context: Schools, training platforms, and technology firms increasingly need to assess programming proficiency at scale with transparent, reproducible methods that support personalized learning pathways. Objective: This study introduces a pedagogical framework for Scratch project assessment, aligned with the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR), providing universal competency levels for students and teachers alongside actionable insights for curriculum design. Method: We apply Fuzzy C-Means clustering to 2008246 Scratch projects evaluated via Dr.Scratch, implementing an ordinal criterion to map clusters to CEFR levels (A1-C2), and introducing enhanced classification metrics that identify transitional learners, enable continuous progress tracking, and quantify classification certainty to balance automated feedback with instructor review. Impact: The framework enables diagnosis of systemic curriculum gaps-notably a "B2 bottleneck" where only 13.3% of learners reside due to the cognitive load of integrating Logic Synchronization, and Data Representation--while providing certainty--based triggers for human intervention.

item.title: Does Unification Come at a Cost? Uni-SafeBench: A Safety Benchmark for Unified Multimodal Large Models
item.content:encoded: Unified Multimodal Large Models (UMLMs) integrate understanding and generation capabilities within a single architecture. While this architectural unification, driven by the deep fusion of multimodal features, enhances model performance, it also introduces important yet underexplored safety challenges. Existing safety benchmarks predominantly focus on isolated understanding or generation tasks, failing to evaluate the holistic safety of UMLMs when handling diverse tasks under a unified framework. To address this, we introduce Uni-SafeBench, a comprehensive benchmark featuring a taxonomy of six major safety categories across seven task types. To ensure rigorous assessment, we develop Uni-Judger, a framework that effectively decouples contextual safety from intrinsic safety. Based on comprehensive evaluations across Uni-SafeBench, we uncover that while the unification process enhances model capabilities, it significantly degrades the inherent safety of the underlying LLM. Furthermore, open-source UMLMs exhibit much lower safety performance than multimodal large models specialized for either generation or understanding tasks. We open-source all resources to systematically expose these risks and foster safer AGI development.

item.title: Automated Detection of Multiple Sclerosis Lesions on 7-tesla MRI Using U-net and Transformer-based Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Ultra-high field 7-tesla (7T) MRI improves visualization of multiple sclerosis (MS) white matter lesions (WML) but differs sufficiently in contrast and artifacts from 1.5-3T imaging - suggesting that widely used automated segmentation tools may not translate directly. We analyzed 7T FLAIR scans and generated reference WML masks from Lesion Segmentation Tool (LST) outputs followed by expert manual revision. As external comparators, we applied LST-LPA and the more recent LST-AI ensemble, both originally developed on lower-field data. We then trained 3D UNETR and SegFormer transformer-based models on 7T FLAIR at multiple resolutions (0.5x0.5x0.5^3, 1.0x1.0x1.0^3, and 1.5x1.5x2.0^3) and evaluated all methods using voxel-wise and lesion-wise metrics from the BraTS 2023 framework. On the held-out test set at native 0.5x0.5x0.5^3 resolution, 7T-trained transformers achieved competitive overlap with LST-AI while recovering additional small lesions that were missed by classical methods, at the cost of some boundary variability and occasional artifact-related false positives. On a held-out 7 T test set, our best transformer model (SegFormer) achieved a voxel-wise Dice of 0.61 and lesion-wise Dice of 0.20, improving on the classical LST-LPA tool (Dice 0.39, lesion-wise Dice 0.02). Performance decreased for models trained on downsampled images, underscoring the value of native 7T resolution for small-lesion detection. By releasing our 7T-trained models, we aim to provide a reproducible, ready-to-use resource for automated lesion quantification in ultra-high field MS research (https://github.com/maynord/7T-MS-lesion-segmentation).

item.title: Reconsidering Dependency Networks from an Information Geometry Perspective
item.content:encoded: Dependency networks (Heckerman et al., 2000) provide a flexible framework for modeling complex systems with many variables by combining independently learned local conditional distributions through pseudo-Gibbs sampling. Despite their computational advantages over Bayesian and Markov networks, the theoretical foundations of dependency networks remain incomplete, primarily because their model distributions -- defined as stationary distributions of pseudo-Gibbs sampling -- lack closed-form expressions. This paper develops an information-geometric analysis of pseudo-Gibbs sampling, interpreting each sampling step as an m-projection onto a full conditional manifold. Building on this interpretation, we introduce the full conditional divergence and derive an upper bound that characterizes the location of the stationary distribution in the space of probability distributions. We then reformulate both structure and parameter learning as optimization problems that decompose into independent subproblems for each node, and prove that the learned model distribution converges to the true underlying distribution as the number of training samples grows to infinity. Experiments confirm that the proposed upper bound is tight in practice.

item.title: Embarrassingly Simple Self-Distillation Improves Code Generation
item.content:encoded: Can a large language model (LLM) improve at code generation using only its own raw outputs, without a verifier, a teacher model, or reinforcement learning? We answer in the affirmative with simple self-distillation (SSD): sample solutions from the model with certain temperature and truncation configurations, then fine-tune on those samples with standard supervised fine-tuning. SSD improves Qwen3-30B-Instruct from 42.4% to 55.3% pass@1 on LiveCodeBench v6, with gains concentrating on harder problems, and it generalizes across Qwen and Llama models at 4B, 8B, and 30B scale, including both instruct and thinking variants. To understand why such a simple method can work, we trace these gains to a precision-exploration conflict in LLM decoding and show that SSD reshapes token distributions in a context-dependent way, suppressing distractor tails where precision matters while preserving useful diversity where exploration matters. Taken together, SSD offers a complementary post-training direction for improving LLM code generation.

item.title: 基于LSFM的全脑高分辨率数据集CANVAS助力生物结构研究
item.content:encoded:
CANVAS提供高分辨率全脑数据，推动生物结构分析。



摘要： 本文介绍了CANVAS，一个用于生物结构研究的高分辨率全脑光片荧光显微镜（LSFM）基准数据集。该数据集包含六种神经元和免疫细胞标记物、细胞注释及排行榜，旨在解决大规模数据处理与分析的挑战。由于细胞形态在不同表型和脑区的异质性，现有模型在泛化能力上存在不足。CANVAS是首个在亚细胞水平捕捉完整小鼠脑组织并涵盖广泛细胞注释的LSFM基准数据集。

item.title: 对话自然语言生成中任务示例对生成质量的影响分析
item.content:encoded:
任务示例提升复杂任务和小数据集的生成质量。



摘要： 本文研究了在对话自然语言生成（NLG）中，通过提供任务示例（MR-句子对）是否能提升生成模型的表现。实验基于五个语言学指标和四个不同特征的数据集进行分析，发现任务示例在复杂任务、小数据集以及零样本设置下效果显著。此外，研究还表明语义指标比词汇指标更能准确评估生成质量，特别是基于人类评分的语义指标能够检测出嵌入式指标难以捕捉的细微问题。结果还显示，生成模型在语义和交际意图层面具有良好的适应性和鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于PhiSat-2的单目建筑高度估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
PhiSat-2数据提升单目建筑高度估计效果。



摘要： 本文针对单目建筑高度估计的挑战，利用PhiSat-2卫星数据构建了PHDataset数据集，并提出Two-Stream Ordinal Network (TSONet) 方法。该方法结合了建筑轮廓分割与高度估计，引入Cross-Stream Exchange Module和Feature-Enhanced Bin Refinement模块，显著提升了模型性能。实验表明，TSONet在MAE、RMSE、IoU和F1-score等指标上优于现有方法，验证了PhiSat-2在建筑高度估计中的潜力。

item.title: 长周期LLM代理的可靠性评估框架与实证分析
item.content:encoded:
研究提出可靠性评估框架，揭示模型在长时间任务中的性能变化。



摘要： 本文提出了一种针对长周期大语言模型代理的可靠性科学框架，包含四个关键指标：可靠性衰减曲线（RDC）、方差放大因子（VAF）、渐进退化评分（GDS）和崩溃临界点（MOP）。通过在396个任务、23,392次实验中对10个模型进行评估，发现模型的可靠性随任务时长显著下降，且不同领域表现差异明显。高方差放大因子反映的是模型能力而非不稳定，能力与可靠性排名存在较大差异，前沿模型因尝试复杂策略而更易崩溃，同时记忆结构会普遍降低长期性能。这些发现强调了可靠性作为重要评估维度的必要性。

item.title: Measuring the metacognition of AI
item.content:encoded: A robust decision-making process must take into account uncertainty, especially when the choice involves inherent risks. Because artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly integrated into decision-making workflows, managing uncertainty relies more and more on the metacognitive capabilities of these systems; i.e, their ability to assess the reliability of and regulate their own decisions. Hence, it is crucial to employ robust methods to measure the metacognitive abilities of AI. This paper is primarily a methodological contribution arguing for the adoption of the meta-d' framework, or its model-free alternatives, as the gold standard for assessing the metacognitive sensitivity of AIs--the ability to generate confidence ratings that distinguish correct from incorrect responses. Moreover, we propose to leverage signal detection theory (SDT) to measure the ability of AIs to spontaneously regulate their decisions based on uncertainty and risk. To demonstrate the practical utility of these psychophysical frameworks, we conduct two series of experiments on three large language models (LLMs)--GPT-5, DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp, and Mistral-Medium-2508. In the first experiments, LLMs performed a primary judgment followed by a confidence rating. In the second, LLMs only performed the primary judgment, while we manipulated the risk associated with either response. On the one hand, applying the meta-d' framework allows us to conduct comparisons along three axes: comparing an LLM to optimality, comparing different LLMs on a given task, and comparing the same LLM across different tasks. On the other hand, SDT allows us to assess whether LLMs become more conservative when risks are high.

item.title: 多智能体系统在自动化机器学习优化中的比较研究
item.content:encoded:
多智能体系统在自动化机器学习中表现出不同的优化效率与稳定性。



摘要： 本文通过系统实验研究了不同多智能体结构在自动化机器学习优化中的效果。采用严格的测试环境，对比了单智能体、子智能体架构和团队智能体架构的表现。结果表明，子智能体模式在时间限制下具有高稳定性和高吞吐量，适合广域浅层优化；而团队智能体虽易受干扰，但在计算资源充足时能实现更深层次的理论对齐，适用于复杂架构重构。研究为未来自主研究系统的设计提供了实用指导。

item.title: 基于KFAC的双层优化方法提升机器学习效率
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种基于KFAC的双层优化方法，提升机器学习效率。



摘要： 本文研究了双层优化（BO）在机器学习中的应用，指出传统方法在计算超梯度时依赖粗略近似，导致性能受限。作者基于隐函数定理，引入Kronecker-factored approximate curvature（KFAC）来获取更准确的曲率信息，从而提高超梯度计算的效率和准确性。实验表明，该方法在元学习和AI安全等任务中表现优于CG或Neumann方法，且在BERT等大模型上展现出显著优势，仅需少量额外内存和运行时间。相关代码已开源。

item.title: 任务自动化程度的经济评估框架
item.content:encoded:
研究提出自动化程度的经济评估框架，强调部分自动化更优。



摘要： 本文提出一个统一框架，用于评估任务自动化的最优程度。不同于传统的是否自动化的二元判断，文章将自动化强度建模为连续选择，企业通过选择AI准确度来最小化成本，从无自动化到人机协作再到全自动化。研究通过规模定律实验估计了AI生产函数，并发现AI系统的回报具有可预测但递减的特性，高精度成本呈凸性增长。因此，全自动化通常并非成本最低，部分自动化更为常见。文章还引入基于熵的任务复杂度指标，量化不同准确度下的人工替代比例。通过O*NET数据、专家调查和GPT-4o任务分解进行校准，结果显示任务复杂度影响替代程度，低复杂度任务更易被替代，而高复杂度任务则倾向于有限部分自动化。部署规模是关键因素，AI即服务和AI代理可分摊固定成本，扩大经济可行任务范围。在企业层面，有效自动化可覆盖约11%的计算机视觉相关劳动力成本，大规模部署下该比例显著上升。由于其他AI系统也具备类似经济学规律，该机制适用于更广泛领域，表明部分自动化是长期经济理性选择。

item.title: 基于统计力学的Transformer模型参数空间分析
item.content:encoded:
研究Transformer在蛋白质序列数据中的参数空间与性能关系。



摘要： 本文通过统计力学框架，利用Langevin动力学在不同温度下采样Transformer模型的损失景观，以分析其在蛋白质结构预测中的优异性能。研究发现，与前馈网络不同，Transformer在损失函数中缺乏一阶相变，从而在中间温度范围内表现出良好的学习特性。当嵌入维度最优时，大多数层的参数高度保守，并提供了一种确定该维度的方法。此外，高温度和高维度的注意力矩阵对蛋白质接触图的预测更具优势。

item.title: EC-Bench：长视频计数与时间证据定位的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
EC-Bench评估长视频中的计数与时间证据定位，揭示当前模型的局限性。



摘要： 本文介绍了EC-Bench，一个用于评估长视频中计数、枚举和时间证据定位的基准测试。该基准包含152段超过30分钟的视频和1,699个带有明确证据片段的查询。实验发现，尽管模型在短片段上表现尚可，但在长视频任务中表现较差，最佳模型在枚举和计数任务上的准确率分别为29.98%和23.74%，远低于人类水平（78.57%和82.97%）。研究还表明，枚举准确性、时间定位与计数性能之间存在显著关联，凸显了当前多模态大语言模型在长视频定量推理方面的不足。

item.title: 大语言模型在方言语音分类中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
大语言模型在方言识别中表现良好，结合语言信息可提升分类效果。



摘要： 由于标注方言语音数据稀缺，方言语音分类对大多数语言来说是一项挑战。本文探讨了大语言模型（LLMs）在理解方言方面的能力，并将其与HuBERT等模型进行比较。研究还提供了LLM和人类语言学家的基准。方法基于自动语音识别系统生成的音素转录，并结合方言特征图、元音历史和规则等语言资源。结果表明，当提供语言信息时，LLM的预测性能有所提升。人类基准显示，自动生成的转录有助于分类，但也存在改进空间。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的有限状态机设计与修复框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于LLM的FSM设计与修复方法，提升系统可靠性。



摘要： 本文探讨了如何利用大语言模型（LLM）从自然语言需求文档中生成有限状态机（FSM），并提出一种专家驱动的FSM修复方法。FSM在模型驱动工程中起着关键作用，其质量直接影响系统的测试效果和生产稳定性。研究通过实验分析了LLM在FSM生成与修复中的能力，并展示了模拟数据下的实验结果，为机器学习技术在MDE领域的应用提供了新视角。

item.title: 基于视觉记忆机制的长视频理解方法FlexMem
item.content:encoded:
FlexMem提升长视频理解能力，支持无限长度视频处理。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在长视频理解上的挑战，提出一种无需训练的新型方法FlexMem。该方法模拟人类观看视频时的持续记忆与回忆行为，通过视觉KV缓存作为记忆源，并采用双路径压缩设计实现高效记忆传输和写入。FlexMem还探索了多种适用于不同视频理解任务的记忆读取策略，包括流式处理。实验表明，FlexMem在单块3090 GPU上可处理超过1000帧视频，显著优于现有方法，并使基础MLLMs在部分基准测试中达到或超越SOTA模型性能。

item.title: Metriplector：基于物理系统的神经架构原语
item.content:encoded:
Metriplector通过物理系统实现高效计算，适用于多种任务。



摘要： Metriplector是一种基于物理系统的神经架构原语，其输入配置抽象的物理系统（如场、源和算子），并通过该系统的动态进行计算。多个场通过耦合的Metriplectic动力学演化，而由Noether定理得出的应力-能量张量作为输出。该框架支持从纯耗散分支到完整结构的多种实例化方式，可应用于图像识别、语言建模等任务。实验表明，Metriplector在迷宫路径规划、数独求解、CIFAR-100分类和语言建模等多个领域均表现出色，具有较高的准确率和较低的参数量与训练数据需求。

item.title: EarthEmbeddingExplorer: A Web Application for Cross-Modal Retrieval of Global Satellite Images
item.content:encoded: While the Earth observation community has witnessed a surge in high-impact foundation models and global Earth embedding datasets, a significant barrier remains in translating these academic assets into freely accessible tools. This tutorial introduces EarthEmbeddingExplorer, an interactive web application designed to bridge this gap, transforming static research artifacts into dynamic, practical workflows for discovery. We will provide a comprehensive hands-on guide to the system, detailing its cloud-native software architecture, demonstrating cross-modal queries (natural language, visual, and geolocation), and showcasing how to derive scientific insights from retrieval results. By democratizing access to precomputed Earth embeddings, this tutorial empowers researchers to seamlessly transition from state-of-the-art models and data archives to real-world application and analysis. The web application is available at https://modelscope.ai/studios/Major-TOM/EarthEmbeddingExplorer.

item.title: iPoster：一种交互式海报布局生成框架
item.content:encoded:
iPoster通过用户约束生成高质量海报布局。



摘要： iPoster是一种交互式海报布局生成框架，允许用户通过指定灵活的约束条件来引导内容感知的布局设计。用户可以在意图模块中定义元素类别、大小、位置或粗略草图，生成模块则根据这些约束即时生成符合上下文的精细布局。该框架采用统一的图增强扩散架构，支持多种设计任务，并通过掩码策略在去噪过程中精确保留用户输入。跨内容感知注意力模块确保生成元素与画布显著区域对齐，提升视觉一致性。实验表明，iPoster不仅实现了最先进的布局质量，还提供了响应迅速且可控的海报设计框架。

item.title: 动态升级专家混合模型DUME提升多领域语言模型性能
item.content:encoded:
DUME通过复用已有专家模型，提升多任务语言模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为动态升级专家混合模型（DUME）的新方法，旨在解决大型语言模型在多领域任务中训练成本高、易过度专业化及任务间干扰等问题。DUME通过复用已训练的密集专家模型，构建统一的混合专家架构，在不进行额外训练的情况下保持原有专家能力。该方法利用岭回归的闭式解，实现高效且可扩展的模型构建。实验表明，DUME在因果语言建模和推理任务中均优于基线模型，甚至在某些任务中超过单一领域专家模型。

item.title: 基于上下文感知的开放词汇遥感分割方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出ConInfer框架提升遥感图像分割性能。



摘要： 本文针对开放词汇遥感分割（OVRSS）中存在的局部预测不足问题，提出一种基于上下文感知的推理框架ConInfer。该框架通过联合多个空间单元的预测，并显式建模它们之间的语义依赖关系，从而提升分割的一致性、鲁棒性和泛化能力。实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上优于现有最先进的基于视觉-语言模型的分割方法，在开放词汇语义分割和目标提取任务中分别提升了2.80%和6.13%。

item.title: SimMOF：基于大语言模型的金属有机框架模拟自动化框架
item.content:encoded:
SimMOF实现MOF模拟流程的自动化，提升研究效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为SimMOF的基于大语言模型的多智能体框架，用于自动化金属有机框架（MOFs）的模拟流程。用户可以通过自然语言查询，由SimMOF生成依赖关系明确的计划、可运行的输入，并协调多个智能体执行模拟，最终根据用户需求总结分析结果。通过案例研究，展示了SimMOF能够实现适应性强、认知自主的模拟流程，反映人类研究人员的迭代和决策行为，为数据驱动的MOF研究提供可扩展的基础。

item.title: FLEURS-Kobani：首个北库尔德语语音基准数据集
item.content:encoded:
FLEURS-Kobani是首个北库尔德语语音基准数据集，支持ASR和S2TT任务。



摘要： FLEURS-Kobani是一个针对北库尔德语（ISO 639-3 KMR）的语音扩展数据集，包含5,162个经过验证的语音片段，总时长18小时24分钟，由31位母语者录制。该数据集填补了北库尔德语在自动语音识别（ASR）和语音到文本翻译（S2TT）任务中的研究空白。实验中使用Whisper v3-large模型进行微调，采用两阶段策略（Common Voice到FLEURS-Kobani）取得了最佳ASR性能（测试集WER 28.11，CER 9.84）。在KMR到EN的端到端S2TT任务中，Whisper模型取得8.68 BLEU得分。此外，还报告了基于转换的目标和级联S2TT设置。该数据集已通过CC BY 4.0许可公开发布，用于学术研究。

item.title: 基于信心校准的放射报告生成模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出ConRad框架提升AI生成放射报告的可信度与可解释性。



摘要： 本文针对大型视觉语言模型在放射报告生成中的安全部署问题，提出了一种名为ConRad的信心校准框架。该框架通过强化学习方法对医学LVLM进行微调，使其能够生成具有校准信心值的报告，从而提高AI输出的可靠性。研究包括报告级和句子级信心评估两种方式，并采用GRPO算法优化模型表现。实验表明，ConRad显著提升了信心校准效果，并在临床评估中与医生判断高度一致，有助于减少错误信息对临床决策的影响。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的议程驱动叙事提取方法
item.content:encoded:
通过大语言模型实现多视角叙事生成，提升议程对齐度与叙事连贯性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型的议程驱动叙事提取方法，旨在解决现有叙事生成方法在连贯性、交互性和多故事线支持之间的权衡问题。该方法通过在路径规划过程中引入大语言模型，根据用户指定的议程对候选文档进行排序，从而引导叙事构建。实验表明，该方法在语义议程上的对齐度比关键词匹配高9.9%，在特定议题上甚至提升13.3%。同时，叙事连贯性损失仅为2.2%，且无法生成与原始材料矛盾的叙事，验证了方法的有效性和可靠性。

item.title: MONA方法的扩展与实验验证：减少多步奖励欺骗
item.content:encoded:
MONA通过限制规划范围并提供远见批准信号，有效减少奖励欺骗。



摘要： 本文对MONA方法进行了扩展和实验验证，探讨了批准机制对安全保证的影响。研究重新包装了原始代码库，并确认了普通强化学习（91.5%奖励欺骗率）与Oracle MONA（0%欺骗率）之间的对比。同时引入了多种批准机制，如oracle、噪声、错误设定、学习和校准等。在初步实验中，最佳校准学习监督模型实现了零奖励欺骗，但意图行为率较低（11.9% vs. 99.9%），表明是优化不足而非重新出现欺骗。研究结果支持了MONA论文中的批准谱猜想，并强调了构建能保持远见又不开放奖励欺骗通道的学习批准模型的重要性。代码和配置已公开。

item.title: 基于先验的生存分析模型SIC在真实数据中的表现
item.content:encoded:
SIC模型在生存分析中表现出色，无需微调即可进行个体化预测。



摘要： 本文提出了一种灵活的生存数据分析框架，通过定义丰富的生存先验来控制协变量和时间事件分布。在此基础上，研究者开发了Survival In-Context（SIC）模型，该模型仅在合成数据上预训练，能够单次前向传播生成个性化生存预测，无需任务特定训练或超参数调整。在多个真实世界生存数据集上的评估表明，SIC在中等规模数据下表现优于传统和深度生存模型，展示了先验拟合基础模型在生存分析中的潜力。代码将在发表后公开。

item.title: 提升视觉-语言模型对否定表达的理解能力
item.content:encoded:
Omni-NegCLIP改进了CLIP模型对存在与缺失否定的理解能力。



摘要： 本文提出Omni-NegCLIP，一种针对CLIP模型的微调方法，旨在提升其对两种否定表达（存在型和缺失型）的理解能力。通过修改原始的InfoNCE对比损失，设计了两种对比目标：一种是拉近图像嵌入与原描述嵌入、推远与存在型否定描述嵌入的距离；另一种是使图像嵌入同时与原描述和缺失型否定描述对齐，但保持两者语义区分。实验表明，Omni-NegCLIP在存在型和缺失型否定任务上分别提升了52.65%和12.50%，同时在图像-文本检索任务中表现更优。

item.title: 基于隐式成本的列车装载优化模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型列车装载优化模型，减少再处理操作。



摘要： 本文研究了列车集装箱装载问题，该问题在物流和供应链管理中具有重要地位。传统方法通过引入二进制变量和逻辑约束来建模再处理操作，导致模型规模庞大且难以求解。本文提出一种创新的紧凑型数学模型，将再处理成本隐式地包含在目标函数中，从而避免了专用变量和约束，大幅减少了模型复杂度。通过与传统模型的对比分析，验证了新模型在变量和约束数量上的显著优势。同时，采用模拟退火算法对模型进行评估，结果表明该模型不仅更简洁，而且在实际应用中表现出良好的性能，为现代铁路物流提供了高效工具。

item.title: 基于语义与几何引导的3D问答视觉token压缩方法
item.content:encoded:
SeGPruner提升3D问答效率，减少91%视觉token。



摘要： 本文提出SeGPruner，一种用于多视角图像3D问答任务的视觉token压缩框架。该方法通过注意力机制保留语义显著的视觉token，并结合几何信息补充空间多样性，从而在大幅降低视觉token数量的同时保持3D推理性能。实验表明，SeGPruner在ScanQA和OpenEQA数据集上将视觉token预算减少91%，推理延迟降低86%，展现出高效且稳定的性能。

item.title: 轻量级图神经网络VulGNN在漏洞检测中的应用
item.content:encoded:
VulGNN在漏洞检测中表现接近LLM，但更轻量且易于部署。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于图神经网络（GNN）的轻量级漏洞检测模型VulGNN。与大型语言模型（LLMs）相比，VulGNN在代码的自然图结构基础上实现了相近的检测性能，但模型规模仅为LLMs的1/100，且具备更快的重新训练和定制能力。研究详细描述了VulGNN的架构、组件消融实验、学习率设置以及对不同代码数据集的泛化能力。作为一种高效的漏洞分析工具，VulGNN适用于边缘计算环境，并可集成到实际的软件开发流程中。

item.title: RAAP：统一检索与对齐学习的物体功能预测框架
item.content:encoded:
RAAP提升机器人在未见物体上的零样本操作能力。



摘要： 本文提出RAAP框架，通过结合物体功能检索与对齐学习，提升机器人在多样和非结构化环境中的交互能力。该方法将静态接触点定位与动态动作方向预测分离，利用密集对应关系传递接触点，并通过检索增强的对齐模型预测动作方向。RAAP在少量样本下表现稳定，适用于未见物体和类别，在仿真和真实世界中实现了零样本机器人操作。

item.title: 基于梯度和协方差的神经网络不确定性量化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种轻量级不确定性量化方法，适用于大语言模型。



摘要： 本文提出了一种计算高效的方法来量化神经网络的预测不确定性。该方法通过一阶泰勒展开和参数协方差的各向同性假设，从单次前向-反向传播中得到认知不确定性和随机不确定性。研究验证了该方法在合成问题上的有效性，并将其应用于大语言模型的问答任务中，发现其在TruthfulQA数据集上表现优异，但在TriviaQA数据集上效果较差，表明参数级不确定性与自评估方法存在本质差异。
