item.title: Positional Segmentor-Guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning for Spatially Localized Image Synthesis
item.content:encoded: Counterfactual image generation enables controlled data augmentation, bias mitigation, and disease modeling. However, existing methods guided by external classifiers or regressors are limited to subject-level factors (e.g., age) and fail to produce localized structural changes, often resulting in global artifacts. Pixel-level guidance using segmentation masks has been explored, but requires user-defined counterfactual masks, which are tedious and impractical. Segmentor-guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning (Seg-CFT) addressed this by using segmentation-derived measurements to supervise structure-specific variables, yet it remains restricted to global interventions. We propose Positional Seg-CFT, which subdivides each structure into regional segments and derives independent measurements per region, enabling spatially localized and anatomically coherent counterfactuals. Experiments on coronary CT angiography show that Pos-Seg-CFT generates realistic, region-specific modifications, providing finer spatial control for modeling disease progression.

item.title: Silicon Bureaucracy and AI Test-Oriented Education: Contamination Sensitivity and Score Confidence in LLM Benchmarks
item.content:encoded: Public benchmarks increasingly govern how large language models (LLMs) are ranked, selected, and deployed. We frame this benchmark-centered regime as Silicon Bureaucracy and AI Test-Oriented Education, and argue that it rests on a fragile assumption: that benchmark scores directly reflect genuine generalization. In practice, however, such scores may conflate exam-oriented competence with principled capability, especially when contamination and semantic leakage are difficult to exclude from modern training pipelines. We therefore propose an audit framework for analyzing contamination sensitivity and score confidence in LLM benchmarks. Using a router-worker setup, we compare a clean-control condition with noisy conditions in which benchmark problems are systematically deleted, rewritten, and perturbed before being passed downstream. For a genuinely clean benchmark, noisy conditions should not consistently outperform the clean-control baseline. Yet across multiple models, we find widespread but heterogeneous above-baseline gains under noisy conditions, indicating that benchmark-related cues may be reassembled and can reactivate contamination-related memory. These results suggest that similar benchmark scores may carry substantially different levels of confidence. Rather than rejecting benchmarks altogether, we argue that benchmark-based evaluation should be supplemented with explicit audits of contamination sensitivity and score confidence.

item.title: Generalization Limits of In-Context Operator Networks for Higher-Order Partial Differential Equations
item.content:encoded: We investigate the generalization capabilities of In-Context Operator Networks (ICONs), a new class of operator networks that build on the principles of in-context learning, for higher-order partial differential equations. We extend previous work by expanding the type and scope of differential equations handled by the foundation model. We demonstrate that while processing complex inputs requires some new computational methods, the underlying machine learning techniques are largely consistent with simpler cases. Our implementation shows that although point-wise accuracy degrades for higher-order problems like the heat equation, the model retains qualitative accuracy in capturing solution dynamics and overall behavior. This demonstrates the model's ability to extrapolate fundamental solution characteristics to problems outside its training regime.

item.title: I3DM: Implicit 3D-aware Memory Retrieval and Injection for Consistent Video Scene Generation
item.content:encoded: Despite remarkable progress in video generation, maintaining long-term scene consistency upon revisiting previously explored areas remains challenging. Existing solutions rely either on explicitly constructing 3D geometry, which suffers from error accumulation and scale ambiguity, or on naive camera Field-of-View (FoV) retrieval, which typically fails under complex occlusions. To overcome these limitations, we propose I3DM, a novel implicit 3D-aware memory mechanism for consistent video scene generation that bypasses explicit 3D reconstruction. At the core of our approach is a 3D-aware memory retrieval strategy, which leverages the intermediate features of a pre-trained Feed-Forward Novel View Synthesis (FF-NVS) model to score view relevance, enabling robust retrieval even in highly occluded scenarios. Furthermore, to fully utilize the retrieved historical frames, we introduce a 3D-aligned memory injection module. This module implicitly warps historical content to the target view and adaptively conditions the generation on reliable warping regions, leading to improved revisit consistency and accurate camera control. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, achieving superior revisit consistency, generation fidelity, and camera control precision.

item.title: A Unified Spatial Alignment Framework for Highly Transferable Transformation-Based Attacks on Spatially Structured Tasks
item.content:encoded: Transformation-based adversarial attacks (TAAs) demonstrate strong transferability when deceiving classification models. However, existing TAAs often perform unsatisfactorily or even fail when applied to structured tasks such as semantic segmentation and object detection. Encouragingly, recent studies that categorize transformations into non-spatial and spatial transformations inspire us to address this challenge. We find that for non-structured tasks, labels are spatially non-structured, and thus TAAs are not required to adjust labels when applying spatial transformations. In contrast, for structured tasks, labels are spatially structured, and failing to transform labels synchronously with inputs can cause spatial misalignment and yield erroneous gradients. To address these issues, we propose a novel unified Spatial Alignment Framework (SAF) for highly transferable TAAs on spatially structured tasks, where the TAAs spatially transform labels synchronously with the input using the proposed Spatial Alignment (SA) algorithm. Extensive experiments demonstrate the crucial role of our SAF for TAAs on structured tasks. Specifically, in non-targeted attacks, our SAF degrades the average mIoU on Cityscapes from 24.50 to 11.34, and on Kvasir-SEG from 49.91 to 31.80, while reducing the average mAP of COCO from 17.89 to 5.25.

item.title: Quality Over Clicks: Intrinsic Quality-Driven Iterative Reinforcement Learning for Cold-Start E-Commerce Query Suggestion
item.content:encoded: Existing dialogue systems rely on Query Suggestion (QS) to enhance user engagement. Recent efforts typically employ large language models with Click-Through Rate (CTR) model, yet fail in cold-start scenarios due to their heavy reliance on abundant online click data for effective CTR model training. To bridge this gap, we propose Cold-EQS, an iterative reinforcement learning framework for Cold-Start E-commerce Query Suggestion (EQS). Specifically, we leverage answerability, factuality, and information gain as reward to continuously optimize the quality of suggested queries. To continuously optimize our QS model, we estimate uncertainty for grouped candidate suggested queries to select hard and ambiguous samples from online user queries lacking click signals. In addition, we provide an EQS-Benchmark comprising 16,949 online user queries for offline training and evaluation. Extensive offline and online experiments consistently demonstrate a strong positive correlation between online and offline effectiveness. Both offline and online experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our Cold-EQS, achieving a significant +6.81% improvement in online chatUV.

item.title: Cross-Instance Gaussian Splatting Registration via Geometry-Aware Feature-Guided Alignment
item.content:encoded: We present Gaussian Splatting Alignment (GSA), a novel method for aligning two independent 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) models via a similarity transformation (rotation, translation, and scale), even when they are of different objects in the same category (e.g., different cars). In contrast, existing methods can only align 3DGS models of the same object (e.g., the same car) and often must be given true scale as input, while we estimate it successfully. GSA leverages viewpoint-guided spherical map features to obtain robust correspondences and introduces a two-step optimization framework that aligns 3DGS models while keeping them fixed. First, we apply an iterative feature-guided absolute orientation solver as our coarse registration, which is robust to poor initialization (e.g., 180 degrees misalignment or a 10x scale gap). Next, we use a fine registration step that enforces multi-view feature consistency, inspired by inverse radiance-field formulations. The first step already achieves state-of-the-art performance, and the second further improves results. In the same-object case, GSA outperforms prior works, often by a large margin, even when the other methods are given the true scale. In the harder case of different objects in the same category, GSA vastly surpasses them, providing the first effective solution for category-level 3DGS registration and unlocking new applications. Project webpage: https://bgu-cs-vil.github.io/GSA-project/

item.title: Minibal: Balanced Game-Playing Without Opponent Modeling
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in game AI, such as AlphaZero and Athénan, have achieved superhuman performance across a wide range of board games. While highly powerful, these agents are ill-suited for human-AI interaction, as they consistently overwhelm human players, offering little enjoyment and limited educational value. This paper addresses the problem of balanced play, in which an agent challenges its opponent without either dominating or conceding. We introduce Minibal (Minimize & Balance), a variant of Minimax specifically designed for balanced play. Building on this concept, we propose several modifications of the Unbounded Minimax algorithm explicitly aimed at discovering balanced strategies. Experiments conducted across seven board games demonstrate that one variant consistently achieves the most balanced play, with average outcomes close to perfect balance. These results establish Minibal as a promising foundation for designing AI agents that are both challenging and engaging, suitable for both entertainment and serious games.

item.title: On associative neural networks for sparse patterns with huge capacities
item.content:encoded: Generalized Hopfield models with higher-order or exponential interaction terms are known to have substantially larger storage capacities than the classical quadratic model. On the other hand, associative memories for sparse patterns, such as the Willshaw and Amari models, already outperform the classical Hopfield model in the sparse regime. In this paper we combine these two mechanisms. We introduce higher-order versions of sparse associative memory models and study their storage capacities. For fixed interaction order $n$, we obtain storage capacities of polynomial order in the system size. When the interaction order is allowed to grow logarithmically with the number of neurons, this yields super-polynomial capacities. We also discuss an analogue in the Gripon--Berrou architecture which was formulated for non-sparse messages (see \cite{griponc}). Our results show that the capacity increase caused by higher-order interactions persists in the sparse setting, although the precise storage scale depends on the underlying architecture.

item.title: FDIF: Formula-Driven supervised Learning with Implicit Functions for 3D Medical Image Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Deep learning-based 3D medical image segmentation methods relies on large-scale labeled datasets, yet acquiring such data is difficult due to privacy constraints and the high cost of expert annotation. Formula-Driven Supervised Learning (FDSL) offers an appealing alternative by generating training data and labels directly from mathematical formulas. However, existing voxel-based approaches are limited in geometric expressiveness and cannot synthesize realistic textures. We introduce Formula-Driven supervised learning with Implicit Functions (FDIF), a framework that enables scalable pre-training without using any real data and medical expert annotations. FDIF introduces an implicit-function representation based on signed distance functions (SDFs), enabling compact modeling of complex geometries while exploiting the surface representation of SDFs to support controllable synthesis of both geometric and intensity textures. Across three medical image segmentation benchmarks (AMOS, ACDC, and KiTS) and three architectures (SwinUNETR, nnUNet ResEnc-L, and nnUNet Primus-M), FDIF consistently improves over a formula-driven method, and achieves performance comparable to self-supervised approaches pre-trained on large-scale real datasets. We further show that FDIF pre-training also benefits 3D classification tasks, highlighting implicit-function-based formula supervision as a promising paradigm for data-free representation learning. Code is available at https://github.com/yamanoko/FDIF.

item.title: The Value of Information in Resource-Constrained Pricing
item.content:encoded: Firms that price perishable resources -- airline seats, hotel rooms, seasonal inventory -- now routinely use demand predictions, but these predictions vary widely in quality. Under hard capacity constraints, acting on an inaccurate prediction can irreversibly deplete inventory needed for future periods. We study how prediction uncertainty propagates into dynamic pricing decisions with linear demand, stochastic noise, and finite capacity. A certified demand forecast with known error bound~$ε^0$ specifies where the system should operate: it shifts regret from $O(\sqrt{T})$ to $O(\log T)$ when $ε^0 \lesssim T^{-1/4}$, and we prove this threshold is tight. A misspecified surrogate model -- biased but correlated with true demand -- cannot set prices directly but reduces learning variance by a factor of $(1-ρ^2)$ through control variates. The two mechanisms compose: the forecast determines the regret regime; the surrogate tightens estimation within it. All algorithms rest on a boundary attraction mechanism that stabilizes pricing near degenerate capacity boundaries without requiring non-degeneracy assumptions. Experiments confirm the phase transition threshold, the variance reduction from surrogates, and robustness across problem instances.

item.title: Forecasting with Guidance: Representation-Level Supervision for Time Series Forecasting
item.content:encoded: Nowadays, time series forecasting is predominantly approached through the end-to-end training of deep learning architectures using error-based objectives. While this is effective at minimizing average loss, it encourages the encoder to discard informative yet extreme patterns. This results in smooth predictions and temporal representations that poorly capture salient dynamics. To address this issue, we propose ReGuider, a plug-in method that can be seamlessly integrated into any forecasting architecture. ReGuider leverages pretrained time series foundation models as semantic teachers. During training, the input sequence is processed together by the target forecasting model and the pretrained model. Rather than using the pretrained model's outputs directly, we extract its intermediate embeddings, which are rich in temporal and semantic information, and align them with the target model's encoder embeddings through representation-level supervision. This alignment process enables the encoder to learn more expressive temporal representations, thereby improving the accuracy of downstream forecasting. Extensive experimentation across diverse datasets and architectures demonstrates that our ReGuider consistently improves forecasting performance, confirming its effectiveness and versatility.

item.title: The DeepXube Software Package for Solving Pathfinding Problems with Learned Heuristic Functions and Search
item.content:encoded: DeepXube is a free and open-source Python package and command-line tool that seeks to automate the solution of pathfinding problems by using machine learning to learn heuristic functions that guide heuristic search algorithms tailored to deep neural networks (DNNs). DeepXube is comprised of the latest advances in deep reinforcement learning, heuristic search, and formal logic for solving pathfinding problems. This includes limited-horizon Bellman-based learning, hindsight experience replay, batched heuristic search, and specifying goals with answer-set programming. A robust multiple-inheritance structure simplifies the definition of pathfinding domains and the generation of training data. Training heuristic functions is made efficient through the automatic parallelization of the generation of training data across central processing units (CPUs) and reinforcement learning updates across graphics processing units (GPUs). Pathfinding algorithms that take advantage of the parallelism of GPUs and DNN architectures, such as batch weighted A* and Q* search and beam search are easily employed to solve pathfinding problems through command-line arguments. Finally, several convenient features for visualization, code profiling, and progress monitoring during training and solving are available. The GitHub repository is publicly available at https://github.com/forestagostinelli/deepxube.

item.title: Avoiding Over-smoothing in Social Media Rumor Detection with Pre-trained Propagation Tree Transformer
item.content:encoded: Deep learning techniques for rumor detection typically utilize Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to analyze post relations. These methods, however, falter due to over-smoothing issues when processing rumor propagation structures, leading to declining performance. Our investigation into this issue reveals that over-smoothing is intrinsically tied to the structural characteristics of rumor propagation trees, in which the majority of nodes are 1-level nodes. Furthermore, GNNs struggle to capture long-range dependencies within these trees. To circumvent these challenges, we propose a Pre-Trained Propagation Tree Transformer (P2T3) method based on pure Transformer architecture. It extracts all conversation chains from a tree structure following the propagation direction of replies, utilizes token-wise embedding to infuse connection information and introduces necessary inductive bias, and pre-trains on large-scale unlabeled datasets. Experiments indicate that P2T3 surpasses previous state-of-the-art methods in multiple benchmark datasets and performs well under few-shot conditions. P2T3 not only avoids the over-smoothing issue inherent in GNNs but also potentially offers a large model or unified multi-modal scheme for future social media research.

item.title: OmniWeaving: Towards Unified Video Generation with Free-form Composition and Reasoning
item.content:encoded: While proprietary systems such as Seedance-2.0 have achieved remarkable success in omni-capable video generation, open-source alternatives significantly lag behind. Most academic models remain heavily fragmented, and the few existing efforts toward unified video generation still struggle to seamlessly integrate diverse tasks within a single framework. To bridge this gap, we propose OmniWeaving, an omni-level video generation model featuring powerful multimodal composition and reasoning-informed capabilities. By leveraging a massive-scale pretraining dataset that encompasses diverse compositional and reasoning-augmented scenarios, OmniWeaving learns to temporally bind interleaved text, multi-image, and video inputs while acting as an intelligent agent to infer complex user intentions for sophisticated video creation. Furthermore, we introduce IntelligentVBench, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to rigorously assess next-level intelligent unified video generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OmniWeaving achieves SoTA performance among open-source unified models. The codes and model will be made publicly available soon. Project Page: https://omniweaving.github.io.

item.title: 长周期桌游任务中机器人系统状态一致性研究
item.content:encoded:
本文研究如何通过系统设计保持机器人桌游任务中的状态一致性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在长周期、回合制的机器人桌游任务中，如何通过系统设计而非单一组件优化来维持内部状态的一致性。以麻将为例，作者提出了一种集成架构，明确维护感知、执行和交互状态，将高层语义推理与时间敏感的感知和控制分离，并引入经过验证的动作原语和触觉触发的恢复机制，以防止状态过早损坏。同时，文章还介绍了用于检测回合违规和隐藏信息泄露的交互级监控机制。实验结果表明，显式的模块划分、受控的状态转换和恢复机制对于长时间运行的可靠性至关重要，而整体化或未经验证的流程会导致端到端可靠性的显著下降。该系统为研究长周期回合交互中的系统级设计原则提供了实证平台。

item.title: 基于概念分解的持续遗忘框架提升视觉语言模型拒绝能力
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架，通过概念分解实现视觉语言模型精准拒绝。



摘要： 本文针对视觉语言模型在持续遗忘任务中因序列删除请求导致的共享表征扭曲问题，提出了一种新的持续遗忘框架。该框架通过识别视觉-语言概念组合来定义遗忘类别，并利用专门的拒绝专家生成对应的拒绝响应。同时，引入多模态、概念驱动的路由机制，以复用相似概念的任务拒绝专家并适应新概念。实验表明，该方法在保持模型通用性的同时，显著提升了拒绝的准确性。

item.title: 基于多模态交互的隐性仇恨言论检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出ARCADE框架提升隐性仇恨言论识别效果。



摘要： 本文聚焦于社交媒体中隐性仇恨言论的检测问题，针对传统系统在处理多模态内容时的不足，提出一种新的检测方法。研究构建了H-VLI基准数据集，强调多模态信息之间的复杂交互对意图判断的关键作用。同时，设计了ARCADE框架，通过模拟法庭辩论过程，增强模型对深层语义线索的分析能力。实验表明，该方法在隐性仇恨言论识别任务中表现优异，优于现有主流方法。

item.title: PolarAPP：联合优化偏振成像去马赛克与下游任务
item.content:encoded:
PolarAPP提升偏振成像去马赛克和下游任务性能。



摘要： 本文提出PolarAPP，首个联合优化去马赛克与下游任务的框架。传统方法依赖低效的原始数据重组，导致重建结果不完整，限制了后续应用效果。PolarAPP通过特征对齐机制，利用元学习使去马赛克与下游网络语义一致，提升任务感知能力。同时引入等效成像约束，直接回归物理有意义的输出，无需重新排列数据。最后通过任务细化阶段进一步提升精度。实验表明，PolarAPP在去马赛克质量和下游任务表现上均优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于语义感知的视觉分词器与生成模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出SMAP分词器提升语义对齐，增强生成性能。



摘要： 本文提出SMAP，一种基于语义感知的前缀分词器，通过注入类别级语义条件到查询式1D分词框架中，提升语义对齐。为使语义在训练中不可或缺，SMAP引入尾部标记丢弃策略，迫使语义条件和早期潜在前缀承担更多责任。同时，引入CARD生成器验证潜在空间的生成能力。实验表明，SMAP在ImageNet上提升了重建质量，并在紧凑标记预算下表现出色。

item.title: 基于分层滑动量化的大型语言模型后训练量化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出SliderQuant框架提升LLM后训练量化效果。



摘要： 本文从新视角探讨了大型语言模型（LLM）的后训练量化（PTQ）问题，指出传统方法对各层一视同仁可能不适用于高挑战性比特宽度设置。通过实验发现浅层和深层对量化更敏感，尤其是首层和末层。为此，作者提出SliderQuant框架，采用分层滑动量化策略，结合三种新型滑动窗口设计和增量量化机制，有效降低量化误差。在多种LLM任务中验证表明，该方法优于现有PTQ技术，包括最新使用旋转变换的方法。

item.title: 基于差分隐私的强化学习微调框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种在奖励学习中引入差分隐私的强化学习微调框架。



摘要： 本文探讨了在基于人类反馈的强化学习中如何设计差分隐私机制。作者提出一个仅在奖励学习阶段应用差分隐私的框架，并通过理论分析证明隐私机制会增加额外的次优性差距。同时，研究还建立了最小最大下界，表明主要项随样本量和隐私水平变化。实验结果验证了理论预测，并显示该方法在隐私预算下优于现有基线方法。

item.title: VLA-MBPO：提升机器人控制的高效微调框架
item.content:encoded:
VLA-MBPO提升机器人控制性能与样本效率。



摘要： 本文提出VLA-MBPO，一种用于视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型微调的高效框架。该方法通过数据高效的多模态建模、视图解码机制和分块滚动策略，解决了真实世界交互中的成本高和安全风险问题。实验表明，VLA-MBPO在模拟和实际任务中均显著提升了策略性能和样本效率，具有良好的可扩展性和鲁棒性。

item.title: 视觉语言引导的离线强化学习框架提升任务泛化能力
item.content:encoded:
VLGOR框架通过视觉语言知识提升任务执行效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为视觉语言知识引导的离线强化学习（VLGOR）框架，旨在解决大语言模型在物理环境中缺乏直接感知能力的问题。该框架利用视觉和语言知识生成虚拟交互数据，增强离线强化学习的效果。通过微调视觉语言模型以基于初始视觉观察和高层指令预测未来状态和动作，确保生成的数据在时间上连贯、空间上合理。此外，采用反事实提示生成更多样化的数据，提高智能体根据语言指令执行任务的能力。实验表明，VLGOR在机器人操作基准测试中显著提升了未见任务的性能，成功率比基线方法高出24%以上。

item.title: 基于双曲空间的脑信号与视觉特征对齐方法
item.content:encoded:
提出HyFI框架，提升脑信号与图像的对齐效果。



摘要： 本文针对脑信号与图像之间的模态差异及语义与感知特征的纠缠问题，提出一种基于双曲空间的新型框架HyFI。该框架利用双曲空间的几何特性，在语义和感知视觉特征之间进行插值，实现感知与语义信息的融合与压缩，从而更准确地反映脑信号的表达限制。实验表明，HyFI在零样本脑到图像检索任务中表现优异，相比以往方法在THINGS-EEG和THINGS-MEG数据集上分别提升了17.3%和9.1%的Top-1准确率。

item.title: 基斯克永方言自然语言处理数据资源综述
item.content:encoded:
研究基斯克永方言NLP数据资源，提升数据稀缺问题。



摘要： 本文针对方言自然语言处理（NLP）中数据稀缺的问题，系统整理了当前可用的基斯克永方言数据与资源。文章区分了两类数据来源：一类是原本以方言撰写的在线数据，如新闻、广播、社交媒体和词典等；另一类是从标准语转换为方言的数据，包括手动和自动转换两种方式。其中，XNLI自然语言推理数据集的测试集被手动转译为西部、中部和纳瓦拉-拉普尔迪亚方言，形成了高质量的平行评估数据集。此外，基于自动转换的物理常识数据集（BasPhyCowest）也经过母语者人工评估，以判断其是否可作为全手动转换的替代方案。

item.title: 改进多模态大模型的OCR性能与训练稳定性
item.content:encoded:
提出Detached Skip-Links提升多模态模型OCR性能。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型在OCR任务中因细粒度视觉信息丢失或错位而表现不佳的问题，指出多层特征融合中的梯度干扰问题。通过引入Detached Skip-Links机制，在不增加可学习参数的情况下，有效减少梯度干扰，提升训练稳定性和收敛性。同时，提出R-Probe评估模型对细粒度视觉信息的保留能力，实验表明该方法在多个ViT模型和多模态基准上均取得显著提升。

item.title: 医学视觉问答模型的幻觉与附和权衡研究
item.content:encoded:
医学VLM在幻觉与附和间存在权衡，影响其临床应用可靠性。



摘要： 本文评估了六种视觉语言模型（包括三种通用型和三种医学专用型）在三个医学视觉问答数据集上的表现，发现模型在幻觉和附和之间存在显著权衡。具有最低幻觉倾向的模型最易附和，而最抗压的模型则表现出更高的幻觉率。为量化这一现象，作者提出三种新指标：L-VASE、CCS 和 Clinical Safety Index (CSI)，用于衡量模型的准确性和抗压性。实验结果显示，所有模型的CSI均低于0.35，表明当前7-8B参数的医学VLM尚未同时具备良好的准确性与抗压性，因此在临床使用前需进行联合评估。代码已开源。

item.title: 多分辨率融合提升视觉基础模型推理性能
item.content:encoded:
提出MuRF方法，通过多分辨率融合提升视觉基础模型的推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为多分辨率融合（MuRF）的方法，用于在推理阶段提升视觉基础模型（VFMs）的性能。传统方法通常只使用单一尺度输入，而MuRF通过在不同分辨率下处理图像并融合特征，充分利用了不同分辨率带来的互补信息。该方法无需训练，适用于多种视觉基础模型，如DINOv2和SigLIP2，并在多个关键任务中验证了其有效性。

item.title: 视觉与文本解释在教育推荐系统中的效果比较研究
item.content:encoded:
视觉解释更易提升用户对推荐系统的信任与满意度。



摘要： 本文通过一项针对54名用户的实验，比较了视觉和文本解释在教育推荐系统中的效果。研究发现，设计良好的视觉解释能够有效提升用户的感知控制、透明度、信任和满意度，且不受用户个人特征的影响。研究还分析了多种个人特征（如大五人格、认知需求等）对解释效果的调节作用，并提出了改进教育推荐系统解释设计的指导原则。

item.title: MARCH框架：通过多智能体强化自我检查减少大语言模型幻觉
item.content:encoded:
MARCH通过多智能体协作降低LLM幻觉，提升事实准确性。



摘要： 文章提出MARCH框架，用于解决大语言模型（LLM）中的幻觉问题。该框架引入三个专门代理——求解器、提议者和验证者，通过信息不对称机制避免自我确认偏差。求解器生成初始响应，提议者将其分解为可验证的命题，验证者独立验证这些命题，从而提高事实一致性。实验表明，MARCH显著降低幻觉率，8B参数模型性能接近封闭源模型。该方法为LLM的事实自优化提供了可扩展路径。

item.title: 基于情绪感知的3D说话头合成框架EmoTaG
item.content:encoded:
EmoTaG提升3D说话头合成的情绪表达与运动稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出EmoTaG，一种基于预训练与适应范式的少样本情绪感知3D说话头合成框架。通过在结构化的FLAME参数空间中进行运动预测，引入显式几何先验以提高运动稳定性。同时，设计了Gated Residual Motion Network（GRMN），从音频中捕捉情绪韵律，并结合头部姿态和上半脸线索，实现更具表现力和连贯性的运动生成。实验表明，EmoTaG在情感表达、唇形同步、视觉真实性和运动稳定性方面均达到领先水平。

item.title: 基于SAR影像的雪崩变化检测研究
item.content:encoded:
利用SAR影像进行大规模雪崩检测，提升灾害监测能力。



摘要： 本文研究了利用Sentinel-1合成孔径雷达（SAR）影像进行大范围雪崩变化检测的方法。通过在多个阿尔卑斯生态区域进行实验，验证了仅依赖前后期SAR图像的单模态变化检测方法在一致性方面表现最佳。所提出的端到端流程在保守配置下达到F1分数0.8061，在更注重召回率的配置下获得F2分数0.8414，并实现了80.36%的雪崩多边形命中率。研究展示了精度与完整性的权衡，并表明阈值调整有助于检测较小或边缘雪崩。研究还发布了标注的多区域数据集，为SAR雪崩检测提供可复现的基准。

item.title: 对比基线对语言模型拒绝行为消除的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究对比基线对模型拒绝行为消除效果的影响。



摘要： 本文研究了在指令调优语言模型中，通过方向消融去除拒绝行为时，使用主题匹配的对比基线是否能产生更优的拒绝方向。实验基于Qwen-3.5 2B模型，采用类别匹配提示对、自组织映射提取和奇异值分解正交化方法进行分析。结果表明，主题匹配的对比基线在任何测试权重层上均未产生有效的拒绝方向，而不匹配的对比基线在同一模型、代码和评估协议下，成功在六层中完全消除了拒绝行为。几何分析显示，主题匹配的减法会抵消有害与无害提示间共享的主要激活成分，使提取方向的幅度低于权重矩阵投影扰动残差流的阈值。研究讨论了对比基线设计对消融研究的启示。

item.title: AI赋能的创业民主化是否带来广泛成功？
item.content:encoded:
AI降低生产成本，但创业竞争加剧，赢家通吃。



摘要： 本文探讨了生成式AI技术如何降低数字生产成本，并挑战了‘广泛参与软件开发将催生大量可行公司’这一观点。作者提出‘建造者饱和效应’模型，指出尽管生产规模可弹性扩展，但人类注意力有限，导致市场中生产者数量增加反而稀释了平均关注度和回报。在零边际成本和自由进入的市场中，总产出虽增长，但每个生产者的收益下降。进一步分析显示，质量异质性和强化动态使均衡结果呈现平均收益下降和集中度上升的趋势，符合幂律分布特征。这表明AI驱动的创业民主化更可能加剧竞争并产生赢家通吃的局面，而非广泛分布的创业成功。

item.title: 基于强化学习的点云微调方法PointRFT研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PointRFT方法提升点云表示学习性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了强化学习在点云微调中的应用，提出了PointRFT这一专门针对点云表示学习的强化微调范式。通过设计准确度奖励和分布奖励函数，有效稳定训练过程并减少分布偏移。实验表明，PointRFT在多种基准测试中优于传统监督微调方法，尤其在数据稀缺场景下表现突出。将PointRFT与预训练-微调-强化微调结合，显著提升了点云基础模型的表征能力。

item.title: i-IF-Learn：一种联合特征选择与聚类的无监督学习框架
item.content:encoded:
提出i-IF-Learn，提升高维数据聚类效果。



摘要： 本文针对高维数据中噪声和无关特征影响聚类效果的问题，提出i-IF-Learn框架，该框架通过迭代方式同时进行特征选择和聚类。其核心创新是自适应特征选择统计量，结合伪标签监督与无监督信号，动态调整以减少误差传播。利用低维嵌入（如PCA或拉普拉斯特征映射）和k-means算法，i-IF-Learn可同时输出关键特征子集和聚类标签。实验表明，该方法在基因微阵列和单细胞RNA测序数据集上优于传统和深度聚类基线，并显著提升下游深度模型的效果。

item.title: EcoThink：一种节能的生成式AI推理框架
item.content:encoded:
EcoThink提升AI效率并减少碳排放。



摘要： 随着网络从静态检索转向生成式交互，大型语言模型（LLMs）的环境足迹成为可持续发展的重大挑战。当前方法对所有查询都采用计算密集型策略，导致LLM过度思考，增加碳排放和运营成本，影响UN可持续发展目标13和10。为此，研究提出EcoThink，一个节能的自适应推理框架，通过轻量级知识蒸馏路由器动态评估查询复杂度，优化计算资源分配。实验显示，EcoThink平均降低40.4%的推理能耗，最高达81.9%，且性能损失不显著，为可持续、包容和高效的生成式AI提供可行路径。

item.title: AI监测下椅子与病床跌倒率比较研究
item.content:encoded:
AI监测显示椅子跌倒率高于病床，需优化椅子设计。



摘要： 本回顾性队列研究利用连续AI监测评估跌倒率，以暴露时间而非床位占用天数为单位。研究期间共收集3980个监测单元的292,914小时数据，得出椅子暴露每千小时跌倒率为17.8次，病床为4.3次。研究中43起经裁定的跌倒事件中，40起与有效暴露时间相关，调整后的椅子与病床跌倒率比值为2.35（95% CI 0.87-6.33；p=0.0907）。在更广泛的观察队列中，7起直接椅子跌倒中有6起涉及脚踏板定位失败。由于是单一医疗系统的观察性研究，结果仍为假设生成，支持测试更安全的椅子设置而非减少使用。

item.title: 基于事件流的任意时间语义分割方法
item.content:encoded:
提出LiFR-Seg框架，实现低帧率下高精度语义分割。



摘要： 本文针对动态环境中标准相机低帧率导致的语义分割性能下降问题，提出了一种新的任务——任意时间语义分割。该方法通过单个过去RGB图像和异步事件数据，在任意时间点进行语义分割。核心是基于事件驱动运动场的不确定性感知变形过程，结合时序记忆注意力模块，提升动态场景下的分割一致性。在DSEC数据集和新提出的SHF-DSEC合成数据集上验证了方法的有效性，其性能接近高帧率系统的上限。

item.title: 基于超几何的自适应时间控制策略提升智能体效率
item.content:encoded:
文章提出一种自适应时间控制方法，提升智能体在连续环境中的决策效率。



摘要： 本文提出了一种轻量级的自适应时间控制机制，通过学习最优认知间隔来替代传统生物启发式定时器。该系统引入了基于双曲几何的预测性曲率信号，用于判断未来不确定性并调整行动时机。同时，设计了区间感知奖励机制以优化时间分配，并结合时空嵌入增强模型表现。实验表明，该方法显著提升了智能体的效率，特别是在处理时间决策问题时表现出色。

item.title: 基于时间LLM的晶圆级蚀刻深度空间预测模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于时间LLM的空间回归模型，用于预测晶圆蚀刻深度分布。



摘要： 本文研究了从原位工艺信号中理解晶圆级空间变化的重要性，并提出了一种基于时间LLM的空间回归模型，直接从多通道原位工艺时间序列预测晶圆蚀刻深度分布。该模型通过重新设计输入嵌入和输出投影，将LLM重编程应用于晶圆级空间估计。实验使用BOSCH等离子体蚀刻数据集验证了其在数据有限条件下的稳定性，证明了LLM重编程在晶圆级空间监测中的可行性。

item.title: ELITE框架提升视觉-语言模型在具身任务中的表现
item.content:encoded:
ELITE通过经验学习与意图感知迁移提升具身代理性能。



摘要： 本文提出ELITE框架，旨在解决基于视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的具身代理在复杂任务中表现不佳的问题。ELITE通过自我反思知识构建和意图感知检索机制，使代理能够从环境交互中持续学习，并将知识迁移到类似任务中。实验表明，在无监督和有监督设置下，ELITE均显著优于基础VLMs和其他先进方法，有效缩小了语义理解与可靠动作执行之间的差距。

item.title: 基于特征洗牌与重建的通用异常检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型框架FSR，解决跨场景异常检测中的相同捷径问题。



摘要： 本文针对工业领域中无监督异常检测面临的挑战，提出了一种名为FSR（Feature Shuffling and Restoration）的通用异常检测框架。该框架通过使用多尺度语义特征作为重建目标，而非原始图像像素，有效缓解了不同场景下异常检测性能下降的问题。FSR将特征块随机洗牌后进行重建，增强模型对全局上下文信息的关注。同时引入洗牌率以调节任务复杂度，并从网络结构和互信息角度进行了理论分析。实验结果表明，FSR在多种场景下均表现出优越性和高效性。

item.title: 基于外显子感知的AI生成文本检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Exons-Detect方法提升AI文本检测准确性与鲁棒性。



摘要： 随着大语言模型的快速发展，AI生成文本与人工写作之间的界限日益模糊，带来了诸如虚假信息传播、署名不明确和知识产权威胁等社会风险。为应对这些挑战，本文提出一种无需训练的AI生成文本检测方法Exons-Detect。该方法通过外显子感知的token重加权视角，识别并增强具有信息量的外显子token，计算可解释的翻译得分。实验表明，Exons-Detect在检测性能和对抗攻击鲁棒性方面均优于现有方法，尤其在DetectRL数据集上相对基线提升了2.2%的平均AUROC。

item.title: GameplayQA: A Benchmarking Framework for Decision-Dense POV-Synced Multi-Video Understanding of 3D Virtual Agents
item.content:encoded: Multimodal LLMs are increasingly deployed as perceptual backbones for autonomous agents in 3D environments, from robotics to virtual worlds. These applications require agents to perceive rapid state changes, attribute actions to the correct entities, and reason about concurrent multi-agent behaviors from a first-person perspective, capabilities that existing benchmarks do not adequately evaluate. We introduce GameplayQA, a framework for evaluating agentic-centric perception and reasoning through video understanding. Specifically, we densely annotate multiplayer 3D gameplay videos at 1.22 labels/second, with time-synced, concurrent captions of states, actions, and events structured around a triadic system of Self, Other Agents, and the World, a natural decomposition for multi-agent environments. From these annotations, we refined 2.4K diagnostic QA pairs organized into three levels of cognitive complexity, accompanied by a structured distractor taxonomy that enables fine-grained analysis of where models hallucinate. Evaluation of frontier MLLMs reveals a substantial gap from human performance, with common failures in temporal and cross-video grounding, agent-role attribution, and handling the decision density of the game. We hope GameplayQA stimulates future research at the intersection of embodied AI, agentic perception, and world modeling.
